mechanics.mechanic: Tilde/approx hedge regex ~\s*\d and ≈\s*\d
mechanics.expected: Inline comment L117 says this covers '~$78B'; Critical Rule #7(c) lists bare '~' as a valid hedge; docstring L30 lists '~, ≈'
mechanics.actual: r'~\s*\d' requires a DIGIT immediately after optional whitespace — the currency symbol intervenes, so '~$78B' is NOT recognized as hedged (probe FAIL: cited=False). '~78%' works. Same flaw for ≈. A hedged estimate like '~$78B' with no other hedge word fires whenever the string is absent from the corpus — and hedged estimates are precisely the numbers most likely to be absent

mechanics.risk: False-positive blocks on properly-tilde-hedged currency estimates; fix is a one-char class addition (~\s*[\$€£¥]?\s*\d), not proposed as UPGRADE because it corrects spec, not mechanism

(2) The figure is a 7-day window extrapolated x30/7, not a cumulative 30-day sum — the briefing honestly labels it 'estimated', the docstring says 'cumulative'. Live warning fired today at ~1238k from integer-parsed in-window lines (27k+101k+161k), so today's number is correct.

mechanics.risk: Windows containing decimal-cost entries understate spend (14.7k->7k halves it); budget-signal precision degrades exactly when actual token counts (which are decimal) replace round estimates.
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Accept an optional fraction: ~?(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)(?:-...)?k with float parse — 1-line diff.

A noisy partial week can flip the verdict of a pre-registered keep/retire decision

mechanics.actual: Header line 6 still says 'Currently instrumented: structural-output-hook.py' only — contradicted by 133 macro-anchor FIRE lines and session-prime lines in the same file. Stale since 2026-06-12; NOT covered by the 2026-07-20 CLAUDE.md count sync (this file's header unchanged)
mechanics.expectation_source: README hook conventions line 47; the log's own contents
mechanics.evidence: hook-fire-log.md lines 6 vs 12-14; fire census

mechanics.mechanic: Wired-hooks table + Stop-hook count (doc surface for assigned hooks' wiring context)
mechanics.expected: Table mirrors .claude/settings.json; CLAUDE.md counts were recomputed 2026-07-20 (19 scripts, Stop x15)
mechanics.actual: README header says '17 wired as of 2026-07-06' and conventions say 'all 14 Stop hooks' — settings.json now wires 15 Stop hooks (+ session-prime-cascade 07-12) plus PreToolUse git-guard (07-19), neither in the README table. The claims are date-qualified ('as of 2026-07-06') and were accurate then; README last touched in commit 1d25fa1 (07-06), so the 2026-07-20 canonical-count sync (already-fixed item) covered CLAUDE.md but not this README. Rows for structural-output-hook and macro-anchor-hook themselves are present and accurate
mechanics.expectation_source: .claude/settings.json; README step-5 documentation duty; CLAUDE.md 2026-07-20 count note
mechanics.evidence: README lines 19, 44 vs settings.json Stop array (15 entries) + PreToolUse; git log shows README untouched since 1d25fa1
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Add session-prime-cascade + git-guard rows and bump counts next time the README is edited (pure doc sync; flagged separately from the already-fixed CLAUDE.md sync)

mechanics.mechanic: Tool-name gate: exit 0 unless stdin tool_name is Bash or absent (line 98-99)
mechanics.expected: Guard scans only Bash tool calls; settings.json PreToolUse matcher is 'Bash'
mechanics.actual: tool_name not in (None,'Bash') -> exit 0; None allowed so manual/pipeline probes still scanned
mechanics.evidence: Probe: payload with tool_name=Write containing rm -rf repo path -> PASS (exit 0). Matches settings.json matcher redundancy.

mechanics.mechanic: ensure_hooks_path(): re-assert core.hooksPath to research/meta/hooks/git before every Bash call, 5s subprocess timeouts, log ACTIVATED on change (lines 68-77)
mechanics.expected: 're-asserts hooksPath before every Bash call' (governance 1c); self-activation closes fresh-clone gap
mechanics.actual: Runs on every Bash-tool call after tool-name gate, before empty-cmd exit; string-compares config to canonical relative path; silent except-pass on any error
mechanics.evidence: hook-fire-log.md line 844: '2026-07-20 20:01:17Z git-guard-pretooluse ACTIVATED core.hooksPath (was unset/wrong — stale-session class)'. Live repo config verified = research/meta/hooks/git.

mechanics.mechanic: Fail-open wrapper: any exception in main() -> log 'ERROR fail-open' + exit 0 (lines 179-186)
mechanics.expected: House standard: hooks FAIL-OPEN on internal error, never brick a session; errors logged
mechanics.actual: SystemExit re-raised (preserves exit 2 blocks); all other exceptions logged with type name and exit 0. Malformed/empty stdin JSON handled earlier (exit 0)
mechanics.evidence: Probes: malformed JSON 'not json{' -> exit 0; empty stdin -> exit 0. grep 'git-guard-pretooluse ERROR' in hook-fire-log.md = 0 hits (no live exceptions to date).

mechanics.mechanic: block(): exit 2 + actionable stderr (governance pointer, token instruction, cmd[:300]) + fire-log append (lines 80-89)
mechanics.expected: Block = exit 2 with actionable stderr; fires logged to research/meta/hook-fire-log.md
mechanics.actual: Exactly this; log line carries reason only (not the command text), stderr carries first 300 chars of command
mechanics.evidence: Live block observed during this audit (fire-log line 863, 2026-07-21 06:51:14Z); 20+ historical BLOCK lines at 780-863.

mechanics.mechanic: Operator token detection: env OPERATOR_APPROVED_FORCE_PUSH/DELETION==1 OR literal 'TOKEN=1' substring anywhere in command (lines 106-109); class separation force vs deletion
mechanics.expected: Override only via matching token after in-conversation operator approval; FORCE_PUSH unlocks history/record class only, DELETION unlocks file-destruction class only
mechanics.actual: Class separation correct (verified: DELETION token does not unlock push -f; FORCE token does not unlock rm). But the substring check disarms on ANY occurrence — 'echo OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION=1 && rm -rf research/' passes: a command that merely mentions/prints the token bypasses the deletion class
mechanics.evidence: Probe suite 1: 'token mentioned in echo then rm' -> PASS; 'deletion token env, push -f' -> BLOCK; 'force token env, rm' -> BLOCK. Design intent is env-prefix form ('OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION=1 git commit ...').
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Require the token as a command-prefix assignment (regex ^\s*(\w+=\w+\s+)*OPERATOR_APPROVED_X=1\s) instead of bare substring. Kills the token-in-payload disarm class; near-zero FP risk since legitimate use is always prefix-form.
mechanics.risk: Token honesty is explicitly instruction-level per governance, so this is acknowledged-by-design weakness; upgrade is small and deterministic.

mechanics.mechanic: Commit-message payload stripping: -m/--message with quoted arg replaced by 'MSG' before scanning (line 114)
mechanics.expected: Messages describing the guard must not self-trip (K3 FP class, demonstrated live 2026-07-19); bash -c wrappers deliberately NOT stripped
mechanics.actual: Strips single- and double-quoted -m/--message payloads (all occurrences); unquoted -m words untouched; inner bash -c content still scanned
mechanics.evidence: Probes: git commit -m 'document rm -rf guard behavior' -> PASS; git commit -m '+main:main push doc' -> PASS (would otherwise trip +refspec).

mechanics.mechanic: --no-verify block on commit/push (line 120-121)
mechanics.expected: Deterministically close the --no-verify bypass of the verified pre-commit/pre-push guards (governance 1c)
mechanics.actual: Long form '--no-verify' blocked on commit and push. BUT git's documented short synonym 'git commit -n' passes the guard, skips pre-commit AND commit-msg entirely (sandbox-verified), and the web permission allowlist Bash(git commit *) auto-approves it — a single 2-char flag silently defeats the entire commit-side destructive/secrets/cap/claims enforcement stack
mechanics.evidence: Probe 2: 'git commit -n -m MSG' -> PASS through guard. Sandbox J2: protected-file deletion committed with -n (pre-commit never ran). J1 confirms --no-verify same behavior at git layer but IS blocked by the guard; -n is not.
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Add \s-n\b to the commit branch of the no-verify regex (commit only — for push, -n means --dry-run and must NOT be blocked).
mechanics.risk: FP risk near zero: -n on git commit has no other meaning.

mechanics.mechanic: Force-push / remote-ref-deletion / +refspec block (lines 122-124): --force, --force-with-lease, -f, --delete, -d, :ref, +ref after 'push' in same pipe segment
mechanics.expected: K3 E5 coverage: push --force/-f/--force-with-lease/--delete/-d/:ref/+ref blocked absent FORCE_PUSH token
mechanics.actual: All seven forms block; plain push, pull, fetch pass
mechanics.evidence: Probes: push --force / -f / --force-with-lease / --delete / :deadbranch / +main:main all BLOCK; 'git push origin main' and 'git pull' PASS.

mechanics.mechanic: core.hooksPath override block, case-insensitive value comparison to canonical (lines 128-130) — G-25 patch
mechanics.expected: Block git config / -c / --unset of core.hooksPath to a non-canonical value; case-insensitive key (G-25 fixed 07-20; do-not-re-report)
mechanics.actual: Set-forms verified blocked (git config X, git -c core.hooksPath=X inline); canonical value passes; case-insensitivity confirmed by (?i). VERIFIED FIXED. Separate FP found: the regex matches any 'core.hooksPath' followed by whitespace + token, so a READ-ONLY query chained with anything ('git config core.hooksPath && echo hi') captures '&&' as the value and blocks
mechanics.evidence: This audit's first live Bash call was blocked exactly this way (fire-log line 863). Probe 2: 'git config core.hooksPath && echo ok' -> BLOCK; 'git config --get core.hooksPath' -> PASS; bare 'git config core.hooksPath' (end of string) -> PASS.
mechanics.expectation_source: docstring line 17 'to a non-canonical value' — a value read is not an override
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Exclude captured values that are shell operators: after match, ignore when group(1) in {'&&','||',';','|'} or starts with '&'/'|'/';'. One-line change; kills a demonstrated FP on read-only audit commands.
mechanics.risk: Fail-closed FP, so current behavior is safe-but-noisy; fix is trivial and cannot open a bypass since operators are never valid hooksPath values.

mechanics.mechanic: core.hooksPath --unset block (line 131-132)
mechanics.expected: Unsetting hooksPath (disabling git guards) blocked
mechanics.actual: Blocks 'git config --unset core.hooksPath' (case-insensitive, same-segment)
mechanics.evidence: Probe 1: hooksPath unset -> BLOCK with correct reason.

mechanics.mechanic: Git plumbing block (lines 133-139): reset --hard, clean -f*, reflog expire, gc --prune, branch -d/-D, update-ref -d, filter-branch/filter-repo, stash drop/clear, checkout/switch -f/--force, worktree remove -f/--force
mechanics.expected: K3 E2 plumbing coverage blocked absent FORCE_PUSH token
mechanics.actual: All listed forms verified blocking (12 probes incl. clean -xdf, worktree remove -f); non-destructive forms (checkout main, clean -n, restore file) pass
mechanics.evidence: Probes 1-3: reset --hard, clean -fd/-xdf, branch -D/-d, stash drop/clear, checkout -f, switch -f/--force, gc --prune, filter-branch, reflog expire, update-ref -d, worktree remove -f/--force all BLOCK.

mechanics.mechanic: restore working-tree-discard alternative 'restore\s+[^|;&]*(--worktree|--staged|\.(\s|$))' inside the plumbing group (line 137)
mechanics.expected: 'git restore .' (bulk working-tree discard) blocked as history/record class
mechanics.actual: The alternation group is followed by \b (line 138). When '\.(\s|$)' matches at end-of-string or before a non-word char, the trailing \b fails — so 'git restore .' and 'git restore . && echo' PASS; only 'git restore . <wordchar...>' blocks. The dot alternative is dead in its most common forms; --worktree/--staged forms still block
mechanics.evidence: Probe 2: 'git restore .' -> PASS; 'git restore . && echo done' -> PASS; 'git restore . foo' -> BLOCK; 'git restore --staged .' -> BLOCK; 'git restore --worktree research/INDEX.md' -> BLOCK.
mechanics.expectation_source: code intent (the \.(\s|$) alternative exists only to catch restore-dot); docstring 'plumbing that discards ... working-tree'
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Move the closing \b inside per-alternative or change the dot alternative to '\.(?=\s|$)' and end the group without requiring \b after it.
mechanics.risk: Working-tree discard of uncommitted work is exactly the 'accepted gap: local uncommitted work' class governance worries about; fix is a 1-char-class regex correction.

mechanics.mechanic: checkout-based working-tree discard forms: 'git checkout -- .', 'git checkout .', 'git checkout HEAD -- path'
mechanics.expected: Docstring claims only 'checkout/switch -f/--force' — these forms are outside stated coverage, but they discard the working tree identically to restore-dot
mechanics.actual: All three pass the guard
mechanics.evidence: Probe 3: 'git checkout -- .' PASS, 'git checkout .' PASS, 'git checkout HEAD -- research/' PASS.
mechanics.expectation_source: docstring line 20 (coverage list matches code, so GAP vs threat-model intent rather than DEFECT)
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Add 'checkout\s+(HEAD\s+)?--\s' and 'checkout\s+\.' alternatives alongside the restore-dot fix.
mechanics.risk: Low FP: 'checkout -- <file>' single-file restores would also block; acceptable given DELETION-token override exists, or scope to '-- .' only.

mechanics.mechanic: mv relocation guard (lines 141-142): mv command containing .git/research/portfolio tokens
mechanics.expected: 'mv of .git / research / protected path out (K3 E7)' blocked
mechanics.actual: Token-named trees block ('mv research /tmp/r', 'mv research/CLAUDE.md /tmp/' BLOCK), but relocating the ENTIRE repo by absolute path — 'mv /home/user/LLMNA /tmp/gone' — passes because the regex looks only for the three basename tokens, never the repo-root path
mechanics.evidence: Probe 3: 'mv /home/user/LLMNA /tmp/gone' -> PASS; 'mv research /tmp/r' and 'mv /home/user/LLMNA/.git /tmp/g' -> BLOCK.
mechanics.expectation_source: docstring line 21 — repo-root relocation is the superset of the protected-tree relocation the rule names
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Add re.escape(REPO) + '|\$\{?CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\}?' to the mv target alternation.
mechanics.risk: mv is reversible (nothing destroyed), so blast radius is confusion not loss; still trivially closable.

mechanics.mechanic: rm recursive-force flag matrix (lines 148-150): short combined, reversed, split, long-form and mixed long/short — G-23 patch
mechanics.expected: G-23 fixed 2026-07-20 (do-not-re-report): long-flag forms covered
mechanics.actual: VERIFIED FIXED: -rf, -fr, -r -f, --recursive --force, --recursive -f, -r --force all block when aimed at protected targets
mechanics.evidence: Probes: 'rm --recursive --force research/' BLOCK; 'rm -fr .' BLOCK; 'rm -rf research/' BLOCK.

mechanics.mechanic: rm target matcher (lines 151-153): tail after first 'rm' must contain repo-root path, research/, .git/, portfolio/, bare '.', or bare '/' as a complete delimiter-bounded token
mechanics.expected: G-24 fix claims dynamic repo root + $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR + bare ./'./' coverage so 'rm -rf $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR' and './'-forms can't pass unexamined
mechanics.actual: The G-24 additions went into REPO_TOKENS (lines 50-56) but the rm branch does NOT consult REPO_TOKENS — it has its own narrower tail regex. Consequences, all probe-verified with production REPO: (a) 'rm -rf $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR' PASSES; (b) 'rm -rf ~/LLMNA' PASSES; (c) './'-prefixed forms pass: 'rm -rf ./research' PASSES (bare '.' and absolute forms do block)
mechanics.evidence: Probe 2 (REPO=/home/user/LLMNA, ensure_hooks_path stubbed, log to scratch): 'rm -rf /home/user/LLMNA' BLOCK, 'rm -rf .'(as -fr) BLOCK, but 'rm -rf $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR' PASS, 'rm -rf ~/LLMNA' PASS, 'rm -rf ./research' PASS. REPO_TOKENS itself contains \$\{?CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\}? and the ./ alternative — the fix was applied to the wrong gate for this branch.
mechanics.expectation_source: code comment lines 47-49 (2026-07-20 K3-Swarm G-24 fix rationale); docstring line 22
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Gate the rm branch on 'flags matched AND (tail-regex OR REPO_TOKENS.search(tail))' — reuses the already-hardened token set; kills the env-var/tilde/dot-slash evasion class the G-24 fix intended to close.
mechanics.risk: 'rm -rf "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"/x' is precisely what a confused agent emits when told to use the canonical env var; REPO_TOKENS is broader so marginal FP increase is bounded to commands that both rm -rf AND mention repo tokens — the intended block set.

mechanics.mechanic: rm target matcher nested-path behavior: alternatives 'research/?', 'portfolio/?' etc. require a delimiter immediately after the optional slash
mechanics.expected: Docstring: 'rm -rf repo/.git/research' blocked — record trees protected from recursive force-delete
mechanics.actual: Only whole-token targets block. Any nested subpath passes: 'rm -rf research/companies', 'rm -rf research/predictions/', 'rm -rf /home/user/LLMNA/research/companies', and notably 'rm -rf research/portfolio' (the REAL portfolio path) all PASS, while 'rm -rf portfolio' (a path that does not exist — portfolio lives under research/) blocks
mechanics.evidence: Probe 3: all four nested forms PASS; 'rm -rf portfolio' BLOCK; 'rm -rf research' BLOCK. Mitigation stack: deleted tracked files are git-recoverable while .git is intact, and pre-commit F4 mass-delete brake blocks committing >3 deletions — so durable-record loss is still stopped one layer down.
mechanics.expectation_source: docstring line 22; REPO_TOKENS comment (the has_repo gate DOES match subpaths via '(^|[\s'\"=(/])research(/|\b)' — the rm branch's private regex is strictly narrower)
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Same fix as the G-24 row: OR the tail against REPO_TOKENS, whose research-token already matches nested paths and slash-prefixed forms.
mechanics.risk: Working-tree-only blast radius (recoverable), hence MEDIUM not HIGH.

mechanics.mechanic: rm recursive without force: 'rm -r <target>' (no -f)
mechanics.expected: Coverage list names recursive FORCE-delete only
mechanics.actual: 'rm -r research/' and 'rm -r /home/user/LLMNA' PASS. In the non-interactive Bash-tool context, rm -r deletes a writable tree exactly as thoroughly as rm -rf (no tty to prompt)
mechanics.evidence: Probe 2: both forms PASS. Code matches docstring (so GAP vs threat model, not DEFECT).
mechanics.expectation_source: docstring line 22 'rm -rf'; threat model line 6 'destructive-command accident'
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Treat -r/--recursive alone as sufficient when the target matches (drop the force requirement).
mechanics.risk: Slight FP increase on interactive-intent rm -r; in this harness all rm goes through the non-interactive Bash tool, so force-flag distinction carries no safety information.

mechanics.mechanic: find -delete / -exec rm|unlink gated on has_repo (REPO_TOKENS) (lines 155-156)
mechanics.expected: K3 E4: find …-delete/-exec rm over a repo path blocked; G-24 bare-. coverage via REPO_TOKENS
mechanics.actual: Blocks 'find . -name *.md -delete' (bare-. token works here because this branch uses REPO_TOKENS), 'find research -exec rm'; non-repo paths pass
mechanics.evidence: Probes: find over repo paths and bare '.' BLOCK; 'find /var/tmp/x -delete' PASS.

mechanics.mechanic: pipe-to-xargs rm|unlink gated on has_repo (lines 157-158)
mechanics.expected: xargs rm over a repo path blocked
mechanics.actual: As specified; requires a literal pipe before xargs
mechanics.evidence: Probe 1: 'ls research | xargs rm' -> BLOCK.

mechanics.mechanic: Interpreter-level deletion (lines 159-161): python/perl/ruby/node with -c/-e AND deletion verb AND has_repo
mechanics.expected: Docstring: 'python/perl/ruby/node rmtree/unlink/remove on repo path' — no -c/-e restriction stated
mechanics.actual: Only -c/-e inline forms are scanned. Heredoc python (python3 <<EOF … rmtree …) and script-file invocation (python3 /tmp/kill.py) pass unexamined — and the web permission allowlist auto-approves Bash(python3 *). Additionally the node deletion-verb list matches literal 'fs.rm' only, so the idiomatic "require('fs').rm(...)" form passes even WITH -e and a repo path
mechanics.evidence: Probes: python -c rmtree forms BLOCK (incl. __import__ obfuscation and rmtree('.')); heredoc rmtree PASS; 'node -e "require('fs').rm('/home/user/LLMNA/research',{recursive:true})"' PASS. This audit itself used the file-based route as instructed — it is a known-open channel.
mechanics.expectation_source: docstring line 24 (overclaims vs code); K3 E4
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: (a) Add heredoc detection: interpreter + '<<' + deletion verb; (b) broaden node verbs to (fs\)?\.?rm(Sync|dir)?|rmSync|unlinkSync and match .rm( after a fs require. Script-file contents are inherently unscannable at this layer — leave documented as residual (Edit/Write matcher is HELD per docstring).
mechanics.risk: File-based interpreter deletion can never be fully closed by command-text scanning; docstring should state the -c/-e scope honestly either way.

mechanics.mechanic: truncate -s0 and dd of= gated on has_repo (lines 162-165)
mechanics.expected: Content erasure/overwrite on repo paths blocked; non-repo paths untouched
mechanics.actual: As specified; '-s 0' and '-s0' both matched
mechanics.evidence: Probes: both repo-path forms BLOCK; '/tmp/x' targets PASS.

mechanics.mechanic: Truncating single-> redirect onto protected files (lines 170-174) — G-26 protected-list parity patch
mechanics.expected: G-26 fixed 07-20 (do-not-re-report): protected list aligned with governance §1b (CLAUDE.md, biases-watchlist, grading-log, calibration-ledger, INDEX.md, portfolio/, meta/tools/ added); >> append exempt
mechanics.actual: VERIFIED FIXED: list now contains all §1b names; '>' onto them blocks, '>>' passes, ': > file' blocks, '2>' onto protected file blocks (correct — stderr redirect also truncates). Residual evasions: '>|' (noclobber-override truncation) passes because the char class after '>' excludes '|'; 'tee file' and 'cp /dev/null file' pass (different operators). FP note: the tokens are matched anywhere in the path, so '> /some/other/settings.json' (outside the repo) blocks — fail-closed, acceptable
mechanics.evidence: Probes: > research/CLAUDE.md BLOCK, >> PASS, ': >' BLOCK, '2>' BLOCK, '>|' PASS, 'tee research/CLAUDE.md' PASS, 'cp /dev/null research/CLAUDE.md' PASS, '> /some/other/settings.json' BLOCK.
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Add '\|' handling for the >| operator and a tee/cp-/dev/null alternative if the class recurs; note tee/cp overlap with the explicitly HELD Edit/Write matcher (K3 E8) so deferral is defensible.
mechanics.risk: '>|' is obscure; tee/cp additions raise FP surface on legitimate log-teeing — only worth it if a live miss occurs.

mechanics.mechanic: REPO_TOKENS has_repo gate (lines 50-56): dynamic repo root, hardcoded /home/user/LLMNA, $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, bare ./'./' , research/.git/protected-file name tokens
mechanics.expected: G-24 fixed (do-not-re-report): dynamic root + env var + bare dot so find/rmtree('.') no longer pass the gate
mechanics.actual: VERIFIED FIXED for the branches that use it (find/xargs/interpreter/truncate/dd). REPO falls back to hardcoded '/home/user/LLMNA' when CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR unset (line 42) — same hardcode class as G-55 but defensible as a guard-side default
mechanics.evidence: Probe: python3 -c rmtree('.') BLOCK; find . -delete BLOCK. re.escape applied to env-derived REPO.

mechanics.mechanic: Embedded --selftest
mechanics.expected: House standard: embedded --selftest with fixtures where present; this is the newest safety-critical hook, patched 2026-07-20 with 4 regex changes (G-23..G-26); verification to date is external (19/19 script matrix, K3 verifier)
mechanics.actual: No selftest, no argv handling at all. The four 07-20 regex patches shipped with no embedded regression fixtures; this audit found two regressions the external matrix missed (restore-dot dead alternative, rm-branch G-24 non-application)
mechanics.evidence: grep selftest/argv in file = 0 hits. session-prime-cascade-hook.py demonstrates the house pattern (21-fixture --selftest).
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Embed --selftest encoding the K3 13-bypass matrix + G-23..G-26 fixtures + the pass-cases (canonical hooksPath, -m strip, appends). Deterministic, runs on demand only, zero runtime cost; kills the silent-regression class for future regex edits.
mechanics.risk: None at runtime; small file-size cost.

mechanics.mechanic: Function 1 diff-before-commit: always print --stat; added lines head-400 if count <=400 else notice + head-60 (lines 14-23)
mechanics.expected: Print the staged diff into commit output so the committer has content in-context; large-diff fallback
mechanics.actual: As specified; grep -c self-prints 0 on no match (the old '|| echo 0' double-zero bug is fixed per inline comment). Cosmetic: '^+[^+]' misses added lines whose content itself starts with '+' and the count excludes them identically, so threshold behavior is self-consistent
mechanics.evidence: Sandbox: every commit printed '=== pre-commit: staged changes ===' + stat; verified in all 22 sandbox cases.

mechanics.mechanic: Function 2 secrets scan (lines 26-34): added lines matching (API_KEY|API_TOKEN|APIKEY|_SECRET|PASSWORD|PRIVATE_KEY)[:=] + 12+ [A-Za-z0-9._-] value -> exit 1 with 6-char-prefix redacted echo; 'secretscan-ok' anywhere on the line exempts
mechanics.expected: NEVER-ECHO enforcement at last gate; false-positive override via appended '# secretscan-ok'
mechanics.actual: As specified. Redaction sed applies to every 6+ run on the line (mangles the key NAME too — safe, over-redacts). Scope: added lines only (documented); value charset excludes $var references so 'KEY=$ENV_NAME' passes correctly
mechanics.evidence: Sandbox E1: 'MY_API_KEY = "abcdefgh12345678"' BLOCK (exit 1, redacted echo); E2 with '# secretscan-ok' PASS.
mechanics.expectation_source: hook header lines 10-12; meta/data-access.md NEVER-ECHO discipline

mechanics.mechanic: Function 3 session-prime cap gate (lines 41-54): trigger only when 'research/meta/session-prime.md' is a staged name (grep -qx); chars via python len(stdin.read()); block when -gt 30000
mechanics.expected: Cap = MAX_INJECT_CHARS in session-prime-hook.py; chars not bytes; boundary -gt mirrors the injection hook's '> 30000' truncation exactly
mechanics.actual: VERIFIED: session-prime-hook.py line 68 MAX_INJECT_CHARS=30000, truncation at '> MAX_INJECT_CHARS' — boundary parity exact. 30000 chars passes, 30001 blocks. Two minor edges: (1) if python3 fails, SP_CHARS='' -> '[ "" -gt 30000 ]' errors and the gate silently skips (fail-open with only a shell error line — borderline vs 'never silently skip'); (2) char counting via stdin depends on locale encoding — in a non-UTF-8 locale non-ASCII bytes count differently than the hook's file-read len()
mechanics.evidence: Sandbox F1 (30000) PASS with '(session-prime cap gate: 30000 chars — OK)'; F2 (30001) BLOCK. MAX_INJECT_CHARS grep verified.
mechanics.expectation_source: hook comment lines 36-45; CLAUDE.md session-prime-hook bullet (hard cap 30000)

mechanics.mechanic: Function 4a protected-path deletion block (lines 62-76): --no-renames --diff-filter=D, core.quotePath=false, PROTECTED_RE anchored list; gated by OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION!=1
mechanics.expected: Governance §1b protected list: CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json, INDEX.md, meta/hooks/, meta/tools/, methodology, biases-watchlist, session-prime, lessons, grading-log, calibration-ledger, portfolio/; rename/quotePath/word-split evasions fixed 07-19 (verified 13/13)
mechanics.actual: List parity confirmed for all governance names. One scoping hole: PROTECTED_RE protects research/CLAUDE.md but NOT the repo-root /CLAUDE.md (the boot-order instruction file that points every session at the corpus) — sandbox-confirmed deletable without block. mechanics.mechanic: MAIN-REF ASSUMPTION (assigned focus): denominator = `git log main --since=... --format=%as` with check=True (lines 40-47) — requires a LOCAL 'main' ref that is CURRENT
mechanics.expected: Docstring/CLAUDE.md: 'weekly main-branch commits (both deterministic from repo data)' — the deterministic adjudicator for the pre-registered 2026-08-06 keep-vs-retire decision must run in any session container
mechanics.actual: PROBE-CONFIRMED CRASH: in a clone checked out on a claude/* session branch with no local main ref (the normal shape of web-session containers — 8 such remote branches exist), git exits 128 ('unknown revision main') → CalledProcessError, no fallback to origin/main. SILENT-WRONG variant: where local main exists but lags origin/main (the documented 11-day-stale-clone incident class; setup.sh ships a STALE banner for exactly this), recent-week commits are undercounted → normalized rate inflated → biases the endpoint compare toward FLAT/RISING → wrongly retires the priming hook. Works correctly in the current container only because HEAD==main==origin/main
mechanics.expectation_source: docstring lines 2-11; CLAUDE.md structural-output-hook entry (pre-registered decision rule); setup.sh stale-clone guard as evidence the stale-local-ref world is real
mechanics.evidence: Scratchpad probe (clone, delete refs/heads/main, run): exit 1, CalledProcessError on ['git','-C',...,'log','main',...] status 128; current-container run: exit 0, 8 weeks printed
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: `git fetch origin main` (best-effort) then log `origin/main` with fallback to `main` then `HEAD` — ~4-line diff; kills both the crash and the silent stale-denominator class before the 08-06 adjudication
mechanics.risk: The one pre-registered deterministic decision in the experiment can crash or silently flip depending on which container runs it

mechanics.mechanic: Format/feature divergence across the 6 logging copies
mechanics.expected: One house format so structural-output-metric.py and audits can parse uniformly; anti-fab's K3-r2 hardening rationale (return line as transcript backup on write failure; repr-escape) applies to every fire logger
mechanics.actual: Status vocabulary diverges: anti-fab FIRE/INCONCLUSIVE with n=/forms=/verdict:pending (only copy with adjudication field, repr-escaping, and return-line-on-write-failure); session-prime-cascade FIRE/ERROR; macro-anchor and structural-output bare FIRE(reason); git-guard BLOCK/ACTIVATED/'ERROR fail-open'; session-prime-hook 'event=X injected=Y (reason)' with NO status token at all. The other 5 loggers silently swallow write failure with no transcript backup.
mechanics.expectation_source: anti-fabrication-hook.py:351-369 docstring (K3 r2 #8) + todo.md 07-24 item ('house format ... format is proven multi-tenant')
mechanics.evidence: hook-fire-log.md tail shows all formats co-existing (e.g. '- 2026-07-21 05:50:27Z anti-fabrication-hook FIRE (n=1 ... verdict:pending)' vs '- 2026-07-20 19:48:08Z session-prime-hook event=resume injected=False (skipped non-startup)'). research/meta/structural-output-metric.py filters by hook name, which tolerates but does not normalize the divergence.

mechanics.mechanic: Fire-logging coverage across the 15 Stop hooks
mechanics.expected: Fire evidence durable enough to drive the README retirement rule ('a hook that fires <5x/month for 3 consecutive months is INERT (retire)') and the falsifier/re-eval metadata every hook carries
mechanics.actual: 11 of 15 wired Stop hooks log NOTHING on a blocking fire: cascade-enforcement, segment-trajectory, nth-order-cascade, bypass-route, bottoms-up, antifragility-mn, analyst-pt-context, signal-ingest-cascade, reasoning-tagging, llm-native-reasoning, borrowed-vs-firstprinciples. Their exit-2 blocks exist only in the session transcript, which dies with the container — the inert-retirement metric and each hook's own falsifier are unverifiable from repo data for 11/15 hooks. Only anti-fab, session-prime-cascade, macro-anchor, structural-output (+ SessionStart session-prime, PreToolUse git-guard) log.
mechanics.expectation_source: hooks/README.md §Retirement discipline + house standard 'fires logged to research/meta/hook-fire-log.md'
mechanics.evidence: hook-fire-log.md name census: session-prime-hook 501, macro-anchor 133, structural-output 118, git-guard 95, session-prime-cascade 5, anti-fab 2 — zero lines for the other 11 hook names in the entire log. Already tracked: todo.md 07-24 item ('migrate remaining Stop hooks opportunistically at the 07-24 monthly audit') — flagged here so the census is complete, not as a new discovery.

mechanics.mechanic: Recursion-guard presence per Stop hook
mechanics.expected: Every wired Stop hook exits 0 immediately when stdin JSON carries stop_hook_active (README hook conventions); non-Stop hooks do not need it
mechanics.actual: 15/15 wired Stop hooks carry the guard (grep census, one hit per file). The 2026-07-20 fix adding the guard to session-prime-cascade-hook.py (ALREADY-FIXED list, K3-Swarm G-20) VERIFIED present at lines 205-212, including the deliberate 'unreadable stdin never disables enforcement' fail-closed stdin branch. Non-Stop hooks (git-guard PreToolUse, session-start/session-prime SessionStart, llm-native-priming UserPromptSubmit) correctly omit it.
mechanics.expectation_source: hooks/README.md §Hook conventions ('all 14 Stop hooks carry this as of the 2026-07-06 audit' — count now 15)
mechanics.evidence: Grep 'stop_hook_active' across hooks/*.py: exactly 15 files, one guard each. Style split is cosmetic: 8 use `is True` strict compare, 7 use truthy; and 4 hooks (llm-native-reasoning:207, macro-anchor:199, reasoning-tagging:136, structural-output:183) bury the guard + sys.exit(0) inside load_last_assistant_message() rather than main() — functionally identical today, a hidden exit path if the loader is ever reused.

mechanics.mechanic: Outer catch-all fail-open (exit 0) wrapper presence
mechanics.expected: House standard: hooks FAIL-OPEN on internal error with exit 0 (git-guard docstring: 'Any exception = allow + log'; anti-fab: 'an unexpected bug in this hook must never block work')
mechanics.actual: Full outer wrapper in only 3/19: anti-fabrication (__main__ try/except → exit 0), git-guard (__main__ try/except → log ERROR → exit 0), session-prime-cascade (in-main try/except → log ERROR → exit 0). session-start is partial (build_briefing + W11 blocks wrapped; in_scope/getcwd not). The remaining 15 rely on per-step try/except whose exception lists have holes: the 7 variant-A transcript readers catch only (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError), so IsADirectoryError/UnicodeDecodeError on the transcript propagate; os.getcwd() and odd entry shapes are unguarded. Uncaught → exit 1 + traceback, not the exit-0 standard.
mechanics.evidence: Probe (read-only): transcript_path pointed at a directory → analyst-pt-context-hook exits 1 with 'IsADirectoryError: [Errno 21] Is a directory' traceback; identical payload to anti-fabrication-hook exits 0 via its wrapper. Severity LOW because exit 1 is non-blocking for Stop hooks — enforcement is skipped and stderr noise surfaces, but nothing bricks.

mechanics.mechanic: Crash-block possibility (can any hook block on an internal error?)
mechanics.expected: block = exit 2 with actionable stderr, only on clean detections; internal errors must never block
mechanics.actual: No hook can crash-block. Every sys.exit(2) in all 19 files sits on an explicit detection path; uncaught exceptions produce exit 1, which is non-blocking for Stop, UserPromptSubmit, and PreToolUse events. The two wrapped hooks correctly re-raise SystemExit (anti-fab:569-570, git-guard:182-183) so their fail-open wrappers cannot swallow a legitimate block, and llm-native-priming (the only hook whose event would hard-block a prompt on exit 2) never exits 2.
mechanics.expectation_source: House standard (block = exit 2); Claude Code hook exit-code semantics
mechanics.evidence: Full read of all 19 files: exit-2 call sites enumerated (14 Stop-hook block paths + git-guard block()); no exit(2) reachable from an except handler anywhere.

mechanics.mechanic: Silent enforcement skip on internal error, without any log
mechanics.expected: House standard: fail-open on internal error but NEVER silently skip enforcement — conformant precedents log the skip (session-prime-cascade logs ERROR; anti-fab logs INCONCLUSIVE)
mechanics.actual: cascade-enforcement-hook.py:60-81 and signal-ingest-cascade-hook.py:215-235 wrap their git subprocess calls in bare `except Exception: pass` — a failing git (lock, missing binary, corrupted repo) yields an empty changeset, i.e. Critical-Rule-#10 enforcement silently skipped with zero durable record. Both predate the telemetry standard.
mechanics.expectation_source: Task house standards; session-prime-cascade-hook.py:45 ('FAIL-OPEN: any exception → log ERROR, exit 0') as the conformant pattern
mechanics.evidence: cascade-enforcement files_in_current_changeset(): both subprocess.run calls use check=True inside try/except-pass with no log_fire; signal-ingest cross_source_log_file_created_or_modified() identical shape. Neither file imports a logger.

mechanics.mechanic: Timeout presence per subprocess call site
mechanics.expected: Every subprocess call bounded (pattern set by the 2026-07 rebuilds: git-guard 5s, session-prime-cascade 15s, anti-fab 10s-per-grep under a 15s shared budget)
mechanics.actual: 8 call sites in 5 files. Timed (4): git-guard-pretooluse.py:70,73 (timeout=5), session-prime-cascade-hook.py:145-147 (timeout=15), anti-fabrication-hook.py:217-224 (timeout≤10, budget-capped). Untimed (4): cascade-enforcement-hook.py:61,72 and signal-ingest-cascade-hook.py:217,227 (git status --porcelain / git diff-tree HEAD, no timeout kwarg) — a hung git stalls the Stop event until the platform's per-hook timeout. The two untimed files are the same two files with the silent-skip defect above.
mechanics.expectation_source: House pattern in the three 2026-07-rebuilt hooks; no explicit written mandate (which is itself part of the gap — the planned 07-24 'house standard' item covers logging but not timeouts)
mechanics.evidence: Grep 'subprocess.run' across hooks/*.py with context: 8 sites, timeout kwarg present on exactly 4. Also VERIFIED (not re-reported) known-open G-29: anti-fabrication-hook.py:291-313 shared 15s budget with per-form BudgetExceeded → trailing needles of dense messages route to INCONCLUSIVE, retry path rechecks the deadline (true bound ≈ budget + one in-flight grep) — present exactly as described.

mechanics.mechanic: Changeset-window duplication of the known-open G-19 class
mechanics.expected: KNOWN-OPEN G-19: session-prime-cascade changeset window = working tree + staged + HEAD only, missing multi-commit turns (verify, don't rediscover)
mechanics.actual: VERIFIED at session-prime-cascade-hook.py:179-197 (diff / diff --cached / show HEAD — exactly as described, still open). Census extension: the identical HEAD+worktree window is independently duplicated in cascade-enforcement-hook.py:57-82 (files_in_current_changeset) and signal-ingest-cascade-hook.py:210-240 (cross-source-log check) — a synthesis artifact or cross-source-log file committed 2+ commits earlier in the same turn escapes (cascade-enforcement) or fails to satisfy (signal-ingest) those hooks too. G-19's wording names only session-prime-cascade; any fix should be applied to all three copies (or to a shared changeset helper).
mechanics.expectation_source: Known-open ledger G-19; cascade-enforcement docstring ('current changeset — working tree, staged, or most recent commit')
mechanics.evidence: All three implementations read git status --porcelain + HEAD-only diff; none walk multi-commit ranges (e.g. @{push}..HEAD or reflog-since-turn-start). Same defect class × 3 files = the fix surface is wider than G-19 states.

mechanics.mechanic: Repo-root resolution convention conformance
mechanics.expected: README §'Where they live': 'every script resolves the repo root as os.environ.get("CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR") or Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]' — zero hardcoded repo paths (CLAUDE.md: 'no hardcoded repo path')
mechanics.actual: 18/19 conform via the standard 4-line preamble. git-guard-pretooluse.py:42 deviates: REPO = os.environ.get("CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR") or "/home/user/LLMNA" — a hardcoded fallback instead of parents[3]. With CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR unset and the repo cloned elsewhere: fire-log writes silently fail, ensure_hooks_path targets the wrong directory (git-level guards never self-activate), and only the generic REPO_TOKENS alternatives (research/, .git, etc.) keep the block logic alive. Hooks-layer sibling of known G-55 (tools/setup.sh) — same hardcode class, different file, not covered by G-55's wording.
mechanics.expectation_source: hooks/README.md line 11 + research/CLAUDE.md §Enforcement hooks ('dynamic repo-root resolution ... no hardcoded repo path')
mechanics.evidence: git-guard-pretooluse.py:42 vs the _REPO_ROOT preamble present in the other 18 files. The literal '/home/user/LLMNA' inside REPO_TOKENS (line 51) is intentional defense-in-depth and NOT part of this finding.

mechanics.mechanic: Hardcoded calendar year in macro-anchor pass-condition regex
mechanics.expected: 'Macro anchor with date' marker accepts any date-stamped anchor ('as of [YYYY-MM-DD]', per docstring line 52)
mechanics.actual: macro-anchor-hook.py:116 pattern is r"\bas of 2026-\d{2}-\d{2}" — year literal. From 2027-01-01, outputs correctly anchored 'as of 2027-01-15' no longer satisfy the pass condition via this marker; hook false-fires on properly-anchored analysis unless another marker happens to match. Deterministic time-bomb with a known detonation date.
mechanics.evidence: Line 116; contrast session-prime-cascade extractors which use the generic 20\d\d. Related but fail-open-direction: bottoms-up-hook.py:69 forward-horizon trigger bounds (20[2-4]\d, FY 2[2-9], Q[1-4] FY 2[2-9]) under-trigger from FY30 — COSMETIC, listed here for census completeness.

mechanics.mechanic: Environment assumptions: parents[3] depth, prefix scope-match, clock, HOME
mechanics.expected: Portability 'across machines and clone locations' (DURABLE-ACTIVATION TL;DR); scope guard limits enforcement to this repo
mechanics.actual: Four latent assumptions: (1) parents[3] assumes the file sits exactly at research/meta/hooks/ depth — under the still-documented install.sh fallback (~/.claude/ copy, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR unset) REPO_ROOT resolves to '/', making in_scope() vacuously true everywhere and pointing RESEARCH_DIR/fire-log at nonexistent root paths; (2) in_scope() uses cwd.startswith(REPO_ROOT) — a sibling dir like /home/user/LLMNA-scratch also matches (no os.path.sep boundary); (3) session-start-hook due-date math uses local date.today() while all log timestamps are UTC — divergence if container TZ ever ≠ UTC; (4) W11 sentinel hardcodes ~/.w11-armed-sentinel (stable-HOME assumption, by design of the container-swap detector).
mechanics.evidence: Path arithmetic: /home/user/.claude/x.py → parents[3] = '/'. in_scope() implementations in all 15 Stop hooks use bare str.startswith. session-start-hook.py:130,133 (date.today()) vs log_fire UTC stamps. All benign in the current deployed environment; listed as census-complete env assumptions.

mechanics.mechanic: get_last_assistant_message duplication and shape divergence
mechanics.expected: One canonical transcript-reading shape (signal-ingest docstring itself claims 'same architecture as cascade-enforcement-hook.py + anti-fabrication-hook.py'; house doctrine: one canonical home per mechanism)
mechanics.actual: 13 near-identical implementations across 12 files in 3 divergent variants. Variant A (reversed-scan, tolerant `entry.get("message") or entry` top-level-role fallback): analyst-pt, antifragility, borrowed-vs, bottoms-up, bypass-route, nth-order, segment-trajectory, plus anti-fab A′ (catches only FileNotFoundError — no PermissionError — absorbed by its outer wrapper). Variant B (forward-scan of the whole file, message.role only, recursion guard embedded in the loader): llm-native-reasoning, macro-anchor, reasoning-tagging, structural-output. Variant C (keys on entry['type'], not message.role; separate user-message reader): signal-ingest ×2 functions. Probe-confirmed divergence: a top-level-role transcript entry is VISIBLE to A but INVISIBLE to B (reasoning-tagging exits 0 on the top-level fixture, 2 on the message-wrapped one) — and meta/tests/test_framework_codifications.py fixtures use exactly the top-level shape, so equivalent fixtures written against any B-hook would silently false-pass.
mechanics.expectation_source: signal-ingest-cascade-hook.py docstring line 43 + hooks/README.md testing example (uses the message-wrapped shape)
mechanics.evidence: Full reads of all 12 files; probe 3: reasoning-tagging exit 0 (top-level-role) vs exit 2 (message-wrapped) on identical content. All variants work on live platform transcripts today; the divergence is a drift/testing hazard, not a live miss.

mechanics.mechanic: Shared transcript-reader + parse canary (upgrade)
mechanics.expected: N/A (no current spec) — upgrade proposal
mechanics.actual: Today, any change to the platform transcript JSONL shape makes every parser return '' → all 13 text-scanning Stop hooks exit 0 silently and simultaneously; clean passes are unlogged by design, so the entire Stop-enforcement layer can die with no repo-visible signal (detectable only as gradual absence of fires — the session-prime-cascade falsifier acknowledges this blind spot). Each Stop event also re-parses the full transcript up to 15 times (once per hook), with variant B parsing every line even when only the tail is needed.
mechanics.proposed_upgrade: Co-ship a shared transcript-reader module with the ALREADY-PLANNED meta/hooks/hook_fire_log.py at the 2026-07-24 migration (one migration wave, not two): single tolerant parser (message-wrapper OR top-level role OR type field), backward tail-scan, plus a parse canary — if transcript_path exists and is non-empty but zero assistant entries parse, log one ERROR line to hook-fire-log.md. Net-positive: kills the silent-simultaneous-layer-death class (highest blast-radius latent failure found in this census), collapses the 3-variant divergence, and removes 13 duplicated code blocks; behavior-preserving so FP risk ≈ 0. Cost/risk: one shared import — each hook must keep a try/except around the import falling back to its current in-file parser (or to exit-0) so a broken helper cannot crash 15 hooks at once.
mechanics.risk: Shared-module import failure becomes a single point of failure for all Stop hooks — mitigated by per-hook import fallback; migration must be verified hook-by-hook against the existing meta/tests fixtures.
mechanics.expectation_source: todo.md 07-24 shared-helper item (extends its scope by one module)
mechanics.evidence: 13 implementations enumerated above; clean-pass-unlogged design at session-prime-cascade-hook.py:41-43; fire-log shows zero mechanism that would surface a parser-dead state.

mechanics.mechanic: Import-time compile vs per-call string patterns
mechanics.expected: No written mandate; functional requirement is only that patterns are valid and evaluation is deterministic
mechanics.actual: Mixed but functionally equivalent: precompiled at import in 4 files (git-guard REPO_TOKENS; session-prime-cascade EXCLUDE; signal-ingest BRIEF_HEADER/ANALYST_ATTRIBUTION; structural-output POSITION_IMPLICATION_RE/TIER_MARKER_RE); everything else is per-call re.search over string lists (671 patterns swept — 100% compile clean). Each hook runs as its own process, so Python's internal regex cache makes per-call cost equivalent to precompiled. One latent nuance: has_any()/any() short-circuits, so a hypothetically-invalid pattern late in a list would raise re.error only on messages where earlier patterns miss — and in the 15 hooks without outer wrappers that would be an exit-1 crash; currently zero such patterns exist.
mechanics.expectation_source: Code-level census (no doc surface specifies a convention)
mechanics.evidence: Probe 1: importlib sweep over all 19 modules, 671 list-pattern compiles, 0 invalid. Precompiled-object sites verified by read (git-guard:50, session-prime-cascade:66, signal-ingest:141-151, structural-output:171-172).

mechanics.mechanic: Executable test coverage per hook
mechanics.expected: House standard: embedded --selftest with fixtures where present; README §How to add a new hook step 3 requires testing (pass/fail/recursion/empty-stdin cases) at minimum
mechanics.actual: Embedded --selftest: 2/19 — anti-fabrication (22 checks) and session-prime-cascade (21 real-corpus fixtures); both re-run live in this audit, both PASS. External execution fixtures (meta/tests/test_framework_codifications.py, BAD/GOOD/NEUTRAL exit-code tests via subprocess): 5 more — segment-trajectory, nth-order-cascade, bypass-route, bottoms-up, antifragility-mn. ZERO executable coverage: 12/19 — analyst-pt-context, borrowed-vs-firstprinciples, reasoning-tagging, llm-native-reasoning, llm-native-priming, macro-anchor, structural-output, signal-ingest-cascade, cascade-enforcement, session-prime-hook, session-start-hook, git-guard-pretooluse. Notably structural-output and macro-anchor are two of the highest-firing hooks (118 and 133 logged fires) with no fixtures at all.
mechanics.expectation_source: Task house standards + hooks/README.md §How to add a new hook + §Testing hooks manually
mechanics.evidence: Grep 'selftest' in hooks/*.py: 2 files. Grep of each hook name in meta/tests/test_framework_codifications.py: 5 covered (segment-trajectory 11 mentions, bottoms-up 19, antifragility 11, nth-order and bypass-route execution blocks at lines ~660-720), 12 with zero mentions. Both embedded selftests executed: 'SELFTEST PASS (22 checks)' and 'selftest OK — 21/21'.

mechanics.mechanic: git-guard-pretooluse regression fixtures (security-critical special case)
mechanics.expected: The guard has been patched in 3 waves for demonstrated bypass classes (v2 after 13 K3 bypasses 2026-07-19; long-flag/find/rmtree/hooksPath-case/redirect classes G-23..G-26 on 2026-07-20, per the ALREADY-FIXED list); house precedent (anti-fab, session-prime-cascade) is that every adjudicated fix ADDS a fixture
mechanics.actual: Zero embedded or external fixtures for git-guard. Every patched bypass class (rm --recursive --force long-flags, find -delete over repo tokens, interpreter rmtree, core.hookspath case-insensitivity, truncating-redirect protected list) exists only as live regex — a future regression (e.g. a regex edit that re-narrows the long-flag alternation) would be undetectable until the next red-team. The 2026-07-20 fixes were verified by live probing (hook-fire-log BLOCK lines), not by repeatable fixtures.
mechanics.expectation_source: anti-fabrication-hook.py:451-454 fixture rule ('each future FP adjudication ADDS a fixture') as house precedent; git-guard's own patch history in its docstring
mechanics.evidence: git-guard-pretooluse.py contains no selftest; meta/tests has 0 mentions. hook-fire-log.md 2026-07-20 19:24-19:28Z shows the bypass-class probes were run manually once. Proposed shape: embedded --selftest with one (cmd, expect-block/allow) fixture per patched class plus the demonstrated-live commit-message false-positive class — same pattern as the two existing selftests, small diff, deterministic.

mechanics.mechanic: Snapshot-drift discipline + recursion-guard jq dependency: header mandates 're-sync on drift, upstream is the system-managed copy'; recursion guard is `jq -r '.stop_hook_active'` == "true"
mechanics.expected: House standard: never silently skip enforcement; a documented re-sync obligation implies some detectable drift signal; recursion guard should degrade safely

If jq is absent/fails, stop_hook_active resolves to empty string, guard goes inert and the hook can re-block recursively (guard fails CLOSED toward blocking, inverse of house fail-open). patches.rationale: Fixes the confirmed DEFECT: README.md L45 and docstring L21-22 spec 'only enforce when cwd is inside the repo', but cwd.startswith(_REPO_ROOT) with no separator check also passes sibling dirs sharing the name prefix — probe on the live file returned in_scope()=True for /home/user/LLMNA-backup and /home/user/LLMNAX (want False). The fix requires an exact root match OR root + os.sep prefix, keeping the any()/ENFORCEMENT_PATHS structure (minimal diff, list semantics unchanged). rstrip(os.sep) additionally tolerates a trailing-slash CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, which under the old code would have EXCLUDED the repo root itself (cwd never carries a trailing slash) — no regression, one adjacent edge closed. Validated on a scratch copy: boundary probe 5/5, e2e main() still blocks fabricated $999B in-scope (rc=2) and exits 0 from the sibling dir. NOTE for parent: the identical bare-startswith idiom exists in all 16 other hooks (in_scope via ENFORCEMENT_PATHS / REPO_ROOT / REPO_GUARD) — same fix pattern replicates; sweep is a 07-24 candidate, out of this patch's assigned scope.

patches.rationale: Fixes the confirmed fall-through: when the newest assistant message is tool_use-only, the old `if parts: return` skipped it and returned an OLDER assistant message's text, re-scanning (and potentially re-firing on) an already-adjudicated message at a later Stop — violating docstring L8 'scans the most recent assistant message'. The fix makes the newest message authoritative BUT groups by message.id rather than naively returning on the first assistant entry, because Claude Code transcripts can split one API response across consecutive JSONL entries (one content block per line, shared message.id): a naive early-return would return "" for a split [text, tool_use] final response and silently skip scanning its text — a missed-fabrication class, violating the house 'never silently skip enforcement' standard and strictly worse than the LOW-severity duplicate-fire it replaces. Grouping semantics: walk stops at the first assistant entry whose id is absent or differs from the newest id, so older messages are unreachable; id-less shapes (README fixture, `msg = entry` top-level-role shape) degrade safely to newest-entry-only. Verified: probe A (tool-only final + fabricated number only in older msg) exits 2 pre-patch (defect repro), 0 post-patch; probes B (split-entry final with fabricated text) and C (plain final text) both still exit 2 post-patch. NOTE for the 07-24 review slot, not this patch: the same copy-pasted helper exists in 7 sibling Stop hooks (segment-trajectory, nth-order-cascade, bypass-route, bottoms-up, antifragility-mn, analyst-pt-context, borrowed-vs-firstprinciples, signal-ingest-cascade) and the inline variants (structural-output L190-218, macro-anchor, reasoning-tagging, llm-native-reasoning) share the same skip-to-older-text semantics — a repo-wide sweep is out of scope for this single-finding patch.

                "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "OLD MESSAGE 42.42%"}]}}
        _usr = {"type": "user", "message": {"role": "user", "content": "ok"}}
        _tool = {"type": "assistant", "message": {"id": "msg_2", "role": "assistant",
                 "content": [{"type": "tool_use", "id": "t1", "name": "Bash", "input": {}}]}}
        _txt2 = {"type": "assistant", "message": {"id": "msg_2", "role": "assistant",
                 "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "NEW 7.77%"}]}}

patches.new_string:
# G-21 fix (2026-07-21): the old <200-char skip ("probably a status
    # update") was a size hole — a short message consisting solely of a
    # fabricated claim was never checked, contradicting Critical Rule #7's
    # "scans every assistant message". No-number short messages still exit 0

patches.rationale: Removes the undocumented 200-char floor (G-21). Spec (CLAUDE.md Critical Rule #7 + Enforcement-hooks bullet 'Scans every assistant message') documents no length carve-out. The gate was near-pure enforcement hole: short status updates without numeric patterns already exit 0 one line later via find_violations returning empty, and the expensive path (repo-grounding greps, 15s shared budget) only engages when a claim pattern actually matches — so removal adds cost ONLY for short messages containing uncited numbers, which is exactly the enforcement target. FP risk is bounded by the existing leniency mechanisms (hedge/citation window covers the whole short message since WINDOW=350 > 200; common short numbers ground via grep -F substring semantics; three-valued grounding never fires on infra error). Verified: unpatched hook RC=0 on 35-char fabricated-claim message; patched RC=2 with actionable stderr; clean/hedged/grounded short messages all RC=0. Recursion guard (stop_hook_active), in_scope, and budget semantics untouched.

patches.old_string:
check("log_fire never raises + returns line on write-failure", ok)

    FIRE_LOG, RESEARCH_DIR = _real_log, _real_dir
patches.new_string:
check("log_fire never raises + returns line on write-failure", ok)

        # 21-24 G-21 fix (2026-07-21): no message-length floor. End-to-end
        # through main() (synthetic transcript + stdin; cwd pinned to repo
        # root because in_scope() is cwd-based; FIRE_LOG stays in td). A
        # SHORT message that IS the fabricated claim must fire; short
        # no-number / hedged / repo-grounded messages must still pass.
        FIRE_LOG = os.path.join(td, "test-fire-log.md")

        def run_main(msg):
            import io
            tp = os.path.join(td, "transcript.jsonl")
            with open(tp, "w") as fh:
                fh.write(json.dumps({"message": {"role": "assistant",
                         "content": [{"type": "text", "text": msg}]}}) + "\n")
            _stdin, _stderr, _cwd = sys.stdin, sys.stderr, os.getcwd()
            sys.stdin = io.StringIO(json.dumps({"transcript_path": tp}))
            sys.stderr = io.StringIO()
            os.chdir(ENFORCEMENT_PATHS[0])
            try:
                main()
                return 0
            except SystemExit as e:
                return e.code or 0
            finally:
                sys.stdin, sys.stderr = _stdin, _stderr
                os.chdir(_cwd)

        rc = run_main("GEV backlog margin is 83.4719% now.")
        check(f"short fabricated message fires rc=2 (got {rc})", rc == 2)
        rc = run_main("Done. Committed and pushed both files.")
        check(f"short no-number message passes rc=0 (got {rc})", rc == 0)
        rc = run_main("Margin is ~83.4719% (my estimate).")
        check(f"short hedged message passes rc=0 (got {rc})", rc == 0)
        rc = run_main("The 30.2% figure is unchanged.")
        check(f"short repo-grounded message passes rc=0 (got {rc})", rc == 0)

    FIRE_LOG, RESEARCH_DIR = _real_log, _real_dir

patches.old_string:
# Currency: $78B, $725 billion, $1.2T, €10.3B
    (r"[\$€£¥]\s*\d+(?:[.,]\d+)*\s*(?:billion|million|trillion|[kKmMbBtT])\b", "currency"),
patches.new_string:
# Currency: $78B, $725 billion, $1.2T, €10.3B; G-22 fix 2026-07-21 —
    # magnitude suffix now OPTIONAL (a bare price like $145 is a numerical
    # claim per Critical Rule #7) + two-letter styles (bn/mn/mm/tn) that
    # failed the single-letter [kKmMbBtT]\b alternation.
    (r"[\$€£¥]\s*\d+(?:[.,]\d+)*\s*(?:billion|million|trillion|bn|mn|mm|tn|[kKmMbBtT])?\b", "currency"),
patches.rationale: G-22 core fix. (1) Suffix optional: bare prices ('$145', '$12,400', '$14.5') become currency claims, matching Critical Rule #7's '$X' mandate; the trailing \b anchors after the last digit (via backtracking to the empty suffix), so '$100km'-style tokens still decline. (2) Two-letter magnitudes bn/mn/mm/tn added BEFORE the single-letter class: '$1.5bn' previously died because 'b' matched [bB] but \b failed before 'n', and no other alternative existed — probe-confirmed re.search -> None pre-patch, '$1.5bn' full match post-patch. Greedy alternation keeps '$78B'/'$725 billion' matching identically (regression-probed). FP risk is bounded by the unchanged downstream layers: hedge patterns in CITATION_PATTERNS ('roughly $145' stays clean, probe-confirmed), the 200-char floor, and the three-valued repo-grounding pass which fires only on grep-proven absence. Known cost: more needles per message marginally raises G-29 budget-exhaustion odds (separate known-open, not worsened structurally).

patches.old_string:
# Energy units
    (r"\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:GW|MW|kW|TWh|MWh|kWh)\b", "energy"),
patches.new_string:
# Energy units (GWh listed before GW — GW's \b fails before a trailing
    # 'h' and no *Wh alternative starts with G, so bare GWh never matched)
    (r"\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:GWh|GW|MW|kW|TWh|MWh|kWh)\b", "energy"),
patches.rationale: Fixes the confirmed GAP: 'GWh' matched nothing because the GW alternative's trailing \b fails before 'h' and no *Wh alternative starts with G (MWh/kWh only work because they are explicit alternatives reached after MW/kW backtrack). Adding GWh as the first alternative closes the gap for GWh-denominated storage/energy claims (live unit in the VST/CEG/GEV power-thesis cohort) with zero behavior change for every previously matched unit: probe-verified that GW, MW, kW, TWh, MWh, kWh and case-insensitive forms all still match identically, and non-unit suffixes ('5 GWhr', '5 GWx') still correctly fail the \b — no new false-positive class. Minimal one-alternation-token diff, deterministic, matches house style of a short rationale comment on the pattern line.

patches.old_string: # Quarterly numbers near $: Q1 FY27 $78B (looser, catches table cells)
patches.new_string: # Quarterly numbers near currency: Q1 FY27 $78B / ¥500 (looser, catches table cells)
patches.rationale: Doc line for the quarterly_financial pattern said 'near $' while the intent (and now the regex) is the full currency-symbol class; keeps the comment byte-consistent with the charclass so the expectation source and code cannot drift apart again.

The confirmed GAP: quarterly_financial charclass omitted ¥ although the currency pattern (L63) includes it, so JPY-denominated quarterly table cells (covered Japan supply-chain names) escaped the needle class entirely. Probe-confirmed: 'Q1 FY27 revenue was ¥ 500' -> [] before, ['quarterly_financial'] after; $/€/£ behavior unchanged. FP risk is nil-adds-nothing-new: yen cells get exactly the same downstream treatment as dollar cells — 350-char citation window, then three-valued repo grounding (grep rc=0 -> GROUNDED), so properly-filed JPY numbers still pass. Deliberately scoped to parity with L63: no full-width ￥ (U+FFE5) added to either class (would be a separate two-line UPGRADE row, not this GAP), and bare '¥ 500' without quarterly context remains missed per known-open G-22 (magnitude-suffix requirement) — not re-fixed here.

patches.rationale: Spec correction, not mechanism change: L117's own comment claims '~$78B' coverage, the docstring lists '~, ≈' as hedges, and Critical Rule #7(c) accepts bare '~' — but r'~\s*\d' requires a digit immediately after the tilde, so the currency symbol defeats the hedge and a properly-tilde-hedged estimate absent from the corpus false-fires (probe-confirmed: cited=False for '~$78B', '≈ $1.2 billion'). Fix adds an optional currency-symbol class — the same [\$€£¥] class the NUMERICAL currency pattern (L63) uses — making the new pattern a strict superset of the old match set: nothing previously hedged loses hedging, and only tilde/approx-prefixed currency amounts gain it, so FP-grant risk is minimal. Comment column alignment (col 43) preserved.

patches.new_string:
def classify_violations(violations: list[tuple[str, str, str]]):
    """Split violations into (fabricated, inconclusive) with diags, under one
    shared time budget. GROUNDED dropped (file = source of truth); SKIP
    dropped silently (degenerate needle, never logged). Duplicate needles
    ground ONCE per invocation (2026-07-21): the same figure repeated in a
    table (header + row + summary) previously re-grepped each occurrence,
    burning shared budget for zero information — an identical needle must
    classify identically within one run — and feeding the G-29
    INCONCLUSIVE-routing class on table-heavy messages."""
    import time
    deadline = time.monotonic() + BUDGET_SECONDS
    fabricated, inconclusive = [], []
    cache = {}  # stripped-needle -> (status, diag); keyed on the same strip
    # ground_needle applies, so ' 83.4719%' / '83.4719%' share one verdict.
    for v in violations:
        key = v[0].strip()
        if key not in cache:
            cache[key] = ground_needle(v[0], deadline)
        status, diag = cache[key]
        if status == FABRICATED:
patches.rationale: The memoization itself. Cache is per-invocation (created inside classify_violations, dies with the call — no cross-message state), keyed on v[0].strip() which is byte-identical to the strip ground_needle applies at its own entry, so the cache grants exactly the verdict a re-ground would compute. Kills the duplicate-needle budget-waste class (G-29 exposure on table-heavy messages: probe showed 2 grep calls for 2 identical needles pre-patch, 1 post-patch). Zero FP risk — identical needle must classify identically within one run; caching a BudgetExceeded INCONCLUSIVE is also safe because the shared deadline is monotonic (once expired, stays expired). Side benefit: removes pre-patch nondeterminism where occurrence 1 could route INCONCLUSIVE on a transient infra error while its duplicate got FABRICATED. Per-occurrence appends unchanged: each duplicate still lands in fabricated/inconclusive with its own context window, so fire-message counts and log n= are identical to pre-patch on the FABRICATED path. No behavior change for unique-needle messages.

patches.new_string:
def classify_violations(violations: list[tuple[str, str, str]]):
    """Split violations into (fabricated, inconclusive) with diags, under one
    shared time budget. GROUNDED dropped (file = source of truth); SKIP
    dropped silently (degenerate needle, never logged).

    Duplicate needles memoized (G-29 mitigation 2026-07-21): a dense message
    restating one headline number (TL;DR + table + prose) used to burn a
    full grep pass PER REPEAT, so trailing needles hit the shared budget and
    fell to INCONCLUSIVE — systematically unenforced. Budget now scales with
    UNIQUE needle strings. Caching INCONCLUSIVE too is deliberate:
    ground_needle already retried once; a duplicate re-retry only re-burns
    budget, and INCONCLUSIVE is pass+log either way (never a false FIRE).
    Keyed on the raw match string — differently-spaced forms of one number
    still grep separately (their diags/matched_form must stay distinct for
    FP adjudication). Residual accepted tradeoff (K3 r2 #6): enough DISTINCT
    slow needles can still starve the tail past the deadline."""
    import time
    deadline = time.monotonic() + BUDGET_SECONDS
    fabricated, inconclusive = [], []
    cache = {}
    for v in violations:
        if v[0] in cache:
            status, diag = cache[v[0]]
        else:
            status, diag = ground_needle(v[0], deadline)
            cache[v[0]] = (status, diag)
        if status == FABRICATED:
            fabricated.append((v, diag))
        elif status == INCONCLUSIVE:
            inconclusive.append((v, diag))
    return fabricated, inconclusive
patches.rationale: G-29 minimal mitigation: memoize grounding results per unique needle string so the shared 15s budget scales with UNIQUE needles, not raw match count. Kills the duplicate-burn class (the finding's risk note: 'duplicates ... burn the same budget') — the most common real-world path to trailing-needle INCONCLUSIVE, since dense messages restate one headline figure in TL;DR + table + prose. Zero FP risk: identical needle string -> identical grep verdict by construction; caching INCONCLUSIVE is conservative (pass+log either way, and ground_needle already retried once internally). Deliberately keyed on the raw match string, not the collapsed form, so per-form diags/matched_form stay distinct for FP adjudication (docstring L249-264 contract). Does NOT change the documented deadline semantics (L186-191): distinct slow needles can still starve the tail — that rebalance (per-needle floors or single-pass multi-needle grep) is a redesign deferred to the 07-24 review-grade slot. Verified: patched scratchpad copy SELFTEST PASS (26 checks); probe with 0.3s/grep + 0.4s budget on 3x-repeated needle went fab=2/inc=1 (original) -> fab=3/inc=0 (patched); end-to-end main() with fake transcript fired exit 2 with all 3 duplicates on one FIRE line.

Exact code (written to /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/probe_rt_gap.py, phase-aware via PHASE=pre|post):

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Probe: reasoning-tagging-hook paren-form operator gap.

Feeds synthetic transcripts to the live hook via stdin JSON and asserts exit
codes. PHASE=pre (default) asserts current behavior (documents the gap);
PHASE=post asserts behavior after the [~=<>] operator-completeness patch.
Read-only on the repo: hook is invoked as a subprocess, never modified.
"""
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

HOOK = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/reasoning-tagging-hook.py"
CWD = "/home/user/LLMNA"  # in_scope() requires cwd under repo root
PHASE = os.environ.get("PHASE", "pre")


def run_hook(assistant_text: str) -> int:
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", suffix=".jsonl", delete=False) as f:
        f.write(json.dumps({
            "message": {
                "role": "assistant",
                "content": [{"type": "text", "text": assistant_text}],
            }
        }) + "\n")
        transcript = f.name
    payload = json.dumps({"transcript_path": transcript})
    proc = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, HOOK], input=payload, capture_output=True,
        text=True, cwd=CWD,
        env={**os.environ, "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR": CWD},
    )
    os.unlink(transcript)
    return proc.returncode


# (text, expected_rc_pre_patch, expected_rc_post_patch, label)
CASES = [
    ("Base view on this name: P(bull)>70% into the print.", 0, 2, "paren-form > (THE GAP)"),
    ("Base view on this name: P(bear)<20% into the print.", 0, 2, "paren-form < (THE GAP)"),
    ("Base view on this name: P(bull)=70% into the print.", 2, 2, "paren-form = (control)"),
    ("Base view on this name: P(bull)~65% into the print.", 2, 2, "paren-form ~ (control)"),
    ("Base view: P(bull)>70% (my model) into the print.", 0, 0, "paren > with hedge -> exempt"),
    ("T2 mgmt guidance: P(bull)>70% into the print.", 0, 0, "paren > with tier label -> exempt post-patch"),
    ("P(bull)>70% into the print [T1 SEC 10-Q].", 0, 0, "paren > with trailing tier label -> exempt post-patch"),
    ("Bare inequality: P>65% on the follow-through move.", 2, 2, "bare-P > (pattern 1, unchanged)"),
    ("P(base) is residual after the two tails.", 0, 0, "paren-P without operator -> never fires"),
]

fails = 0
print(f"== reasoning-tagging paren-operator probe (PHASE={PHASE}) ==")
for text, pre, post, label in CASES:
    want = pre if PHASE == "pre" else post
    rc = run_hook(text)
    ok = rc == want
    fails += 0 if ok else 1
    print(f"  rc={rc} want={want} {'PASS' if ok else 'FAIL'} :: {label}")

print(f"== {'ALL PASS' if fails == 0 else str(fails) + ' FAILURES'} ==")
sys.exit(0 if fails == 0 else 1)

patches.rationale: Fixes the confirmed DEFECT: '.' does not match newline without re.DOTALL, so a T1-T4 label on the adjacent line never satisfied the docstring-L29 'nearby' proximity exemption — a false-positive class on correctly-tiered multi-line thesis blocks (the house Conviction Format itself puts 'P(bull case): X%' on its own line). [\s\S]{0,200} is newline-transparent while keeping the exact 200-char proximity budget, so a label >200 chars away still does NOT exempt (probe-verified: cross-line fixture rc 2->0; untagged and >200-char fixtures stay rc=2; same-line stays rc=0). Chosen over re.DOTALL flag to keep the change local to the two patterns instead of altering matching semantics for all 40+ exemption patterns sharing the re.search call.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Selftest — exemption/trigger fixtures (added 2026-07-21 with the cross-line
# tier-label fix). Run: python3 reasoning-tagging-hook.py --selftest
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SELFTEST = [
    # (name, text, expect_exempt, expect_claims)
    ("same-line tier label exempts",
     "T1 Samsung earnings call: P~65% HBM4 share holds", True, True),
    ("cross-line tier label exempts (2026-07-21 fix)",
     "Bull case sourced T1 (Samsung earnings call)\nP~65% HBM4 share holds", True, True),
    ("untagged claim fires",
     "Odds check: P~65% that MU raises HBM guidance in Q3.", False, True),
    ("tier label >200 chars away does NOT exempt",
     "T1 Samsung earnings call\n" + "x" * 220 + "\nP~65% HBM4 share holds", False, True),
    ("hedged claim exempts",
     "P~65% (my model) HBM4 share holds", True, True),
]


def selftest():
    failures = []
    for name, text, want_exempt, want_claims in SELFTEST:
        got_exempt = has_exemption(text)
        got_claims = bool(find_probability_claims(text))
        if got_exempt != want_exempt or got_claims != want_claims:
            failures.append(f"FAIL {name}: exempt={got_exempt} (want {want_exempt}) "
                            f"claims={got_claims} (want {want_claims})")
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(SELFTEST)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        selftest()
    main()

def _selftest() -> int:
    """Fixtures for transcript-shape parsing (2026-07-21 audit).

    load_last_assistant_message() must see assistant text in BOTH transcript
    shapes -- nested {"message": {...}} and flat {"role": ...} -- so a format
    flatten cannot leave this hook silently inert. Run:
        python3 reasoning-tagging-hook.py --selftest
    Exit 0 = all fixtures pass.
    """
    import io
    import tempfile

    fixtures = [
        ("nested", '{"message": {"role": "assistant", "content": '
                   '[{"type": "text", "text": "nested-shape claim"}]}}', True),
        ("flat", '{"role": "assistant", "content": '
                 '[{"type": "text", "text": "flat-shape claim"}]}', True),
        ("flat-string-content", '{"role": "assistant", '
                                '"content": "flat-string claim"}', True),
        ("flat-user-skipped", '{"role": "user", "content": '
                              '[{"type": "text", "text": "user text"}]}', False),
        ("message-not-dict", '{"message": "not-a-dict"}', False),
    ]
    failures = 0
    for name, line, want_text in fixtures:
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", suffix=".jsonl",
                                         delete=False) as tf:
            tf.write(line + "\n")
            tpath = tf.name
        stdin_saved = sys.stdin
        sys.stdin = io.StringIO(json.dumps({"transcript_path": tpath}))
        try:
            text = load_last_assistant_message()
        finally:
            sys.stdin = stdin_saved
            os.unlink(tpath)
        ok = bool(text) == want_text
        failures += 0 if ok else 1
        print(f"[selftest] {name}: {'PASS' if ok else 'FAIL'}")
    print(f"[selftest] {len(fixtures) - failures}/{len(fixtures)} fixtures pass")
    return failures


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        sys.exit(_selftest())
    main()

Discriminating power verified: 5/5 PASS (exit 0) on the patched parser; 3/5 with "flat" and "flat-string-content" FAIL (exit 2) when grafted onto the ORIGINAL parser — the fixtures pin exactly the behavior this patch changes. --selftest dispatch runs before any stdin read, so normal Stop-hook invocation is unaffected.

matches.append({

def log_fire(claims) -> str:
    """Append a house-format fire line to meta/hook-fire-log.md. Never
    raises (fail-open). Returns the line EITHER WAY so the caller embeds it
    in the block feedback — the transcript backs up the log if the write
    fails (anti-fabrication-hook K3 r2 #8 pattern). Added 2026-07-21: the
    retirement triggers above were uncomputable — no activation log
    existed for the monthly audit to review. verdict:pending flips to
    TRUE-FIRE / FALSE-POSITIVE at monthly adjudication."""
    line = ""
    try:
        from datetime import datetime, timezone
        ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%SZ")
        first = claims[0]["match"] if claims else ""
        line = (f"- {ts} reasoning-tagging-hook FIRE "
                f"(n={len(claims)} first={first!r} verdict:pending)")
        with open(FIRE_LOG_PATH, "a") as lf:
            lf.write(line + "\n")
    except Exception:
        pass
    return line


def main():
patches.rationale: The port itself: house-format line (matches the '- YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ {hook-name} FIRE' spec at hook-fire-log.md header), fail-open try/except like every sibling hook, n= + first-match repr so the >30%-FP retirement trigger is adjudicatable from the log (anti-fab precedent), verdict:pending for the monthly flip discipline. Returning the line implements the transcript-backup guarantee: a disk/perms failure cannot silently recreate the uncounted-fire condition this patch kills.

def selftest() -> int:
    """Fixtures for the 2026-07-21 log_fire addition. Run:
    python3 reasoning-tagging-hook.py --selftest. Log lines go to a temp
    file (monkeypatches FIRE_LOG_PATH), never the real hook-fire-log."""
    global FIRE_LOG_PATH
    import tempfile
    failures = []
    ran = {"n": 0}

    def check(name, cond):
        ran["n"] += 1
        if not cond:
            failures.append(name)

    _real_log = FIRE_LOG_PATH
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
        FIRE_LOG_PATH = Path(td) / "test-fire-log.md"

        # 1-3 log_fire writes + returns a house-format line
        claims = find_probability_claims("NVDA thesis P~65% upside case")
        check("fixture text triggers", len(claims) >= 1)
        line = log_fire(claims)
        check("line written+returned",
              "reasoning-tagging-hook FIRE" in line and FIRE_LOG_PATH.exists())
        check("verdict field present", "verdict:pending" in line)

        # 4 write-failure: silent, line still RETURNED (transcript backup)
        FIRE_LOG_PATH = Path("/nonexistent-dir/x.md")
        try:
            line = log_fire(claims)
            ok = "reasoning-tagging-hook" in line
        except Exception:
            ok = False
        check("log_fire never raises + returns line on write-failure", ok)

        # 5-6 trigger/exemption sanity (pre-existing behavior, pinned)
        check("tagged claim exempt", has_exemption("P~65% (my model)"))
        check("meta-discussion exempt", has_exemption("the hook fired"))

    FIRE_LOG_PATH = _real_log
    if failures:
        print("SELFTEST FAIL:\n  " + "\n  ".join(failures))
        return 1
    print(f"SELFTEST PASS ({ran['n']} checks)")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        sys.exit(selftest())
    main()

Six checks pin the new mechanic (write+return, verdict field, never-raises-on-write-failure transcript backup) plus two pre-existing trigger/exemption behaviors so future edits to the regex lists have a baseline. FIRE_LOG_PATH monkeypatch guarantees the suite never writes the committed log (anti-fab selftest precedent).
