patches.new_string:
# Selftest — regression fixtures for the 5 trigger + 33 exemption patterns.
# Run: python3 reasoning-tagging-hook.py --selftest  (exit 0 = all pass).
# Fixture rule (house): every future FP adjudication or pattern edit ADDS a
# fixture pinning the newly observed shape. The GAP-pinned fixtures at the
# tail encode CURRENT behavior deliberately (newline breaks tier proximity;
# paren-form operator misses) — flip them in the same diff as any pattern fix.
# Exercises only the pure regex functions: no stdin, no transcript, no log.
SELFTEST = [
    # (name, text, want_claims, want_exempt) — hook fires iff claims and not exempt
    # Trigger positives, no exemption (would FIRE):
    ("P~ scalar fires", "Base case P~65% on the demand ramp; sizing unchanged.", True, False),
    ("P= spaced fires", "Odds check: P = 65 % that the ramp lands in Q3.", True, False),
    ("P~ decimal fires", "Updated read: P~62.5% after the print.", True, False),
    ("lowercase p fires (IGNORECASE)", "call it p~65% that the ramp lands.", True, False),
    ("P(paren)= fires", "P(bull)=55% after the print.", True, False),
    ("P~ range fires", "P~30-40% that the ramp slips a quarter.", True, False),
    ("range+keyword fires", "There is a 30-40% probability the ramp slips.", True, False),
    ("~% upside fires", "~15% upside from here if the multiple holds.", True, False),
    # Trigger negatives (no probability claim at all):
    ("bare % is not a claim", "Revenue grew 30% YoY and margins expanded.", False, False),
    ("~% growth not keyworded", "~30% growth is the consensus number.", False, False),
    ("GDP no word-boundary P", "GDP=3% was the print.", False, False),
    # Exemptions (claim present, message passes):
    ("tier before claim", "Tagged T2 from the call transcript - P~65% on the ramp.", True, True),
    ("tier after claim", "P~65% on the ramp (T2, management guidance).", True, True),
    ("hedge (my model)", "P~65% (my model) on the ramp.", True, True),
    ("hedge (directional)", "(directional) read: ~25% downside if the multiple compresses.", True, True),
    ("inference-log ref", "Logged to inference-log.md: P~65% on the ramp.", True, True),
    ("meta 'hook' word", "The hook fires on P~65% shaped claims.", True, True),
    ("N-order band P>80%", "1st order (P>80%): direct pull-forward of orders.", True, True),
    ("URL source", "P~65% per https://example.com/filing.", True, True),
    ("repo file ref", "research/companies/NVDA/thesis.md updated with P~65%.", True, True),
    ("per SEC", "per SEC filing the backlog is real; P~65% conversion.", True, True),
    # KNOWN-GAP pins (current behavior on purpose — see comment above):
    ("GAP: newline breaks tier proximity", "T1 receipts below.\nP~65% on the ramp.", True, False),
    ("GAP: paren claim misses tier exemption", "T1 filing basis: P(bull)=55%.", True, False),
    ("GAP: paren + inequality not caught", "P(bear)>30% now.", False, False),
]


def selftest():
    failures = []
    for name, text, want_claims, want_exempt in SELFTEST:
        got_claims = bool(find_probability_claims(text))
        got_exempt = has_exemption(text)
        if got_claims != want_claims or got_exempt != want_exempt:
            failures.append(
                f"FAIL {name}: claims={got_claims} (want {want_claims}), "
                f"exempt={got_exempt} (want {want_exempt})"
            )
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(SELFTEST)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        selftest()
    main()

Single unique anchor point (the file's only __main__ block, L241-242); zero behavior change on the live Stop-hook path — the module-level list costs one tuple-list construction, main() and its recursion guard are untouched (verified by e2e probe: untagged claim exit 2, tagged exit 0, stop_hook_active exit 0, empty stdin exit 0). The 3 KNOWN-GAP fixtures deliberately encode the two open pattern defects this audit found by external probing — missing-DOTALL tier proximity across newlines, and the paren-form operator misses — so the future pattern fix ships with a deterministic red-to-green fixture flip instead of unverified regex edits (exactly the risk named in the finding). patches.new_string:
def _git(args):
    p = subprocess.run(
        ["git", *args], cwd=REPO_ROOT, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15
    )
    if p.returncode != 0:
        # Non-zero WITHOUT raising (exit 128/129: corruption, not-a-repo) would
        # otherwise parse as "no changes" — a silent enforcement skip the
        # FAIL-OPEN contract forbids. Log ERROR, then fail open on empty stdout.
        err = (p.stderr or "").strip().splitlines()
        log_fire("ERROR", f"git-{args[0]}-exit-{p.returncode}: {err[0] if err else ''}"[:160])
    return p.stdout
patches.rationale: Core fix. A git failure that returns non-zero without raising (exit 128 repo corruption, exit 129 not-a-repo — both empirically confirmed) currently yields empty stdout, parsed as 'no changes', silent exit 0 with zero telemetry; only raising failures (TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError) reach the logged except-handler at lines 232-234. This violates the docstring FAIL-OPEN contract ('any exception → log ERROR, exit 0') and the v2 telemetry charter ('v1 logged nothing, which made its own falsifier unverifiable'). Fix logs a house-format ERROR line (subcommand + exit code + first stderr line, truncated to 160 chars matching the existing except-handler style) and still returns p.stdout so any surviving git command keeps contributing keys (partial enforcement beats none) and fail-open is preserved. Log volume is bounded: worst case 3 ERROR lines per Stop event in a fully-dead git env, because changeset_files() never runs when changeset_keys() finds no keys — verified empirically. This restores the 30-day zero-fires falsifier's ability to disambiguate 'discipline perfect' from 'detection dead'.

def selftest():
    global FIRE_LOG
    failures = []
    for name, diff_text, expected in SELFTEST:
        a, r, _ = parse_diff_keys(diff_text)
        got = a - r
        if got != expected:
            failures.append(f"FAIL {name}:\n  expected {expected or '{}'}\n  got      {got or '{}'}")
    # Telemetry fixture (G-56, 2026-07-21): a git command exiting non-zero
    # WITHOUT raising must log ERROR — never silently parse as "no changes".
    _orig_log = FIRE_LOG
    try:
        import tempfile
        _tf = tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".md", delete=False)
        _tf.close()
        FIRE_LOG = _Path(_tf.name)
        _git(["rev-parse", "--verify", "no-such-ref-selftest"])
        _logged = _Path(_tf.name).read_text()
        if "ERROR" not in _logged or "git-rev-parse-exit-" not in _logged:
            failures.append(f"FAIL git-nonzero-exit telemetry:\n  expected ERROR log line\n  got      {_logged!r}")
    except Exception as e:
        failures.append(f"FAIL git-nonzero-exit telemetry: probe raised {type(e).__name__}: {e}")
    finally:
        FIRE_LOG = _orig_log
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{len(SELFTEST) + 1} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(SELFTEST) + 1}/{len(SELFTEST) + 1} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)
patches.rationale: Selftest fixture for the behavior change (house standard: every behavior change ships a fixture). Redirects FIRE_LOG to a temp file, runs a deterministic non-raising failing git command (rev-parse --verify on a nonexistent ref: exit 128, 'fatal: Needed a single revision' — verified in this repo), asserts the ERROR line landed, restores FIRE_LOG in finally. Counted as the 22nd fixture in both pass and fail messages. Wrapped in try/except so a git-less environment reports a clean FAIL instead of a traceback. Never touches the real hook-fire-log.md.

WINDOW v2.1 (2026-07-21, K3-Swarm G-19): committed leg of the changeset =
  refs/llmna/session-prime-cascade-last-stop..HEAD (marker ref advanced only
  on CLEAN passes), not bare `git show HEAD` — a turn landing multiple
  commits is enforced as ONE changeset. Pre-fix, a codification in HEAD~1
  under an unrelated HEAD commit escaped (FN), and a cascade completed one
  commit before the new ID false-positived (FP). Marker missing (fresh

patches.rationale: Docstring is the hook's CHARTER surface; the window semantics change must be documented where the v2 design is documented, including the accepted first-run residual (fresh containers have no marker until their first clean pass — refs/llmna/* is never pushed). Doc-sync note: the CLAUDE.md hook bullet and hooks/README row should gain a matching one-line WINDOW v2.1 mention during the already-booked 07-24 doc-sweep (G-02/G-05/G-06 batch); not patched here to keep this diff single-file.

Core mechanism, exactly the remedy the 07-20 adjudication booked (todo item 3: 'diff vs last-Stop ref, not bare HEAD'). A git ref (not a file) keeps state inside .git — deterministic, survives cwd resets, invisible to the untracked-files gate of stop-hook-git-check.sh, and its object is a gc root so the base commit can never be pruned out from under the diff. Missing-marker fallback is byte-identical to pre-fix behavior (no new failure class on fresh clones); base==head suppresses the stale re-inspection of last turn's HEAD, which was itself a small FP source. update-ref failure degrades to a wider window (enforcement-conservative), never to silence.

Both windows (keys and files) MUST use the identical committed leg or the FP direction reappears: the pass criterion is 'SESSION_PRIME in changeset_files()' paired against keys from the same range. Working-tree and staged legs unchanged. FN fix: marker..HEAD sees a codification in HEAD~1 (probe C). FP fix: a session-prime update one commit before the new ID is inside the same range (probe D).

def _window_selftest():
    """G-19 window fixtures: multi-commit turns in a throwaway git repo.

    Probe C (FN pre-fix): codification in HEAD~1 under an unrelated HEAD
    commit must be detected. Probe D (FP pre-fix): session-prime.md updated
    one commit BEFORE the new ID, same window, must count as cascaded."""
    import shutil, tempfile
    global REPO_ROOT
    saved, failures = REPO_ROOT, []
    tmp = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="spc-window-")
    try:
        REPO_ROOT = tmp

        def sh(*args):
            subprocess.run(["git", *args], cwd=tmp, capture_output=True,
                           text=True, check=True)

        def commit(msg, path, text):
            p = _Path(tmp) / path
            p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            p.write_text(text)
            sh("add", "-A")
            sh("-c", "user.email=t@t", "-c", "user.name=t",
               "-c", "commit.gpgsign=false", "commit", "-q", "-m", msg)

        bias = "research/meta/biases-watchlist.md"
        sh("init", "-q")
        commit("seed", bias, "# biases\n")
        _mark_clean_stop()  # marker = seed (the turn boundary)
        # Probe C: codify in HEAD~1, unrelated commit takes HEAD.
        commit("codify", bias, "# biases\n### B99 — window fixture bias\n")
        commit("unrelated", "research/notes.md", "noise\n")
        a, r, _ = changeset_keys()
        if "B99" not in (a - r):
            failures.append("FAIL window probe C: B99 in HEAD~1 not detected")
        if SESSION_PRIME in changeset_files():
            failures.append("FAIL window probe C: session-prime falsely present")
        # Probe D: remediation lands one commit BEFORE the next new ID.
        commit("prime", SESSION_PRIME, "prime\n")
        commit("codify2", bias,
               "# biases\n### B99 — window fixture bias\n### B98 — second fixture\n")
        a, r, _ = changeset_keys()
        if "B98" not in (a - r):
            failures.append("FAIL window probe D: B98 not detected")
        if SESSION_PRIME not in changeset_files():
            failures.append("FAIL window probe D: in-window session-prime update missed")
        # Clean pass advances the marker; the window must then be empty.
        _mark_clean_stop()
        a, r, _ = changeset_keys()
        if a - r:
            failures.append("FAIL window marker: keys survive a clean-pass advance")
    except Exception as e:
        failures.append(f"FAIL window fixtures errored: {type(e).__name__}: {e}")
    finally:
        REPO_ROOT = saved
        shutil.rmtree(tmp, ignore_errors=True)
    return failures

def in_scope() -> bool:
    # README hook convention (all Stop hooks): only enforce when cwd is inside
    # the repo. Moot under Architecture A project wiring, load-bearing under the
    # legacy ~/.claude/ fallback — without it a dirty LLMNA tree would block
    # Stops in unrelated repos (git ops here anchor on REPO_ROOT, not cwd).
    return _os.getcwd().startswith(REPO_ROOT)


# Update-class headers referencing existing IDs — never "new codification".
patches.rationale: Adds the documented scope guard (README.md 'Hook conventions', line 45: 'Scope guard: only enforce when cwd is inside the repo (prefix match on the dynamic root)') using the exact sibling pattern (nth-order-cascade-hook.py:134-135, bypass-route-hook.py:116-117, et al.). Uses _os (the file imports 'os as _os' on line 2 — plain 'os' is not bound here). Error class killed: under the retained legacy ~/.claude/ mirror-settings fallback this hook runs in every session regardless of project, and because all its git ops anchor on REPO_ROOT it would block Stop events in UNRELATED repos whenever the LLMNA tree holds an uncommitted new codification. Prefix-startswith (not realpath) deliberately matches all 14 siblings — no innovation beyond convention.

Wires the guard first in main(), matching sibling ordering (scope check precedes stdin read/recursion guard in all 14 Stop-hook siblings). Placed INSIDE the try block — unlike siblings — so an os.getcwd() failure (e.g. deleted cwd) routes through this file's own documented FAIL-OPEN path (log ERROR, exit 0) instead of an unhandled-traceback exit 1; SystemExit(0) passes through the existing 'except SystemExit: raise'. Probe-verified: out-of-scope exits 0 without touching git; in-scope synthetic positive still blocks with exit 2; stop_hook_active still short-circuits.

House standard: every behavior change ships with an embedded-selftest fixture. The guard is cwd-dependent so it cannot live in the diff-fixture table; these two assertions (in-repo cwd -> in scope, / -> out of scope) run first and feed the existing failures/exit-1 machinery. cwd is saved and restored so the 21 table fixtures are unaffected (verified: patched copy prints 'selftest OK — 21/21 fixtures pass').

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Post-apply probe: README wired-hooks section consistent for the session-prime-cascade GAP fix."""
import json, re, sys
R = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/README.md"
txt = open(R).read()
fails = []
# 1. Table row exists for the hook
if not re.search(r"^\| Stop \| `session-prime-cascade-hook\.py` \|", txt, re.M):
    fails.append("no wired-hooks table row for session-prime-cascade-hook.py")
# 2. Header count equals number of hook rows in the table (self-adjusting, not hardcoded)
m = re.search(r"## Wired hooks \((\d+) in", txt)
rows = re.findall(r"^\| (?:PreToolUse|SessionStart|UserPromptSubmit|Stop) \| `[^`]+\.py` \|", txt, re.M)
if not m or int(m.group(1)) != len(rows):
    fails.append(f"header count {m.group(1) if m else '?'} != table rows {len(rows)}")
# 3. Instrumentation line names the hook
inst = [l for l in txt.splitlines() if l.startswith("- Instrumentation:")]

    fails.append("instrumentation line does not name session-prime-cascade")
# 4. Recursion-guard count updated (14 -> 15)

    fails.append("recursion-guard Stop-hook count not updated to 15")
# 5. Row claim matches reality: hook is wired as a Stop hook in settings.json

stops = [h["command"] for grp in s["hooks"]["Stop"] for h in grp["hooks"]]

Standalone probe fixture (validated this session), at /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/probe_prime_stdin.py:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Read-only probe for session-prime-hook.py malformed-stdin path."""
import json, os, shutil, subprocess, sys

SCRATCH = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
SANDBOX = os.path.join(SCRATCH, "prime-sandbox")
REAL_HOOK = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/session-prime-hook.py"
PRIME_CONTENT = "# session-prime fixture\n- B45 regime priors\n"

def reset_sandbox():
    shutil.rmtree(SANDBOX, ignore_errors=True)
    meta = os.path.join(SANDBOX, "research", "meta")
    os.makedirs(meta)
    with open(os.path.join(meta, "session-prime.md"), "w") as f:
        f.write(PRIME_CONTENT)
    open(os.path.join(meta, "hook-fire-log.md"), "w").close()

def run_case(hook, label, stdin_bytes):
    reset_sandbox()
    env = dict(os.environ, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=SANDBOX)
    p = subprocess.run(["python3", hook], input=stdin_bytes, cwd=SANDBOX,
                       env=env, capture_output=True, timeout=30)
    log = open(os.path.join(SANDBOX, "research", "meta", "hook-fire-log.md")).read()
    injected = b"additionalContext" in p.stdout
    print(f"[{label}] exit={p.returncode} injected={injected} "
          f"log_lines={len(log.splitlines())} log={log.strip()!r}")
    return p.returncode, injected, log

def main():
    mode = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "current"
    hook = REAL_HOOK if mode == "current" else sys.argv[2]
    print(f"=== probing {hook} ===")
    run_case(hook, "malformed-stdin", b"not-json")
    run_case(hook, "empty-stdin", b"")
    run_case(hook, "startup", json.dumps({"source": "startup"}).encode())
    run_case(hook, "resume", json.dumps({"source": "resume"}).encode())
    run_case(hook, "no-source-key", b"{}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

patches.new_string:
# Hard cap to avoid token blow-up if the file grows unmaintained.
# Session-prime.md cap rules state 500-line hard limit; if file exceeds
# this size the hook truncates and flags. This protects the context
# window even if the maintenance discipline lapses.
MAX_INJECT_CHARS = 30000  # ~7-8K tokens hard ceiling
# On overflow keep this many chars of the file TAIL: session-prime.md is
# maintained append-newest-at-tail (newest codifications sit at the end),
# so a pure head-keep would delete the newest ledger items first — the
# items a cold session most needs. Middle is elided with an explicit
# marker instead (2026-07-21 audit fix).
TAIL_KEEP_CHARS = 4000  # ~1K tokens of newest tail entries
patches.rationale: Adds the tail-retention constant with the why-comment in house style. VERIFICATION CORRECTION to the finding's evidence: the live file is 30,200 BYTES (wc -c) but the hook caps on len() of the decoded text = 29,826 CHARS — so truncation is NOT firing live today; the file sits 174 chars under cap (99.4%) and the next codification append trips it. The mechanic itself (head-keep deletes the newest tail on overflow) is confirmed by sandboxed probe: a >30K fixture run through the unpatched hook loses its tail sentinel.

patches.old_string:
truncation_note = ""
    if len(content) > MAX_INJECT_CHARS:
        content = content[:MAX_INJECT_CHARS]
        truncation_note = (
            "\n\n=== TRUNCATED: session-prime.md exceeded MAX_INJECT_CHARS "
            f"({MAX_INJECT_CHARS}). Audit at monthly cycle and prune. ===\n"
        )
patches.new_string:
truncation_note = ""
    if len(content) > MAX_INJECT_CHARS:
        # Head + marked mid-file elision + newest tail (never a tail cut).
        # Output is exactly MAX_INJECT_CHARS chars; head and tail cannot
        # overlap because len(content) > MAX_INJECT_CHARS.
        elision_marker = (
            "\n\n=== ELIDED MID-FILE (cap overflow; newest tail retained) ===\n\n"
        )
        head_keep = MAX_INJECT_CHARS - TAIL_KEEP_CHARS - len(elision_marker)
        elided = len(content) - head_keep - TAIL_KEEP_CHARS
        content = (
            content[:head_keep] + elision_marker + content[-TAIL_KEEP_CHARS:]
        )
        truncation_note = (
            "\n\n=== TRUNCATED: session-prime.md exceeded MAX_INJECT_CHARS "
            f"({MAX_INJECT_CHARS}); {elided} chars elided mid-file, newest "
            "tail retained. Audit at monthly cycle and prune. ===\n"
        )
patches.rationale: Replaces the pure head-keep (content[:MAX_INJECT_CHARS], which silently deletes the newest codifications first — PC-20/21/22 sit at the tail) with head + explicit ELIDED MID-FILE marker + final TAIL_KEEP_CHARS. Deterministic arithmetic: output is exactly MAX_INJECT_CHARS chars (head_keep + marker + tail = cap); head/tail overlap impossible since len(content) > cap implies the elided middle is at least len(marker) > 0 chars. The truncation_note keeps its original wording and gains the elided-char count so the monthly-audit flag stays actionable. No behavior change while the file is under cap (byte-identical passthrough verified); resume/clear/compact skip path, fail-open paths, and log_fire untouched. Both old_strings verified byte-exact and unique (count==1) against the live file before probing.

# Hard cap to avoid token blow-up if the file grows unmaintained.
# Session-prime.md's cap rules are a JOINT budget — 500 lines / 30,000
# chars (session-prime.md "Cap rules") — and this hook enforces only the
# character half: Python len(), chars not lines and not bytes, same
# strict > 30000 boundary as pre-commit Function 3. The char cap is the
# one that binds in practice: dense lines had the live file at 29,826
# chars (99% of cap) at only 249/500 lines (2026-07-21 audit), so
# line-count headroom is NOT truncation headroom. Over-cap content is
# truncated and flagged. This protects the context window even if the
# maintenance discipline lapses.
MAX_INJECT_CHARS = 30000  # ~7-8K tokens hard ceiling
patches.rationale: Confirmed GAP (COSMETIC): the comment at lines 64-68 frames the cap against the '500-line hard limit' as if the hook enforced it ('if file exceeds this size the hook truncates'), but the hook never counts lines — the operative gate is MAX_INJECT_CHARS=30000 measured in Python len() characters (verified by sandboxed probe: 30,000 chars passes untouched, 30,001 truncates+flags; a 29,999-char/32,999-byte file passes, proving chars-not-bytes). Evidence refinement vs the finding as filed: the live session-prime.md is 29,826 CHARS / 30,200 BYTES / 249 lines — wc -c reported bytes, so the file is at 99.4% of the char cap (not over it; pre-commit Function 3, added 2026-07-17, blocks over-cap commits and its own comment records the identical bytes-vs-chars confusion caught 2026-07-17). The GAP is real and material to maintenance discipline: at current line density the char cap binds at ~half the line budget, and session-prime.md's own early-warning threshold (line 53, 'exceeds 450 lines') is keyed to lines, so a maintainer trusting line headroom (249/500) gets no warning before silent injection truncation. This comment block is the LAST stale line-framed reference in the harness — session-prime.md line 16 ('hard cap 500 lines / 30,000 chars (hook MAX_INJECT_CHARS)') and pre-commit Function 3 already state the char cap as operative. Fix is comment-only (no behavior change), matches house comment style (dated evidence snapshots, cf. pre-commit's 'file was 30,339 bytes / 29,977 chars'), and records the binding-cap asymmetry so future curation passes track chars, not lines. old_string is byte-exact from lines 64-68 and unique in the file. Companion note for the 07-24 review slot (out of this file's scope, not patched): session-prime.md's 450-line early-warning row would ideally gain a char-based early warning (~27,000 chars) so the warning fires before the binding cap does — that is a spec-file edit belonging to the curation-rule owner, not this hook.

def parse_todo_items(text: str) -> list[dict]:
    """
    Parse open to-do items from todo.md.

    Each item starts with `- [ ] **P0 / category / 2026-05-20** [TAGS] — Title`

        fd, tmp = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=".md")
        os.close(fd)
        p = Path(tmp)
        p.write_text(entry)
        s = parse_ledger_recent(p)
        p.unlink()
        dist = s["high"] + s["medium"] + s["low"] + s["fec"] + s["zero"]
        if s["fires"] != 1:
            failures.append(f"FAIL {name}: fires={s['fires']} (expected 1 — entry dropped)")
        elif s[bucket] != 1:
            failures.append(f"FAIL {name}: bucket {bucket}={s[bucket]} (expected 1)")
        elif dist != s["fires"]:
            failures.append(f"FAIL {name}: distribution sum {dist} != fires {s['fires']}")
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{len(LEDGER_SELFTEST)} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(LEDGER_SELFTEST)}/{len(LEDGER_SELFTEST)} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)

designs.defer_reason: DEFER to the 2026-07-24 review-grade slot — the finding is a verified PENDING migration, not an unowned defect, and patching ahead of the slot is net-negative. (1) Ownership: todo.md line 27 (P2/harness/2026-07-24 [INFRA, DUE]) explicitly owns fire-logging-as-HOUSE-STANDARD via a shared helper module (meta/hooks/hook_fire_log.py: house format, repr-escape, fail-open) plus migration of remaining hooks; its scope line ALSO bundles two other session-start-hook.py changes (Test-D prose-deadline extractor wiring + parser coverage tests), so this file is already slated for coordinated 07-24 work. A bespoke inline logger shipped 3 days early would be immediately reworked by the helper migration and risks double-logging when the migration mechanically adds helper calls. (2) Open design decisions assigned to that slot would be pre-empted: the K3 r2 fire-log rotation/cap policy (adding a new writer to an uncapped append-only log before the cap decision compounds the exact MAX_INJECT-class failure the item cites), and the format contract (structural-output-metric.py parses the log filtered by hook name — new row shapes are a helper-level format decision, not a per-hook improvisation). (3) Volume: house convention (hook-fire-log.md header) instruments hooks whose FIRE rates are audit-relevant; session-start-hook is informational, always-exit-0, firing every session start — per-fire rows would replicate the session-prime-hook resume-row noise already polluting the log with zero information. The only audit-relevant signal is the ERROR path (a silently broken briefing kills DUE/OVERDUE elevation invisibly, since SessionStart stderr is ephemeral in cloud containers — the fire-log's founding rationale). (4) No silent-slip risk: live-verified today that this hook's own DUE-tag mechanism surfaces the 07-24 item as 'DUE SOON (in 3 day(s))' in every session briefing — the deferral has a deterministic wake, satisfying the house's 'deferral dates without deterministic wakes are procrastination with a timestamp' bar. patches.old_string:
cost_match = re.search(r"\*\*Estimated token cost:\*\*\s*~?(\d+)(?:-(\d+))?k", body)
        if cost_match:
            low_k = int(cost_match.group(1))
            high_k = int(cost_match.group(2)) if cost_match.group(2) else low_k
            window_cost_total += ((low_k + high_k) // 2) * 1000
patches.new_string:
# Decimal-aware since 2026-07-21 audit: actual subagent_tokens entries
        # are decimal ("14.7k", "~51.2k" — the ledger-dominant shape since 07-02);
        # the old \d+ regex NO-MATCHed those lines, silently dropping the whole
        # entry from the window cost and understating the budget signal.
        cost_match = re.search(
            r"\*\*Estimated token cost:\*\*\s*~?(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)(?:-(\d+(?:\.\d+)?))?k", body
        )
        if cost_match:
            low_k = float(cost_match.group(1))
            high_k = float(cost_match.group(2)) if cost_match.group(2) else low_k
            window_cost_total += round((low_k + high_k) / 2 * 1000)
patches.rationale: The confirmed defect, with mechanism corrected vs. the finding text: decimal cost lines do not fraction-capture — the anchored literal plus the digits-immediately-before-k requirement makes the whole match FAIL, so the entry contributes ZERO tokens (probe: ledger lines 172 '14.7k' and 212 '~51.2k' both NO MATCH under the old regex). Decimal 'actual subagent_tokens' is the corpus-dominant shape since ~2026-07-02 (~60 of the last 70 cost lines), so any window containing them understates spend — the budget signal degrades exactly when real token counts replace round estimates. Fix: optional-fraction groups on both range bounds + float parse; round() replaces the //-floor (identical on every integer shape in the live corpus — all existing integer ranges have even sums — and exact on decimals). Today's live window is all-integer (27k/101k/161k), so the patched hook reproduces today's ~1238k warning byte-identically (verified end-to-end on a scratchpad copy, exit 0).

COST_SELFTEST = [

    ("integer-tilde", "**Estimated token cost:** ~27k (actual subagent_tokens)", 27000),
    ("decimal-bare", "**Estimated token cost:** 14.7k (actual subagent_tokens).", 14700),

]


def selftest():
    import tempfile
    failures = []
    entry_date = (date.today() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    for name, cost_line, want_window in COST_SELFTEST:
        text = (
            f"### [{entry_date} AM] selftest fixture\n"
            "**Material yield class:** HIGH\n"
            f"{cost_line}\n"
        )
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", suffix=".md", delete=False) as f:
            f.write(text)
            tmp = Path(f.name)
        got = parse_ledger_recent(tmp, window_days=7)["cost_30d_estimate"]
        tmp.unlink()
        want = int(want_window / 7 * 30)  # mirror the extrapolation exactly
        if got != want:
            failures.append(f"FAIL {name}: expected {want}, got {got}")
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{len(COST_SELFTEST)} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(COST_SELFTEST)}/{len(COST_SELFTEST)} fixtures pass")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Read-only probe: verify llm-native-priming-hook behavior on meta-discussion
prompts (pre-patch baseline, and identical post-patch expectation)."""
import importlib.util
import io
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys

HOOK = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/llm-native-priming-hook.py"

# --- Unit level: load module, exercise should_inject directly ---
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("priming", HOOK)
mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(mod)

cases = [
    # (prompt, expected_inject, label)
    ("Can you review the anti-fabrication hook and settings.json wiring for Principle #43b compliance today?",
     True, "meta-discussion >=50 chars (hook/settings.json/Principle-#) -> PRIMED"),
    ("Let's codify this as B66 in the biases-watchlist and update the .py hook accordingly, please.",
     True, "meta-discussion codify/.py -> PRIMED"),
    ("what is NVDA", False, "<50 chars -> skip"),
    ("/compact", False, "slash command -> skip"),
    ("ok", False, "one-word ack -> skip"),
    ("thanks!", False, "one-word ack -> skip"),
]
fails = 0
for prompt, want, label in cases:
    got = mod.should_inject(prompt)
    status = "PASS" if got == want else "FAIL"
    if got != want:
        fails += 1
    print(f"[{status}] should_inject={got} expected={want} :: {label}")

# --- Dead-code check: is META_DISCUSSION_PATTERNS still defined? ---
has_dead = hasattr(mod, "META_DISCUSSION_PATTERNS")
print(f"[INFO] META_DISCUSSION_PATTERNS defined: {has_dead}"
      f" ({'expected pre-patch' if has_dead else 'expected post-patch'})")

# --- End-to-end: feed stdin JSON, confirm injection emitted for meta prompt ---
env = dict(os.environ, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="/home/user/LLMNA")
payload = json.dumps({"prompt": "Can you review the anti-fabrication hook and settings.json wiring for Principle #43b compliance today?"})
r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, HOOK], input=payload, capture_output=True,
                   text=True, env=env, cwd="/home/user/LLMNA", timeout=30)
injected = "LLM-NATIVE DISCIPLINE PRIMING" in r.stdout
print(f"[{'PASS' if r.returncode == 0 and injected else 'FAIL'}] e2e meta prompt: exit={r.returncode} injected={injected}")
if not (r.returncode == 0 and injected):
    fails += 1

sys.exit(1 if fails else 0)

SELFTEST_FIXTURES = [
    # (prompt, expect_inject, expect_skip_reason)

]

def _selftest() -> int:
    failures = 0
    for prompt, expect_inject, expect_reason in SELFTEST_FIXTURES:
        got = should_inject(prompt)
        if got != expect_inject:
            print(f"FAIL: should_inject({prompt[:40]!r}...) = {got}, expected {expect_inject} (reason class: {expect_reason})")

    print(f"selftest: {len(SELFTEST_FIXTURES) - failures}/{len(SELFTEST_FIXTURES)} pass")

NOTE for the sweep owner: the identical naive-prefix in_scope() exists in ~15 sibling hooks (any(cwd.startswith(p)) variant in anti-fabrication, cascade-enforcement, llm-native-priming, llm-native-reasoning, macro-anchor, reasoning-tagging, session-prime, session-start, signal-ingest; bare os.getcwd().startswith(REPO_ROOT) in analyst-pt-context, antifragility-mn, borrowed-vs-firstprinciples, bottoms-up, bypass-route, nth-order-cascade; REPO_GUARD in segment-trajectory) and README.md line 45 documents the convention as 'prefix match on the dynamic root' — the sweep should replicate this exact patch shape per hook and reword README line 45 to 'boundary-aware prefix match (root itself or root + separator)'. Probe already written and run at /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/scope_probe.py; exact code:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Sandboxed probe for structural-output-hook.py in_scope() sibling-dir leak.

Builds a fake repo root <scratch>/LLMNA plus a sibling <scratch>/LLMNA-backup,
sets CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR to the fake root (so the hook's _REPO_ROOT, scope
guard, and fire-log append all resolve inside the sandbox — the real repo is
never touched), then runs the hook end-to-end from four cwds with a transcript
that MUST fire (>800 chars, analytical marker 'thesis', zero structural
markers, zero exemption tokens).

Expected exit codes:
  pre-patch : root=2  subdir=2  sibling=2 (DEFECT: sibling treated in-scope)  outside=0
  post-patch: root=2  subdir=2  sibling=0 (fixed)                             outside=0
"""
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys

SCRATCH = "/tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad"
ROOT = os.path.join(SCRATCH, "sandbox", "LLMNA")          # fake repo root
SIBLING = os.path.join(SCRATCH, "sandbox", "LLMNA-backup")  # sibling dir sharing the prefix
SUBDIR = os.path.join(ROOT, "research")

REAL_HOOK = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/structural-output-hook.py"
PATCHED_HOOK = os.path.join(SCRATCH, "structural-output-hook.patched.py")

# Exact-string patch under test (must match the repo file byte-exact, once).
OLD = """def in_scope() -> bool:
    cwd = os.getcwd()
    return any(cwd.startswith(p) for p in ENFORCEMENT_PATHS)
"""
NEW = """def in_scope() -> bool:
    cwd = os.getcwd()
    # Boundary-aware match (fixed 2026-07-21): cwd must equal the root or live
    # under it (root + os.sep); plain startswith treated a sibling dir like
    # <root>-backup as in-scope.
    return any(cwd == p or cwd.startswith(p + os.sep) for p in ENFORCEMENT_PATHS)
"""


def build_sandbox():
    os.makedirs(os.path.join(ROOT, "research", "meta"), exist_ok=True)
    os.makedirs(SIBLING, exist_ok=True)
    # Transcript: fires the structural-markers-missing path deterministically.
    text = "The NVDA thesis remains intact after the quarter. " * 30  # 1500 chars
    transcript = os.path.join(SCRATCH, "sandbox", "transcript.jsonl")
    with open(transcript, "w") as f:
        f.write(json.dumps({"message": {"role": "assistant",
                                        "content": [{"type": "text", "text": text}]}}) + "\n")
    return transcript


def make_patched_copy():
    with open(REAL_HOOK) as f:
        src = f.read()
    n = src.count(OLD)
    print(f"old_string occurrences in repo file: {n} (must be exactly 1)")
    assert n == 1, "patch old_string is not unique/byte-exact"
    with open(PATCHED_HOOK, "w") as f:
        f.write(src.replace(OLD, NEW))


def run_hook(hook_path, cwd, transcript):
    env = dict(os.environ, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=ROOT)
    payload = json.dumps({"transcript_path": transcript, "stop_hook_active": False})
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, hook_path], input=payload, cwd=cwd,
                       env=env, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
    return p.returncode


def main():
    transcript = build_sandbox()
    make_patched_copy()
    cases = [("repo root  ", ROOT), ("repo subdir", SUBDIR),
             ("sibling dir", SIBLING), ("outside    ", "/tmp")]
    for label, hook in [("PRE-PATCH ", REAL_HOOK), ("POST-PATCH", PATCHED_HOOK)]:
        print(f"\n{label} ({hook})")
        for name, cwd in cases:
            rc = run_hook(hook, cwd, transcript)
            print(f"  cwd={name} -> exit {rc}")
    print("\nexpected pre : 2 / 2 / 2(defect) / 0")
    print("expected post: 2 / 2 / 0        / 0")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

                        "content": [{"type": "text", "text": text}],
                    }

                cwd=sandbox,
                env=env,
                timeout=10,
            )
            return r.returncode

patches.old_string: POSITION_IMPLICATION_RE = re.compile(r"^.*Position implication:.*$", re.MULTILINE)
patches.new_string:
# Case-insensitive (added 2026-07-21 audit): a lowercase "position
# implication:" line previously escaped this check entirely — while still
# counting as an analytical marker via has_pattern IGNORECASE — so an
# untiered lowercase sizing recommendation faced only the general
# structural gate. Rule #11's canonical capitalization stays socially
# enforced; tier presence is enforced deterministically in either case.
POSITION_IMPLICATION_RE = re.compile(r"^.*Position implication:.*$", re.MULTILINE | re.IGNORECASE)
patches.rationale: Closes the confirmed GAP: the Principle #37 tier check compiled case-sensitively while ANALYTICAL_MARKERS credits r"\bposition\s+implication" case-insensitively via has_pattern (line 223, re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE) — an asymmetry where a lowercase 'position implication: TRIM to 10%...' line counts as analytical content but never faces the tier requirement, verified by probe (unpatched hook exits 0 on a >=800-char lowercase-implication message with a structural marker; capitalized variant exits 2). Adding re.IGNORECASE is the minimal fix: FP risk is marginal because the match still requires the literal colon-suffixed phrase (prose mentions of 'position implication' without a colon never match), and the check's downstream logic (tier on same line or line directly above, _log_fire('position-implication-tier-missing'), exit 2 feedback) is unchanged. Canonical capitalization of the Rule #11 template remains socially enforced, per the finding's proposed_upgrade. old_string uniqueness verified programmatically (src.count == 1). Comment block matches the file's dated-annotation style (cf. '(moved 2026-06-27)', '(added 2026-06-27...)' comments).

r"\bharness[- ](meta|design)\b",
    r"\b(harness-meta|scan-design)\s+exemption\b",
]

# Lead-in exemptions (split out of EXEMPTION_PATTERNS 2026-07-21): docstring
# promises "PURE file-write narration" / acknowledgment lead-ins, but
# has_pattern()'s re.MULTILINE turned these ^ anchors into any-line anchors —
# one incidental mid-message "Committed ..." line exempted an entire
# analytical response. Checked via has_lead_pattern() WITHOUT re.MULTILINE,
# so ^ means start-of-message only. Token exemptions above stay message-wide
# (accepted 2026-06-27 Option B looseness).
EXEMPTION_LEAD_PATTERNS = [
    r"^(?:Updated|Saved|Created|Wrote|Pushed|Committed)\s+",  # file-write narration
    r"^(?:Yes|No|Confirmed|Acknowledged)\s*[—-]",             # acknowledgment lead-in
]
patches.rationale: Reinstates the two patterns byte-identical in a dedicated EXEMPTION_LEAD_PATTERNS list with a dated comment in house style (matches the 2026-06-27 dated-comment convention in this file). Keeping the regexes unchanged means the ONLY behavior delta is the anchor semantics — no new pattern surface, no new FP class beyond the intended tightening.

patches.new_string:
# Exemption: meta-discussion / file-narration / acknowledgment / scan-design.
    # Narration/acknowledgment exemptions require the pattern to OPEN the
    # message (fixed 2026-07-21 — MULTILINE previously let one mid-message
    # "Committed ..." line exempt a full analytical response).
    if has_pattern(text, EXEMPTION_PATTERNS):
        sys.exit(0)
    if has_lead_pattern(text, EXEMPTION_LEAD_PATTERNS):
        sys.exit(0)

Ordering vs the Position-implication tier check (which runs BEFORE all exemptions per the 2026-06-27 move) is untouched — probe S12 (narration lead + untier'd 'Position implication:' line) exits 2 both pre- and post-patch. patches.new_string:
# Analytical content markers (must have >=1 for hook to consider firing).
# Ticker-shaped patterns live in the CASED list below — has_pattern applies
# IGNORECASE, under which the uppercase classes matched any short filename
# ("facts.md", "tags.md", "INDEX.md"), counting routine file-talk as

ANALYTICAL_MARKERS_CASED = [
    r"\b[A-Z]{2,5}\.[A-Z]{1,3}\b",      # non-US ticker (e.g., ASML.AS)
    r"\b[A-Z]{2,5}\b\s+thesis",          # TICKER thesis

patches.rationale: Moves the two ticker-shaped patterns (whose [A-Z] classes encode intent: uppercase exchange-suffixed tickers per the line-69 comment) out of the IGNORECASE-searched list into a new ANALYTICAL_MARKERS_CASED list. Under IGNORECASE the ticker regex matched any 2-5-letter filename with a 1-3-letter extension — probe-confirmed on facts.md/tags.md/todo.md/INDEX.md — so routine file-talk counted as analytical content and could false-fire the hook (probe Case A: exit 2 + spurious structural-markers-missing fire-log line, which pollutes the 08-06 priming-experiment fire counts and the <5/month inert rule). Also fixes the comment example: 5201.T is digit-prefixed and NEVER matched this regex (numeric JP tickers are the 4-digit-JP hook's job per research/CLAUDE.md); ASML.AS is an example the regex actually matches. Moving the TICKER-thesis pattern too loses nothing: any 'X Thesis' text is still caught by the case-insensitive \bthesis\b marker (probe Case C unchanged).

Adds a flags parameter defaulting to the existing re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE so every current call site (exemptions, analytical, structural) behaves byte-identically; only the new CASED call passes different flags. Smallest possible mechanism for a case-sensitive search path — no second function, no behavior change anywhere else.

patches.new_string:
# Trigger gate: must contain analytical markers
    # (ticker-shaped patterns are case-sensitive — see ANALYTICAL_MARKERS_CASED)
    if not (has_pattern(text, ANALYTICAL_MARKERS)
            or has_pattern(text, ANALYTICAL_MARKERS_CASED, flags=re.MULTILINE)):
        sys.exit(0)
patches.rationale: Trigger gate now ORs the unchanged case-insensitive list with the ticker-shaped list searched WITHOUT IGNORECASE (MULTILINE kept — harmless for these patterns, consistent with house search flags). Net effect is strictly narrowing: real uppercase tickers (ASML.AS) still trip the gate (probe Case B: exit 2 before and after); lowercase filename-talk no longer does (probe Case A: exit 2 -> exit 0). Structural-marker pass path and recursion guard unchanged (probe Cases D + stop_hook_active: exit 0 before and after).

patches.rationale: Removes the dead-weight ANALYTICAL_MARKERS entry (line 79). Because exemptions run before the analytical gate and the structural pass runs immediately after it (main(), lines 299-308), any text matching analytical \bcascade\b necessarily also matches structural \bcascade\b (line 105) and exits 0 — the entry provably can never contribute to an exit-2 fire. Deliberately NOT changed: the structural \bcascade\b entry stays — bare cascade/knock-on/ripple as N-th-order markers mirrors nth-order-cascade-hook.py's marker set, and tightening it (e.g. requiring adjacency to order/P-weight tokens) would be a behavioral change with FP risk on legitimate TRACE outputs; if the 'bare cascade immunizes' residual is judged worth killing, that is a separate behavioral finding for the 07-24 review-grade slot.

patches.old_string: r"\bH1\b.*\bH2\b.*\bH3\b",          # H1...H2...H3 (parallel hypothesis pattern)
patches.new_string:
# ((?s) so the enumeration may span lines — the natural format, and the format
    #  the hook's own feedback item (a) requests; single-line-only miss fixed
    #  2026-07-21 audit)
    r"(?s)\bH1\b.*\bH2\b.*\bH3\b",      # H1...H2...H3 (parallel hypothesis pattern)
patches.rationale: Confirmed DEFECT (severity LOW): has_pattern() compiles with re.IGNORECASE|re.MULTILINE only, so '.' never crosses newlines and the H1/H2/H3 marker credits ONLY same-line enumerations. A multi-line enumeration — the natural format, and the exact format the hook's own feedback item (a) requests on a block — is not credited (probe: current hook exits 2 on an 862-char analytical payload whose only structural content is 'H1 (P=40%)\nH2 (P=35%)\nH3 (P=25%)'; the re-block after compliance is masked in practice only by the stop_hook_active recursion guard, i.e. compliance is never actually re-checked). Fix is the scoped inline flag (?s) on THIS pattern alone — adding re.DOTALL globally in has_pattern() would silently loosen every other marker (e.g. the 'base rate ... signal adjustment' pattern) and every ANALYTICAL/EXEMPTION pattern sharing the helper. (?s) must be pattern-leading per Python 3.11+ inline-global-flag rules. FP loosening is bounded: all three tokens must still appear and \bH3\b has no half-year homonym (probe confirms 'H1 2026 ... H2 2026' financial half-year text still does not match). Verified in sandbox: patched copy exits 0 on the multi-line payload, still exits 2 with the structural-markers feedback when the H3 line is removed, still honors stop_hook_active. Comment style/dating matches the file's 2026-06-27 fix annotations; alignment preserves the trailing-comment column.

patches.new_string:
def _selftest() -> None:
    """
    Regex-level fixtures for the STRUCTURAL_MARKERS pass-gate. Added
    2026-07-21 with the (?s) fix to the H1/H2/H3 marker: a multi-line
    enumeration (the natural format, and the one feedback item (a)
    requests) must be credited as a structural pass.
    """
    fixtures = [
        # (name, text, expected has_pattern(text, STRUCTURAL_MARKERS))
        ("h123-same-line", "H1 (P=40%) vs H2 (P=35%) vs H3 (P=25%)", True),
        ("h123-multi-line", "H1 (P=40%)\nH2 (P=35%)\nH3 (P=25%)", True),
        ("h123-blank-line-gaps", "H1: supply-gated.\n\nH2: plateau.\n\nH3: compression.", True),
        ("h12-half-years-no-h3", "H1 2026 was strong and H2 2026 guidance was raised.", False),
        ("no-markers-linear-prose", "The thesis rests on demand staying firm into 2027.", False),
    ]
    failed = 0
    for name, text, expected in fixtures:
        got = has_pattern(text, STRUCTURAL_MARKERS)
        if got != expected:
            failed += 1
            print(f"FAIL {name}: expected {expected}, got {got}", file=sys.stderr)
    if failed:
        print(f"selftest: {failed}/{len(fixtures)} fixtures FAILED", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest: {len(fixtures)}/{len(fixtures)} fixtures passed")
    sys.exit(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        _selftest()
    main()
patches.rationale: House standard: every behavior change ships with a fixture; this hook had NO embedded --selftest (grep confirms), so the regex fix would otherwise be regression-unguarded. Minimal scaffold matching house precedent (session-prime-cascade-hook v2 embedded --selftest): 5 fixtures targeting exactly the changed marker — two would FAIL without patch 1 (probe-verified: selftest exits 1 flagging h123-multi-line when the regex fix is reverted, so the fixtures are load-bearing, not decorative) and two negatives guard against over-loosening (H1/H2-only half-year notation; plain linear prose must still not be credited). Normal hook invocation path is untouched: --selftest only triggers via explicit argv flag, and _selftest() reads no stdin and exits before main(); the live Stop-hook wiring passes no argv, so runtime behavior is byte-identical. Exercises has_pattern() with the real production flags rather than re-deriving them.

Exit 0 outside', but raw startswith treats any sibling directory whose name extends the repo root (/home/user/LLMNA-backup, /home/user/LLMNAx) as in-scope. Probe-confirmed end-to-end: with cwd in <root>-sibling the hook exits 2 (blocks Stop outside the repo) AND log_fire appends to the real root's research/meta/hook-fire-log.md from outside, polluting the fire metrics that feed the hook's own retirement/FP falsifiers (docstring lines 66-69). Fix compares at an os.sep boundary (exact root, or root + separator prefix), with rstrip(os.sep) so a trailing slash in CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR cannot produce a double-separator prefix that would silently disable enforcement. #!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Scope-boundary fixture for macro-anchor-hook.py (sibling-dir collision, 2026-07-21)."""
import json, os, subprocess, sys, tempfile
HOOK = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/macro-anchor-hook.py"
S = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="macro-anchor-fixture-")
fake_root = os.path.join(S, "LLMNA"); sibling = os.path.join(S, "LLMNA-sibling")
os.makedirs(os.path.join(fake_root, "research", "meta")); os.makedirs(sibling)
body = ("NVDA bull case remains intact. " * 40) + " Position implication: HOLD."
t = os.path.join(S, "t.jsonl")
open(t, "w").write(json.dumps({"type": "assistant", "message": {"role": "assistant",
    "content": [{"type": "text", "text": body}]}}) + "\n")
env = dict(os.environ, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=fake_root)
payload = json.dumps({"transcript_path": t, "stop_hook_active": False})
FIXTURES = [
    (sibling, "sibling dir must NOT enforce", 0),      # the patched behavior; pre-patch exits 2
    (fake_root, "repo root must still enforce", 2),
    (S, "unrelated dir must NOT enforce", 0),
]
ok = True
for cwd, label, want in FIXTURES:
    r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, HOOK], input=payload, cwd=cwd, env=env,
                       capture_output=True, text=True)
    good = r.returncode == want; ok &= good
    print(f"{label}: exit={r.returncode} want={want} {'PASS' if good else 'FAIL'}")
# house regressions: empty payload + recursion guard fail-open
for pl in ("{}", '{"stop_hook_active": true}'):
    r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, HOOK], input=pl, cwd=fake_root, env=env,
                       capture_output=True, text=True)
    ok &= r.returncode == 0
    print(f"regression {pl}: exit={r.returncode} want=0")
sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)

(Fire-log appends go to the fake root under the tempdir, never the real repo. The unrelated-dir case exercises fail-open OUTSIDE scope; the repo-root case proves enforcement is unchanged.)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Selftest — deterministic end-to-end fixtures (added 2026-07-21 audit, per
# session-prime-cascade-hook.py precedent). Each case runs main() against a
# temp fake transcript with cwd + fire log redirected to a temp dir, so no
# live transcript is needed and the real hook-fire-log.md is never touched.
# Covers: recursion guard, empty/malformed stdin, size gate, tier check
# (fire / same-line / line-above / ordering-beats-exemption, 2026-06-27),
# exemption gate, analytical gate, structural fire + both 2026-06-27 table
# detectors, last-assistant-message-wins, fire-log write, pass-paths-unlogged.
# Run: python3 structural-output-hook.py --selftest
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# >800-char linear analytical prose: analytical markers (thesis/scenario)
# present, ZERO structural markers, ZERO exemption tokens. The revert-pattern
# the hook exists to catch.
_BASE_LINEAR = (
    "The thesis for this name is that datacenter capex stays strong into "
    "next year. Management guided above expectations and the supply picture "
    "remains tight across the board. My read is that pricing holds through "
    "the second half, margins stay elevated, and the market continues to "
    "underestimate the duration of the build-out. The bear scenario would "
    "require a visible slowdown in bookings, which no supplier has reported "
    "so far. Checks with distributors point the same direction, and channel "
    "inventory remains lean by historical standards. Overall the setup "
    "remains favorable and the risk-reward is acceptable at current levels, "
    "with the caveat that the position is already sized appropriately given "
    "the run it has had since the spring. Nothing in this quarter changes "
    "the medium-term picture materially, and the follow-through from "
    "customer commitments supports the standing view into next fiscal year."
)

# >800-char plain prose: no analytical markers at all — must never fire.
_PLAIN_PROSE = (
    "The plan for the reading room is straightforward. We move the "
    "reference shelving to the north wall, replace the worn carpet tiles "
    "in the entry, and add two study tables near the windows. Volunteers "
    "arrive at nine and the building opens at noon, which leaves a "
    "comfortable window for the heavy lifting. Boxes should be labeled by "
    "aisle and shelf so returns go back in a single pass. The catalog "
    "terminals stay online the whole time because the network cabinet is "
    "untouched. Lunch is provided for everyone who signs up before Friday, "
    "and the coordinator will send a reminder the evening before. If the "
    "weather turns, the outdoor book sale moves under the awning and the "
    "signage crew adjusts the arrows accordingly. By closing time the goal "
    "is simple: every shelf back where it belongs, every cart empty, and "
    "the front desk ready for a normal Saturday opening without surprises."
)


def _transcript_payload(td, texts, name):
    """Write a fake transcript JSONL (user line + junk lines + one assistant
    entry per text, exercising the role-skip / blank / bad-JSON branches of
    load_last_assistant_message) and return the stdin payload for it."""
    path = os.path.join(td, name)
    with open(path, "w") as fh:
        fh.write(json.dumps({"message": {"role": "user", "content": [
            {"type": "text", "text": "ignore me"}]}}) + "\n")
        fh.write("\n")            # blank-line branch
        fh.write("not json\n")    # malformed-line branch
        for text in texts:
            fh.write(json.dumps({"message": {"role": "assistant", "content": [
                {"type": "text", "text": text}]}}) + "\n")
    return json.dumps({"transcript_path": path, "stop_hook_active": False})


def _run_case(payload):
    """Feed a raw stdin payload through main(); return (exit_code, stderr)."""
    import io
    real_stdin, real_stderr = sys.stdin, sys.stderr
    sys.stdin, sys.stderr = io.StringIO(payload), io.StringIO()
    code, err = 0, ""
    try:
        main()
    except SystemExit as e:
        code = e.code if isinstance(e.code, int) else 0
    finally:
        err = sys.stderr.getvalue()
        sys.stdin, sys.stderr = real_stdin, real_stderr
    return code, err


def selftest():
    import tempfile
    failures = []
    n_checks = 0  # computed, never hard-coded (anti-fabrication-hook K3-r2 rule)
    real_cwd, real_root = os.getcwd(), ENFORCEMENT_PATHS[0]
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
        os.makedirs(os.path.join(td, "research", "meta"))
        ENFORCEMENT_PATHS[0] = td  # scope passes; fire log lands in temp dir
        os.chdir(td)
        try:
            cases = [
                # (name, stdin payload, expected exit, required stderr substring)
                ("recursion guard exits 0",
                 '{"stop_hook_active": true}', 0, None),
                ("empty stdin exits 0", "", 0, None),
                ("no transcript_path exits 0", "{}", 0, None),
                ("missing transcript file exits 0",
                 json.dumps({"transcript_path": os.path.join(td, "nope.jsonl")}),
                 0, None),
                ("size gate: short analytical text passes",
                 _transcript_payload(td, ["Short thesis note, nothing more."],
                                     "t_short.jsonl"), 0, None),
                # KNOWN-OPEN G-27: the 800-char size gate runs BEFORE the tier
                # check, so a SHORT untiered Position implication passes. This
                # fixture pins CURRENT behavior — flip expected exit to 2 in
                # the same commit that fixes G-27.
                ("G-27 pin: short untiered Position implication passes",
                 _transcript_payload(
                     td, ["Position implication: HOLD — no size change — ok."],
                     "t_g27.jsonl"), 0, None),
                # Tier check fires even with exemption tokens present — the
                # 2026-06-27 ordering move (tier check BEFORE exemption gate).
                ("tier-missing fires despite exemption tokens",
                 _transcript_payload(
                     td, [_BASE_LINEAR
                          + "\n\nThe hook context: this also touches "
                            "settings.json wiring.\n\nPosition implication: "
                            "HOLD — no size change — quarter in line."],
                     "t_tier_fire.jsonl"), 2, "Principle #37"),
                ("tier marker on same line passes",
                 _transcript_payload(
                     td, [_BASE_LINEAR + "\n\n1st order read: demand holds."
                          "\n\nPosition implication: HOLD 🟢 — no size change "
                          "— backlog receipt."],
                     "t_tier_same.jsonl"), 0, None),
                ("tier marker on line directly above passes",
                 _transcript_payload(
                     td, [_BASE_LINEAR + "\n\n1st order read: demand holds."
                          "\n\n🟡 DIRECTIONAL (my model)\nPosition "
                          "implication: HOLD — no size change — directional."],
                     "t_tier_above.jsonl"), 0, None),
                ("exemption token suppresses structural fire",
                 _transcript_payload(
                     td, [_BASE_LINEAR + "\n\nThis turn also touches "
                          "settings.json wiring."],
                     "t_exempt.jsonl"), 0, None),
                ("analytical gate: plain prose never fires",
                 _transcript_payload(td, [_PLAIN_PROSE], "t_plain.jsonl"),
                 0, None),
                ("structural fire: linear analytical prose blocks",
                 _transcript_payload(td, [_BASE_LINEAR], "t_fire.jsonl"),
                 2, "STRUCTURAL-OUTPUT HOOK"),
                ("structural marker (1st order) passes",
                 _transcript_payload(
                     td, [_BASE_LINEAR + "\n\n1st order read: demand holds."],
                     "t_marker.jsonl"), 0, None),
                ("separator-row table detector passes (2026-06-27)",
                 _transcript_payload(
                     td, [_BASE_LINEAR + "\n\n| Name | Read |\n|---|---|\n"
                          "| A | steady |"],
                     "t_sep.jsonl"), 0, None),
                ("hyphenated 3-col header detector passes (2026-06-27 fix)",
                 _transcript_payload(
                     td, [_BASE_LINEAR + "\n\n| Held-name effect | Brain-dump "
                          "component | AI-flation read |"],
                     "t_hyph.jsonl"), 0, None),
                ("last assistant message wins (earlier fire-worthy msg moot)",
                 _transcript_payload(td, [_BASE_LINEAR, "Done."],
                                     "t_last.jsonl"), 0, None),
            ]
            n_checks = len(cases) + 2  # + the two fire-log assertions below
            for name, payload, want_exit, want_err in cases:
                code, err = _run_case(payload)
                if code != want_exit:
                    failures.append(
                        f"FAIL {name}: expected exit {want_exit}, got {code}")
                elif want_err and want_err not in err:
                    failures.append(f"FAIL {name}: stderr missing {want_err!r}")
            # Fire-log assertions: exactly the two fire cases logged, one per
            # reason; pass paths stay unlogged (house norm).
            log_path = Path(td) / "research/meta/hook-fire-log.md"
            log = log_path.read_text() if log_path.exists() else ""
            fire_lines = [l for l in log.splitlines() if l.strip()]
            if len(fire_lines) != 2:
                failures.append(
                    f"FAIL fire-log: expected 2 lines, got {len(fire_lines)}")
            for reason in ("position-implication-tier-missing",
                           "structural-markers-missing"):
                if reason not in log:
                    failures.append(f"FAIL fire-log: missing reason {reason}")
        finally:
            ENFORCEMENT_PATHS[0] = real_root
            os.chdir(real_cwd)
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{n_checks} selftest checks FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {n_checks}/{n_checks} checks pass")
    sys.exit(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        selftest()
    main()
patches.rationale: Embed the house-standard --selftest (GAP fix per confirmed finding). Purely additive: main() and all enforcement logic untouched; anchor 'if __name__ == "__main__":\n    main()' is unique in the file (verified). Design follows both precedents: fixture-table + 'selftest OK — N/N' output from session-prime-cascade-hook.py; temp-dir monkeypatch of the fire-log target + computed (never hard-coded) check count from anti-fabrication-hook.py K3-r2. Cases run main() end-to-end in-process against fake transcripts, so the suite covers the real pipeline: stdin parsing, recursion guard, transcript loading (role-skip/blank/bad-JSON/last-message-wins), 800-char size gate, Position-implication tier check including the 2026-06-27 ordering (tier fires DESPITE exemption tokens), exemption gate, analytical gate, structural pass via marker + both 2026-06-27 table detectors (separator-row and hyphenated-3-col-header, each isolated from the other), fire-log writes with per-reason strings, and pass-paths-unlogged. Known-open G-27 is pinned as current behavior with a flip-on-fix comment, not re-reported. Mutation-tested: regressing the tier-check ordering fails 3 checks; deleting the separator-row detector fails 2 checks. Parity-tested: original vs patched produce identical exit codes and byte-identical stderr on fire/pass payloads.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Probe for macro-anchor-hook.py exemption-collision finding (L\\d / methodology).
Expected PRE-patch : A=0 B=0 C=0 D=0 E=2 F=0   (A/B = the silent-skip defect)
Expected POST-patch: A=2 B=2 C=0 D=0 E=2 F=0
"""
import json, os, subprocess, sys, tempfile
from pathlib import Path

HOOK = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \
    "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/macro-anchor-hook.py"

PAD = ("Supply detail continues below with additional context on packaging, "
       "power delivery, and interconnect budgets across the rack. ") * 6

CASES = {
    "A_l2_cache_collision": (
        "NVDA bear case, cache-hierarchy detail. The successor part leans "
        "harder on SRAM and the L2 cache per die grows materially, which "
        "compresses HBM bandwidth pressure at the margin. That weakens the "
        "memory-attach leg of the thesis if it repeats next generation. " + PAD),
    "B_methodology_collision": (
        "NVDA bear case around index flows. The provider changed its index "
        "inclusion methodology and the resulting weight cap trims passive "
        "demand at the margin, which weakens the flow-support leg of the "
        "thesis into the rebalance period. " + PAD),
    "C_lesson_tag_exempt": (
        "NVDA bear case follow-up on process notes. Cross-check against "
        "lessons.md L25 before grading; L17/L18 tombstoned entries stay "
        "excluded from the count. The thesis text itself is unchanged "
        "here. " + PAD),
    "D_methodology_md_exempt": (
        "NVDA bear case follow-up on process notes. See meta/methodology.md "
        "section on segment trajectory for the treatment; the thesis text "
        "itself is unchanged here. " + PAD),
    "E_baseline_fire": (
        "NVDA bear case on supply digestion. Order momentum slows into the "
        "second half and the thesis weakens at the margin without any "
        "offsetting datapoint. " + PAD),
    "F_anchor_pass": (
        "NVDA bear case on supply digestion. On a first-principles read as "
        "of 2026-07-21 the order momentum slows into the second half and "
        "the thesis weakens at the margin. " + PAD),
}

def run_case(text):
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
        root = Path(td) / "fakerepo"
        (root / "research/meta").mkdir(parents=True)
        tp = root / "transcript.jsonl"
        tp.write_text(json.dumps({"message": {"role": "assistant",
                      "content": [{"type": "text", "text": text}]}}) + "\n")
        payload = json.dumps({"transcript_path": str(tp)})
        env = dict(os.environ, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=str(root))
        p = subprocess.run(["python3", HOOK], input=payload, text=True,
                           capture_output=True, env=env, cwd=str(root))
        return p.returncode

print(f"hook under test: {HOOK}")
for name, text in CASES.items():
    print(f"  {name}: exit {run_case(text)}")

patches.old_string:
# Need a TICKER mention to be position-specific
TICKER_PATTERN = r"\b[A-Z]{2,5}(?:\.[A-Z]{1,3})?\b"
patches.new_string:
# Need a TICKER mention to be position-specific. The bare uppercase-run
# pattern alone was decorative (2026-07-21 audit): "AI" and "TL"/"DR"
# (from the house-mandated TL;DR opener) appear in virtually every
# analytical output, so the gate matched everything and position-
# relevance rested entirely on POSITION_RELEVANT_MARKERS. A match now
# counts as a ticker only if it is NOT in the stop-list of house-
# frequent non-ticker acronyms. Maintenance rule: the stop-list must
# NEVER contain a universe ticker (ARM, MU, NOW, META, ...) — the
# selftest collision guard enforces this. Accepted tradeoff: "AI"
# (C3.ai) is suppressed as a ticker; it is not in the covered universe
# and is overwhelmingly the sector term in this OS.
TICKER_PATTERN = r"\b[A-Z]{2,5}(?:\.[A-Z]{1,3})?\b"
NON_TICKER_STOPLIST = {
    # House-universal (TL;DR opener + sector name)
    "AI", "TL", "DR",
    # Roles / orgs / places
    "CEO", "CFO", "CTO", "USA", "US", "EU", "UK", "WSJ", "OK",
    # Sector vocabulary (tech acronyms, units)
    "GPU", "CPU", "TPU", "ASIC", "HBM", "LLM", "API", "AGI", "OEM",
    "PDF", "URL", "GW", "MW",
    # Finance acronyms / currencies
    "USD", "EUR", "JPY", "KRW", "ETF", "IPO", "CAGR", "BOM", "SOTP",
    "TAM", "ROI", "ASP", "EPS", "GAAP", "YOY", "QOQ", "PT", "FY",
    "ADR", "ISIN",
}


def ticker_mentioned(text: str) -> bool:
    for m in re.finditer(TICKER_PATTERN, text):
        if m.group(0) not in NON_TICKER_STOPLIST:
            return True
    return False
patches.rationale: Restores the documented 'Need a TICKER mention to be position-specific' semantics (comment line 106; docstring trigger line 48). Verified pre-patch: a 912-char position-relevant text whose only uppercase runs are AI/TL/DR/CEO/GPU/HBM blocks with exit 2 — the gate did zero work because 'AI' and 'TL;DR' appear in essentially every house output (fire-log: ~135 fires in 5.5 weeks). A finditer + stop-list filter is the minimal deterministic tighten; this is the hook's own pre-registered recalibration path (docstring line 68: 'If false-positive rate >30% -> tighten'). Stop-list checked against the CLAUDE.md universe table (NVDA..CRWD) — zero collisions, permanently enforced by the selftest guard in patch 3. Case sensitivity preserved (no flags). Residual behavior: SK/TSMC/NBIS still count as tickers; >=6-letter runs (IBIDEN, NASDAQ) never matched even pre-patch (pre-existing \b behavior, unchanged).

patches.new_string:

def selftest() -> int:
    """Regression fixtures for the ticker gate (2026-07-21 audit: the
    bare uppercase-run pattern matched "AI"/"TL;DR" in every analytical
    output, so the gate was decorative). Run:
    python3 macro-anchor-hook.py --selftest. Pure text fixtures — no
    transcript read, no fire-log writes."""
    failures = []
    ran = {"n": 0}

    def check(name, cond):
        ran["n"] += 1
        if not cond:
            failures.append(name)

    # 1-3 Stop-listed house acronyms alone are NOT a ticker mention.
    check("TL;DR alone is not a ticker", not ticker_mentioned("TL;DR: the read"))
    check("AI alone is not a ticker", not ticker_mentioned("the AI capex cycle"))
    check("CEO/GPU/HBM alone are not tickers",
          not ticker_mentioned("the CEO said GPU and HBM supply is tight"))
    # 4-6 Real tickers count, including beside stop-listed tokens.
    check("NVDA is a ticker", ticker_mentioned("NVDA guided above consensus"))
    check("ticker beside stop-listed tokens still counts",
          ticker_mentioned("TL;DR: AI capex ties out; MRVL is the beneficiary"))
    check("dotted ticker counts", ticker_mentioned("ASML.AS printed bookings"))
    # 7 Collision guard: stop-list must never contain a universe ticker.
    universe_overlap = NON_TICKER_STOPLIST & {
        "NVDA", "AMD", "AVGO", "MRVL", "ARM", "TSM", "ASML", "AMAT",
        "LRCX", "MU", "MSFT", "GOOG", "META", "AMZN", "ORCL", "ANET",
        "CIEN", "CSCO", "VST", "CEG", "GEV", "TLN", "NEE", "ETN",
        "SMCI", "DELL", "HPE", "PLTR", "SNOW", "MDB", "DDOG", "NOW",
        "CRWD",
    }
    check("stop-list collides with universe: %s" % sorted(universe_overlap),
          not universe_overlap)
    # 8 Case sensitivity preserved: lowercase never matches.
    check("lowercase text has no ticker", not ticker_mentioned("nvda in lowercase"))

    if failures:
        print("SELFTEST FAIL:", file=sys.stderr)
        for f in failures:
            print("  - %s" % f, file=sys.stderr)
        return 1
    print("SELFTEST PASS (%d checks)" % ran["n"])
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) > 1 and sys.argv[1] == "--selftest":
        sys.exit(selftest())
    main()

# Verdict tokens are the Rule #11 UPPERCASE convention — scope this one
    # pattern case-sensitive ((?-i:) overrides has_pattern's IGNORECASE) so
    # ordinary prose ("add to the pile", "enter the market", "hold at
    # current levels") cannot qualify a message (2026-07-21 audit).
    r"(?-i:\b(?:HOLD|TRIM|ENTER|EXIT|ADD)\s+(?:existing|to|at|the))",
patches.rationale: Docstring (lines 48-49) specifies the trigger as uppercase Rule #11 verdict tokens HOLD|TRIM|ENTER|EXIT, but has_pattern() forces re.IGNORECASE on every pattern, so ordinary prose ('add to the pile', 'enter the market', 'hold at current levels') qualifies a message as position-relevant. Combined with TICKER_PATTERN matching incidental uppercase tokens ('AI' matches \b[A-Z]{2,5}\b), mundane >=800-char prose blocks with exit 2 — reproduced live (probe M3, exit 2, fire-logged 2026-07-21 11:45:26Z). Fix: wrap this ONE pattern in a scoped (?-i:...) inline-flag group, which locally overrides the IGNORECASE flag passed by has_pattern (Python 3.6+; repo runs 3.11.15; inline-flag precedent already in git-guard-pretooluse.py's (?i)). All other POSITION_RELEVANT/ANCHOR/EXEMPTION patterns keep their IGNORECASE semantics unchanged; genuine uppercase verdicts ('ADD to the NVDA position') still trigger (probe M4 exit 2 post-patch), anchored outputs still pass (probe M5 exit 0). Single-line old_string verified unique (grep -cF = 1). No docstring change needed — the code now matches the documented uppercase convention.

designs.fixtures:
macro-anchor-hook.py has NO embedded --selftest scaffold (house standard is "--selftest where present"; adding one would be a larger diff than the fix), so verification is probe-based. The validated standalone probe is at /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/probe_macro_anchor_case.py — exact code:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Probe: macro-anchor-hook POSITION_RELEVANT_MARKERS verdict-token case-sensitivity.

Runs the hook (or a patched copy given as argv[1]) against three transcripts:
  M3   lowercase prose "add to the pile" + >=800 chars filler + incidental
       uppercase token (AI) -> should NOT be position-relevant (expect exit 0
       after patch; exits 2 on the unpatched hook = the confirmed defect)
  M4   genuine uppercase verdict "ADD to the position" without any anchor
       marker -> must still block (expect exit 2 pre and post patch)
  M5   same uppercase verdict WITH an anchor marker -> pass (expect exit 0)
All filler avoids EXEMPTION_PATTERNS and ANCHOR_MARKERS tokens.
"""
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

HOOK = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \
    "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/macro-anchor-hook.py"
REPO = "/home/user/LLMNA"

# When probing a patched copy, run it inside a mock repo root so the hook's
# _REPO_ROOT/in_scope()/FIRE_LOG_PATH resolve there — NOT the real repo
# (keeps probe fires out of the real fire log; the copy's parents[3]
# fallback would otherwise resolve to /tmp/claude-0 and skip enforcement).
if len(sys.argv) > 1:
    MOCK = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(sys.argv[1])),
                        "mockrepo")
    os.makedirs(os.path.join(MOCK, "research", "meta"), exist_ok=True)
    REPO = MOCK
ENV = dict(os.environ, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=REPO)

FILLER = (
    "The debate about compute allocation keeps returning to the same point. "
    "Over the past year the industry has moved from single-cluster training "
    "runs toward distributed workloads, and the vendors selling networking "
    "gear have benefited from that shift. Observers keep asking whether the "
    "spending pace is sustainable, and each quarter the answer so far has "
    "been that budgets keep expanding. Utilities are signing supply "
    "agreements, data-center builders are pre-leasing capacity years out, "
    "and the labor market for electricians in those regions is tight. None "
    "of this settles the question of where returns accrue, but it does "
    "describe the direction of travel. Skeptics point to depreciation "
    "schedules; optimists point to usage growth. Both sides agree the "
    "buildout is large by historical standards and neither expects it to "
    "resolve quickly. "
)

CASES = {
    # Defect reproduction: ordinary prose, lowercase "add to the pile",
    # incidental uppercase token (AI) satisfies the ticker gate.
    "M3-lowercase-prose": (
        FILLER + "In the meantime we could add to the pile of evidence over "
        "time as AI develops and revisit the question next quarter.",
        {"pre": 2, "post": 0},
    ),
    # Regression guard: genuine uppercase Rule #11 verdict, no anchor marker.
    "M4-uppercase-verdict-no-anchor": (
        FILLER + "Given all that, the view here is ADD to the NVDA position "
        "on weakness and revisit after the next report.",
        {"pre": 2, "post": 2},
    ),
    # Pass-condition guard: uppercase verdict + anchor marker present.
    "M5-uppercase-verdict-with-anchor": (
        FILLER + "Given all that, the view here is ADD to the NVDA position "
        "on weakness; this is macro-anchored per the 2026-07 review.",
        {"pre": 0, "post": 0},
    ),
}


def run_case(name, text):
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
        "w", suffix=".jsonl", delete=False,
        dir=os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
    ) as tf:
        tf.write(json.dumps({
            "message": {"role": "assistant",
                        "content": [{"type": "text", "text": text}]}
        }) + "\n")
        transcript = tf.name
    payload = json.dumps({"transcript_path": transcript})
    proc = subprocess.run(
        ["python3", HOOK], input=payload, capture_output=True,
        text=True, cwd=REPO, env=ENV,
    )
    os.unlink(transcript)
    return proc.returncode


def main():
    phase = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else (
        "post" if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "pre")
    failures = 0
    for name, (text, expect) in CASES.items():
        assert len(text) >= 800, f"{name}: filler under 800 chars"
        rc = run_case(name, text)
        want = expect[phase]
        ok = "OK " if rc == want else "FAIL"
        if rc != want:
            failures += 1
        print(f"{ok} [{phase}] {name}: exit={rc} expected={want}")
    sys.exit(1 if failures else 0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Probe-confirmed: re.search(r'\b\(T1\b', '(T1 filing 2026-05-01)') -> None, and end-to-end the live hook exits 2 on a compliant '(T1 filing 2026-05-01)' message while '(T1 source 2026-05-01)' passes only via the separate \bT1\s+(?:source|verified|tier) rescue pattern (line 122). Exact code:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Probe for README 'How to add a new hook' step 5: every hook script wired in
.claude/settings.json must be named in research/CLAUDE.md's
'## Enforcement hooks (live)' section (bullet list).
Exit 0 = every wired hook documented; exit 1 = missing bullets listed."""
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path("/home/user/LLMNA")
settings = json.loads((ROOT / ".claude/settings.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
wired = set()
for event, groups in settings["hooks"].items():
    for g in groups:
        for h in g["hooks"]:
            m = re.search(r"research/meta/hooks/([\w.-]+\.(?:py|sh))", h["command"])
            if m:
                wired.add(m.group(1))

md = (ROOT / "research/CLAUDE.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
start = md.index("## Enforcement hooks (live)")
end = md.index("\n---", start)
section = md[start:end]

bullet_lines = [ln for ln in section.splitlines() if ln.startswith("- **")]
missing = sorted(n for n in wired if not any(n in ln for ln in bullet_lines))
print(f"wired hook scripts found in settings.json: {len(wired)}")
if missing:
    print("MISSING bullets for: " + ", ".join(missing))
    sys.exit(1)
print("PASS - every wired hook script is named in the Enforcement hooks (live) section")
