"""
patches.new_string:
Scope: only enforces inside research OS repo. Exit 0 outside.

Selftest (added 2026-07-21 audit): python3 macro-anchor-hook.py --selftest
-> M0-M3 fixture suite, exit 0 all-pass.
"""
patches.rationale: Document the new --selftest entry point in the header doc block, matching the session-prime-cascade-hook convention of advertising the selftest invocation in the file header.

patches.new_string:
def should_fire(text: str) -> bool:
    """Pure decision core (extracted 2026-07-21 for --selftest; gate order
    and regexes identical to the inline block it replaced)."""
    # Size gate: only fire on substantial analytical outputs
    if len(text) < 800:
        return False

    # Exemption: harness-meta / restatement / Q&A
    if has_pattern(text, EXEMPTION_PATTERNS):
        return False

    # Position-relevance gate: need TICKER + analytical marker
    ticker_present = bool(re.search(TICKER_PATTERN, text))
    position_relevant = has_pattern(text, POSITION_RELEVANT_MARKERS)
    if not (ticker_present and position_relevant):
        return False

    # Pass condition: at least ONE anchor / tag / tie-together marker
    if has_pattern(text, ANCHOR_MARKERS):
        return False

    return True


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Selftest — M0-M3 fixtures from the 2026-07-21 harness audit. M0 = the
# canonical FIRE shape (recall-anchored MRVL-style position output, the
# 2026-06-12 origin failure); M1 = same text macro-anchored -> pass; M2 =
# same text with an explicit exemption marker -> pass; M3 = position text
# under the 800-char size gate -> pass. Run: --selftest
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_M0_FIRE = (
    "MRVL bear case: AWS concentration dominates the customer mix and the "
    "Trainium demotion narrative implies the custom ASIC ramp underdelivers "
    "versus street expectations. Bull case rests on 800G optical attach "
    "rates and a broadening of the design-win base beyond the lead "
    "hyperscaler. Sizing consideration: position stays below target until "
    "the concentration question resolves. "
) + "Attach-rate math, supplier reallocation, and capex phasing detail continues. " * 8

SELFTEST = [
    ("M0 recall-anchored position output FIRES", _M0_FIRE, True),
    ("M1 macro-anchored output passes",
     _M0_FIRE + " First-principles read as of 2026-07-21: structural-demand "
                "supercycle intact.", False),
    ("M2 exemption marker passes",
     _M0_FIRE + " Q&A exemption per Critical Rule #15.", False),
    ("M3 under the 800-char size gate passes",
     "NVDA bull case: pre-earnings sizing check, no add.", False),
]


def selftest():
    failures = []
    for name, text, expected in SELFTEST:
        got = should_fire(text)
        if got != expected:
            failures.append(f"FAIL {name}: expected fire={expected}, got fire={got}")
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(SELFTEST)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)


def main():
    if not in_scope():
        sys.exit(0)

    text = load_last_assistant_message()
    if not text:
        sys.exit(0)

    if not should_fire(text):
        sys.exit(0)
patches.rationale: Closes the confirmed GAP (no embedded --selftest, house standard per anti-fabrication + session-prime-cascade siblings). Extracts the four inline gates of main() into a pure should_fire(text) decision core — same order, same regexes, same thresholds, byte-identical comments — so the selftest exercises the ACTUAL production decision path rather than a re-implementation. Adds the M0-M3 fixture suite: M0 is the canonical recall-anchored FIRE shape modeled on the 2026-06-12 MRVL origin failure (991 chars, TICKER + bear/bull/sizing markers, zero anchor/exemption markers — probe-verified to hit no EXEMPTION_PATTERNS or ANCHOR_MARKERS); M1/M2/M3 cover the anchored-pass, exemption-pass, and size-gate paths. selftest() style mirrors session-prime-cascade-hook.py (SELFTEST list + selftest() + sys.exit). E2E parity probe confirmed identical exit codes and fire-log behavior before/after the refactor.

patches.old_string:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
patches.rationale: Dispatch --selftest before main(), exactly mirroring the session-prime-cascade-hook.py __main__ block. selftest() exits itself, so main() (which would block on stdin) is never reached in selftest mode; live Stop-hook invocation (no argv flag) is unchanged.

patches.new_string:
EXPERIMENT_START = date(2026, 6, 1)  # hook pair shipped 2026-06-01

FIRE_RE = re.compile(
    r"- (\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}) .*structural-output.*FIRE(?: \((.+)\))?\s*$")


def week_key(d: date) -> str:
    iso = d.isocalendar()
    return f"{iso[0]}-W{iso[1]:02d}"


def classify(line: str):
    """Return (date, kind) for a structural-output FIRE line, else None.

    'prose' = experiment numerator (linear-prose reversion — the failure
    mode the priming hook targets). Unlabeled fires are 'prose': reason
    labels and the tier check shipped in the SAME hook commit (8c3da98
    2026-06-15), so an unlabeled fire predates the tier path and can only
    be a structural-markers fire. 'tier' = Principle #37 fires, excluded
    from the numerator. 'other' = excluded entirely (e.g. the 2026-06-12
    smoke-test line, which the log itself says not to count).
    """
    m = FIRE_RE.match(line)
    if not m:
        return None
    d = datetime.strptime(m.group(1), "%Y-%m-%d").date()
    reason = m.group(2)
    if reason is None or reason == "structural-markers-missing":
        return d, "prose"
    if reason == "position-implication-tier-missing":
        return d, "tier"
    return d, "other"
patches.rationale: Core fix: extend the regex with an optional reason-capture group anchored to end-of-line, and classify each fire by reason instead of counting every structural-output FIRE line. main() now routes through classify(): 'prose' fires feed the experiment numerator, 'position-implication-tier-missing' fires accumulate in their own bucket, 'other' (smoke-test) drops entirely. Removes 5 tier + 1 smoke-test from the numerator; keeps the 6 provably-markers unlabeled fires.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Probe for the W23-baseline fix in structural-output-metric.py.

Usage: python3 som_w23_baseline_probe.py [path-to-metric-script]
Default path: /home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/structural-output-metric.py
(after the patch is applied; pass the scratch copy to pre-verify).

Checks, via a synthetic log injected into the module (no repo writes):
  1. Header baseline line parses to 8 fires, all landing in 2026-W23.
  2. Range entry "06-06→12: 0" contributes nothing (no crash, no count).
  3. Ordinary fire lines still count exactly as before (W24 == 1).
  4. The baseline line itself is NOT double-counted by the fire-line regex.
  5. Against the real committed log: W23 == 8 and W24..W30 unchanged from
     the pre-patch live run (5, 4, 15, 24, 32, 31, 6).
"""
import importlib.util
import io
import re
import sys
import tempfile
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from pathlib import Path

SCRIPT = Path(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else Path(
    "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/structural-output-metric.py")

spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("som", SCRIPT)
som = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(som)


def run_with_log(log_path: Path) -> dict:
    som.LOG = log_path
    buf = io.StringIO()
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        som.main()
    table = {}
    for line in buf.getvalue().splitlines():
        m = re.match(r"(\d{4}-W\d{2})\s+(\d+)\s+(\d+)", line)
        if m:
            table[m.group(1)] = int(m.group(2))
    return table


failures = []

# --- synthetic log: header baseline + one genuine fire line ---------------
synthetic = (
    "# Hook fire log (probe fixture)\n"
    "Baseline before instrumentation (from transcript archaeology, deduped — "
    "see `recurring-audit-log.md` 2026-06-12 entry): 06-01: 1, 06-03: 1, "
    "06-04: 4, 06-05: 2, 06-06→12: 0.\n"
    "---\n"
    "- 2026-06-13 06:42:10Z structural-output-hook FIRE\n"
)
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", suffix=".md", delete=False,
                                 dir=tempfile.gettempdir()) as tf:
    tf.write(synthetic)
    synth_path = Path(tf.name)

t = run_with_log(synth_path)
if t.get("2026-W23") != 8:
    failures.append(f"synthetic W23 expected 8, got {t.get('2026-W23')}")
if t.get("2026-W24") != 1:
    failures.append(f"synthetic W24 expected 1, got {t.get('2026-W24')}")
synth_path.unlink()

# --- real committed log: W23 corrected, later weeks untouched -------------
real = run_with_log(Path("/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hook-fire-log.md"))
expected_real = {"2026-W23": 8, "2026-W24": 5, "2026-W25": 4, "2026-W26": 15,
                 "2026-W27": 24, "2026-W28": 32, "2026-W29": 31}
for wk, n in expected_real.items():
    if real.get(wk) != n:
        failures.append(f"real log {wk} expected {n}, got {real.get(wk)}")

if failures:
    print("FAIL")
    for f in failures:
        print(" -", f)
    sys.exit(1)

patches.new_string:
["git", "-C", str(REPO), "log", "main",
         # %cs (committer date), NOT %as: --since filters on committer date,
         # so author-date bucketing can shift a rebased/grafted commit into a
         # week the filter never admitted it under (caught 2026-07-21 audit;
         # no-op on current history — 1142/1142 commits have %as == %cs).
         f"--since={EXPERIMENT_START.isoformat()}", "--format=%cs"],
patches.rationale: Aligns the commit-bucketing basis (printed date) with the commit-selection basis (--since, which git evaluates against COMMITTER date). With %as, a commit authored in week N but committed in week N+1 — the rebase/cherry-pick/graft signature this repo demonstrably produces (the 2026-07-06 migration graft that the adjacent --first-parent comment already guards against) — is admitted by the filter yet bucketed into a week the filter never considered, and author-local timezones can shift week boundaries by a day vs the otherwise-UTC series. Verified safe: all 1142 main-branch commits since EXPERIMENT_START currently have %as == %cs, so the patched script's output is byte-identical to baseline today (diff-confirmed) — the recorded 08-06 adjudicator series (0.062 -> 0.045 -> 0.106 -> 0.131 ...) is untouched; the fix only removes a latent distortion class at week edges going forward. Comment block matches the file's existing style of documenting git-semantics traps inline at the call site.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Probe: demonstrate --since (committer-date filter) vs %as (author-date
print) mixing in a synthetic repo, and verify %cs matches the filter basis."""
import subprocess
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path


def run(args, cwd, env=None):
    import os
    full_env = dict(os.environ)
    if env:
        full_env.update(env)
    return subprocess.run(args, cwd=cwd, env=full_env,
                          capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)


def main():
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(dir=str(Path(__file__).parent)) as td:
        repo = Path(td)
        run(["git", "init", "-q", "-b", "main"], repo)
        run(["git", "config", "user.email", "probe@test"], repo)
        run(["git", "config", "user.name", "probe"], repo)
        (repo / "a.txt").write_text("a\n")
        run(["git", "add", "a.txt"], repo)
        run(["git", "commit", "-q", "-m", "A"], repo, env={

patches.new_string:
def week_key(d: date) -> str:
    iso = d.isocalendar()
    return f"{iso[0]}-W{iso[1]:02d}"


def trend(rates, today):
    """Window read fixed 2026-07-21 (adjudicator change, logged pre-decision):
    (1) the in-progress ISO week is EXCLUDED — a partial week's noisy rate
    must not be the deciding endpoint of a pre-registered call (on 08-06,
    a Thursday, the last 4 complete weeks are W28-W31 = exactly the 07-06
    extension window); (2) FALLING = second-half mean < first-half mean of
    the tail, per the registered "falling over the extension window"
    wording — endpoint-only compare let one spike/dip week flip the verdict.
    Returns (first_mean, second_mean, direction) or None if <2 usable weeks."""
    complete = [(w, r) for w, r in rates if w != week_key(today)]
    tail = [r for _, r in complete[-4:] if r == r]  # drop NaN
    if len(tail) < 2:
        return None
    half = len(tail) // 2
    first, second = tail[:half], tail[half:]
    m1, m2 = sum(first) / len(first), sum(second) / len(second)
    return m1, m2, ("FALLING" if m2 < m1 else "FLAT/RISING")


def main() -> None:
patches.rationale: Core fix, extracted as a pure function so it is fixture-testable. (1) Excludes the current in-progress ISO week — today's run used W30 with ~1.5 days of data (6 fires/66 commits = 0.091) as the deciding endpoint, and an 08-06 Thursday run would use a partial W32; methodology.md:1694 already documents the house posture ('W28 partial, 2 days — do NOT adjudicate early') that the code violated. (2) Half-mean compare implements 'FALLING over the extension window' instead of endpoint-only (which let a rising middle read FALLING). Calendar fact making this registration-faithful by construction: 2026-07-06 is the Monday of W28, so on decision day the last 4 complete ISO weeks are W28-W31 = exactly the pre-registered extension window. Deterministic, no new dependencies; orthogonal to the separately booked G-28 numerator fix (which edits the fires regex/filter, not the tail/direction).

        print("Decision rule: FALLING -> keep priming hook; FLAT/RISING -> retire (adjudicate 2026-08-06).")

patches.new_string:
def selftest():
    today = date(2026, 7, 21)  # ISO 2026-W30 (Tuesday — W30 in progress)
    wk = ["2026-W26", "2026-W27", "2026-W28", "2026-W29", "2026-W30"]
    nan = float("nan")
    cases = [
        # monotone fall across complete weeks -> FALLING (regression guard)
        (list(zip(wk[:4], [0.4, 0.3, 0.2, 0.1])), "FALLING"),
        # dip endpoint after a rising middle: endpoints fall but the window
        # mean rises -> FLAT/RISING (old endpoint-only compare said FALLING)
        (list(zip(wk[:4], [0.10, 0.30, 0.35, 0.09])), "FLAT/RISING"),
        # in-progress W30 excluded: its low partial rate must not flip the
        # read of the 4 complete weeks before it (old code said FALLING)
        (list(zip(wk, [0.10, 0.20, 0.30, 0.40, 0.05])), "FLAT/RISING"),
        # NaN (zero-commit) week dropped, remainder still adjudicated
        (list(zip(wk[:4], [0.4, nan, 0.2, 0.1])), "FALLING"),
        # <2 usable complete weeks -> no verdict printed
        ([(wk[3], 0.2), (wk[4], 0.1)], None),
    ]
    for i, (rates, want) in enumerate(cases, 1):
        got = trend(rates, today)
        got_dir = got[2] if got else None
        assert got_dir == want, f"fixture {i}: want {want}, got {got_dir}"
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(cases)}/{len(cases)} fixtures pass")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        selftest()
    else:
        main()

# line mentioned structural-output before FIRE (anchored 2026-07-21;

FIRE_RE = re.compile(

)

def _selftest() -> None:

        ("- 2026-06-13 06:42:10Z structural-output-hook FIRE", True),

        ("- 2026-06-23 11:37:54Z structural-output-hook FIRE (position-implication-tier-missing)", True),
        # Adversarial: 'structural-output' substring before FIRE, wrong hook
        # (the loose pre-2026-07-21 regex counted this class)

patches.old_string:
def main() -> None:

patches.new_string:
def token_armed(name: str, cmd: str) -> bool:
    # 2026-07-21 audit fix (token-in-payload disarm): the old bare-substring check
    # armed on ANY occurrence, so a command that merely PRINTED the token (echo,
    # commit-message text — same data-not-flags class as the -m stripper below)
    # unlocked its class. Canonical use is the env-prefix form (`OPERATOR_APPROVED_X=1
    # git ...` — git/pre-commit L60, governance §1), so accept ONLY that: assignment
    # at command start, optionally after `env` and/or other simple VAR=val
    # assignments. Token honesty stays instruction-level per governance.
    return (os.environ.get(name) == "1"
            or re.match(rf"\s*(env\s+)?([A-Za-z_]\w*=\S+\s+)*{name}=1\s", cmd)
            is not None)


def main() -> None:
    raw = sys.stdin.read()
patches.rationale: Core fix. Anchored re.match kills the token-in-payload disarm class: the token must be a shell env-prefix assignment at command start (optionally after `env` and/or other simple VAR=val assignments), matching the only documented legitimate usage. Quoted/echoed/commit-message occurrences can no longer arm because a non-assignment word (echo, git, quote char) at position 0 breaks the match. Module-level (not nested in main) so the embedded selftest exercises it directly. Deterministic, no backtracking risk (\S+/\s+ classes are disjoint).

]


def selftest() -> int:
    os.environ.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_FORCE_PUSH", None)
    os.environ.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION", None)
    fails = 0
    for fix_cmd, name, want in SELFTEST_FIXTURES:
        got = token_armed(name, fix_cmd)
        if got != want:
            fails += 1
            print(f"FAIL token_armed({name}): {fix_cmd!r} -> {got}, want {want}")
    print(f"git-guard-pretooluse --selftest: "
          f"{len(SELFTEST_FIXTURES) - fails}/{len(SELFTEST_FIXTURES)} pass")
    return 1 if fails else 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        sys.exit(selftest())
    try:
        main()

patches.rationale: Closes the confirmed HIGH defect: `git commit -n` is byte-for-byte git's --no-verify and passed the guard (probe-verified exit 0), silently skipping pre-commit Functions 1-4 (diff-print, secrets scan, session-prime cap gate, destructive-change guard) and the commit-msg claims guard, while the web allowlist Bash(git commit *) auto-approves it. Fix is a separate commit-only check placed inside the same `if not force_ok:` branch so the OPERATOR_APPROVED_FORCE_PUSH token exempts it identically to the long form (probe-verified). Regex `\bcommit\b[^|;&]*\s-n\b` mirrors the existing check's segment-scoped style: `[^|;&]*` stops at pipe/;/&& so `git commit -m 'x' && grep -n f` passes; `\s-n\b` requires a standalone whitespace-preceded token so `-am`, `--dry-run`, `--no-verify` itself, and quoted message payloads (already stripped to MSG at line 114) never false-positive. Push branch deliberately untouched: `git push -n` = --dry-run, harmless, must stay allowed (probe case exit 0). Bundled forms (`-an`) intentionally NOT matched: a bundle regex would false-positive on `-uno` (--untracked-files=no) and the confused-agent threat model emits plain `-n`; conservative per house small-diff rule.

patches.new_string:
m = re.search(r"(?i)core\.hookspath[=\s]+(\S+)", s)
        # A captured shell operator means a READ chained with another command
        # (`git config core.hooksPath && ...`), not a set-form — a value read is not an
        # override (FP fix 2026-07-21, found during G-25 verification). Operators are
        # never valid paths; a quoted ';evil' value keeps its quote in the capture, so
        # it still fails the canonical compare and blocks.
        if m and not re.match(r"[;&|]", m.group(1)) and m.group(1).strip("'\"") != CANON_HOOKS:
            block("core.hooksPath override to a non-canonical value (disables git guards)", cmd)
patches.rationale: The docstring (line 17) scopes this rule to 'git config / -c / --unset of core.hooksPath to a NON-CANONICAL VALUE' — an override. The regex (?i)core\.hookspath[=\s]+(\S+) captures whatever token follows the key, so a read-only query chained with anything ('git config core.hooksPath && echo hi') captures '&&' as the 'value' and blocks — demonstrated live on this audit's first Bash call (hook-fire-log.md line 863, 2026-07-21 06:51:14Z) and reproduced by probe (exit 2 for &&, ||, and | chains). Fix: when the captured token BEGINS with a shell operator char (; & |), it is a chained read, not a set-form — skip the block. Safe because (a) operators are never valid hooksPath values; (b) to SET a value starting with ;/&/| the shell requires quoting, and the quote char survives in the \S+ capture ('\';evil\'' starts with the quote, not the operator), so quoted operator-leading values still fail the canonical compare and block — verified by adversarial probe; (c) unquoted 'git config core.hooksPath ;evil' / '&&evil' / '|evil' parse in the shell as a hooksPath READ followed by a separate command, so passing them is behaviorally correct; (d) set-forms with a real value ('git config core.hooksPath /tmp/evil && echo done', 'git -c core.hooksPath=/tmp/evil status && echo hi', backgrounded 'set &') capture the value token first and still block. Fail-closed residual accepted: a read with an immediately-following redirect ('git config core.hooksPath 2>/dev/null') still FPs — token starts with '2', not an operator; the sanctioned clean reads remain '--get' and the bare end-of-command form. One-line condition change + comment, matching the file's G-fix comment style; no exit-path, logging, or token-gate changes.

designs.fixtures:
The hook has NO embedded --selftest (unlike sibling hooks), so fixtures ship as a standalone probe runnable against any copy of the hook; suggested durable home: research/meta/tests/test_git_guard_hookspath.py (new file — this audit is repo-read-only, so it currently lives in scratchpad). Exact code (verified working, encodes patched-correct behavior; against the UNPATCHED hook the first 3 cases fail, demonstrating the FP):

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""git-guard core.hooksPath rule fixtures (FP fix 2026-07-21, found during G-25 verify).
Usage: python3 test_git_guard_hookspath.py /path/to/git-guard-pretooluse.py
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR points at a scratch dir so log_line()/ensure_hooks_path() side
effects never touch the live repo (both fail silently — hook is fail-open)."""
import json, os, subprocess, sys, tempfile

HOOK = sys.argv[1]
FAKE = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="gitguard-fixture-")  # plain dir, NOT a git repo
env = dict(os.environ, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=FAKE)
env.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_FORCE_PUSH", None)
env.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION", None)

CASES = [
    # -- read-forms chained with shell operators: a value READ is not an override -> pass
    ("git config core.hooksPath && echo ok", 0, "read chained with && (demonstrated FP, fire-log:863)"),
    ("git config core.hooksPath || true", 0, "read chained with ||"),
    ("git config core.hooksPath | cat", 0, "read piped into cat"),
    # -- already-passing read forms (regression guard)
    ("git config --get core.hooksPath", 0, "sanctioned --get read"),
    ("git config core.hooksPath", 0, "bare read at end of command"),
    # -- set-forms: canonical passes, everything else blocks (G-25 surface, must NOT regress)
    ("git config core.hooksPath research/meta/hooks/git", 0, "set to canonical value"),
    ("git config core.hooksPath /tmp/evil-hooks", 2, "set to non-canonical path"),
    ("git -c core.hooksPath=/tmp/evil-hooks push origin main", 2, "inline -c non-canonical"),
    ("git config core.hookspath /tmp/evil-hooks", 2, "case-insensitive set (G-25) still blocked"),
    ("git -c core.hooksPath= push", 2, "empty value (disables hooks) still blocked"),
    ("git config --unset core.hooksPath", 2, "unset form still blocked"),
    # -- quoted operator-leading values are real set-forms: quote survives in \S+ capture
    ("git config core.hooksPath ';evil'", 2, "single-quoted ;-leading value still blocked"),
    ('git config core.hooksPath "&evil"', 2, "double-quoted &-leading value still blocked"),
    # -- adversarial: exemption must not open a set-form bypass
    ("git config core.hooksPath /tmp/evil && echo done", 2, "set-then-chain still blocked"),
    ("git config core.hooksPath /tmp/evil &", 2, "backgrounded set still blocked"),
    ("git -c core.hooksPath=/tmp/evil status && echo hi", 2, "inline -c set-then-chain still blocked"),
    ("git config core.hooksPath '&& /tmp/evil'", 2, "quoted operator-prefixed value still blocked"),
    ("git config core.hooksPath&&echo hi", 0, "tight && chain: [=\\s]+ unmatched, shell reads then echoes"),
    ("git -c core.hooksPath=| cat", 0, "empty-value pipe: git gets no subcommand, inert"),
]

fails = 0
for cmd, want, label in CASES:
    payload = json.dumps({"tool_name": "Bash", "tool_input": {"command": cmd}})
    r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, HOOK], input=payload,
                       capture_output=True, text=True, env=env, timeout=30)
    ok = r.returncode == want
    fails += 0 if ok else 1
    print(f"{'PASS' if ok else 'FAIL'}  exit={r.returncode} want={want}  {label}")
print("RESULT:", "ALL PASS" if fails == 0 else f"{fails} FAILURE(S)")
sys.exit(1 if fails else 0)

patches.old_string:
r"restore\s+[^|;&]*(--worktree|--staged|\.(\s|$))|"
                r"worktree\s+remove[^|;&]*(--force|-f))\b", s):
patches.new_string:
# restore-dot alternative ends on "." — a trailing \b can never
                # hold after a non-word char, so it gets lookahead + a \b-or-
                # after-dot group close (K3-class fix: `git restore .` passed).
                r"restore\s+[^|;&]*(--worktree|--staged|\.(?=\s|$))|"
                r"worktree\s+remove[^|;&]*(--force|-f))(\b|(?<=\.))", s):
patches.rationale: The plumbing alternation (lines 133-138) closes with `)\b`. When the `\.(\s|$)` alternative matched a dot at end-of-string or before `&&`/`;`, the match's last char was `.` (or trailing whitespace followed by a non-word char) — both sides non-word, so `\b` failed and the dot alternative was dead in its most common forms: `git restore .`, `git restore . && echo`, and `git restore -- .` all PASSED (probe-confirmed exit 0 on the live hook); only `git restore . <wordchar...>` blocked. Fix is two coordinated 1-token regex edits: (1) `\.(\s|$)` -> `\.(?=\s|$)` makes the terminator zero-width so the match deterministically ends ON the dot; (2) `)\b` -> `)(\b|(?<<=\.))` [actual text: `)(\b|(?<=\.))`] accepts the group close either at a word boundary (identical to before for every other alternative) or immediately after a matched `.` — and the lookbehind arm is reachable ONLY via the dot alternative, since no other alternative in the group can end on a literal dot, so all other families keep byte-identical accept/reject behavior. Verified: 18-case matrix on a patched scratch copy — 3 formerly-passing discard forms now exit 2; all 6 previously-blocking forms still exit 2; 6 legitimate forms (`git restore file.txt`, `git restore .gitignore`, `git restore --source=HEAD~1 notes.md`, `git status`, `git reset --hardware`, `git commit -m 'restore . of calm'`) still exit 0; malformed-stdin fail-open still exit 0. The one FP shape the patch extends (`echo restore . nothing-git`, where `\bgit\b` matches the hyphenated `-git` token) is the hook's pre-existing prose-FP class — CURRENT code already blocks `echo restore --staged . nothing-git` and `echo reset --hard nothing-git` (probe-confirmed) — so no new FP mechanism is introduced; the loose is_git gate is a separate, pre-existing ledger row, out of scope for this patch. old_string is byte-exact and unique (count==1 asserted programmatically before replacement).

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Fixture set for the restore-dot dead-alternative fix in git-guard-pretooluse.py.
Runs the hook as a subprocess with CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR pointed at a scratch dir so
ensure_hooks_path()/log_line() never touch the real repo. exit 2=BLOCK, 0=PASS."""
import json, os, subprocess, sys
HOOK = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/git-guard-pretooluse.py"
SCRATCH = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
FAKE = os.path.join(SCRATCH, "fake-repo"); os.makedirs(os.path.join(FAKE, "research", "meta"), exist_ok=True)
CASES = [
    # the fixed defect class: bulk working-tree discard must BLOCK
    ("git restore .",                            2),
    ("git restore . && echo done",               2),
    ("git restore -- .",                         2),
    ("git restore . foo",                        2),   # blocked before fix too
    # sibling alternatives: no regression, must keep BLOCKING
    ("git restore --staged .",                   2),
    ("git restore --worktree research/INDEX.md", 2),
    ("git reset --hard HEAD~1",                  2),
    ("git stash drop",                           2),
    ("git worktree remove --force wt",           2),
    # legitimate forms: must keep PASSING (FP guard)
    ("git restore file.txt",                     0),
    ("git restore .gitignore",                   0),   # single dotfile, not bulk
    ("git restore --source=HEAD~1 notes.md",     0),
    ("git reset --hardware",                     0),   # trailing-\b class still guarded
    ("git commit -m 'restore . of calm'",        0),   # -m payload stripped to MSG
    ("echo restore . nothing-here",              0),   # no `git` token -> is_git gate holds
]
fails = 0
for cmd, want in CASES:
    env = dict(os.environ, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=FAKE)
    env.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_FORCE_PUSH", None); env.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION", None)
    r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, HOOK],
                       input=json.dumps({"tool_name": "Bash", "tool_input": {"command": cmd}}),
                       capture_output=True, text=True, env=env, timeout=30)
    ok = r.returncode == want; fails += 0 if ok else 1
    print(f"{'ok  ' if ok else 'FAIL'} exit={r.returncode}(want {want})  {cmd!r}")
r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, HOOK], input="not json",
                   capture_output=True, text=True, env=dict(os.environ, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=FAKE), timeout=30)
ok = r.returncode == 0; fails += 0 if ok else 1
print(f"{'ok  ' if ok else 'FAIL'} malformed-stdin fail-open exit={r.returncode}(want 0)")
print("RESULT:", "ALL OK" if fails == 0 else f"{fails} FAILURES"); sys.exit(1 if fails else 0)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Two-sided verification of the checkout-gap patch: runs LIVE guard vs PATCHED
copy on the same payload set. Payloads constructed here (never inline in Bash).
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR -> sandbox so no writes touch the real fire-log."""
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys

SCRATCH = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
LIVE = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/git-guard-pretooluse.py"
PATCHED = os.path.join(SCRATCH, "git-guard.checkout-gap-patched.py")
FAKE_REPO = os.path.join(SCRATCH, "fakerepo-checkout")
os.makedirs(os.path.join(FAKE_REPO, "research", "meta"), exist_ok=True)
subprocess.run(["git", "init", "-q", FAKE_REPO], capture_output=True)

env = dict(os.environ)
env["CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"] = FAKE_REPO
env.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_FORCE_PUSH", None)
env.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION", None)

# (label, command, patched_expectation)
CASES = [
    ("GAP checkout -- .",            "git checkout -- .",                    "BLOCK"),
    ("GAP checkout .",               "git checkout .",                       "BLOCK"),
    ("GAP checkout HEAD -- path",    "git checkout HEAD -- research/",       "BLOCK"),
    ("GAP checkout HEAD~2 -- path",  "git checkout HEAD~2 -- research/",     "BLOCK"),
    ("GAP checkout ./research",      "git checkout ./research",              "BLOCK"),
    ("GAP checkout -- file",         "git checkout -- research/INDEX.md",    "BLOCK"),
    ("GAP checkout main -- .",       "git checkout main -- .",               "BLOCK"),
    ("GAP restore . (dead 07-20 leg)","git restore .",                       "BLOCK"),
    ("GAP restore . && more",        "git restore . && git status",          "BLOCK"),
    ("GAP restore --source=HEAD .",  "git restore --source=HEAD .",          "BLOCK"),
    ("token override",  "OPERATOR_APPROVED_FORCE_PUSH=1 git checkout -- .",  "PASS"),
    ("checkout -f",                  "git checkout -f main",                 "BLOCK"),
    ("restore --worktree",           "git restore --worktree research/",     "BLOCK"),
    ("restore --staged",             "git restore --staged research/",       "BLOCK"),
    ("reset --hard",                 "git reset --hard HEAD~1",              "BLOCK"),
    ("checkout branch",              "git checkout main",                    "PASS"),
    ("checkout -b new",              "git checkout -b feature-x",            "PASS"),
    ("checkout prev branch (-)",     "git checkout -",                       "PASS"),
    ("checkout dotted tag",          "git checkout v1.2.0",                  "PASS"),
    ("checkout slash-dot branch",    "git checkout release/1.2.x",           "PASS"),
    ("checkout --track",             "git checkout --track origin/main",     "PASS"),
    ("checkout dashed branch",       "git checkout feature--cleanup",        "PASS"),
    ("checkout dotfile by name",     "git checkout .gitignore",              "PASS"),
    ("restore single file",          "git restore somefile.md",              "PASS"),
    ("switch branch",                "git switch main",                      "PASS"),
    ("diff dashdash dot",            "git diff -- .",                        "PASS"),
    ("log dot pathspec",             "git log --oneline -- .",               "PASS"),
    ("add dot",                      "git add .",                            "PASS"),
    ("status dot",                   "git status .",                         "PASS"),
    ("stash push dot",               "git stash push .",                     "PASS"),
    ("msg mentions checkout -- .",   'git commit -m "guard checkout -- . forms now"', "PASS"),
    ("msg mentions restore .",       'git commit -m "note: restore . is guarded"',    "PASS"),
    ("non-git checkout word",        "svn checkout . && ls",                 "PASS"),
    ("redirect with dotfile",        "git checkout main > .log 2>&1 || true","PASS"),
]


def run(hook, cmd):
    payload = json.dumps({"tool_name": "Bash", "tool_input": {"command": cmd}})
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, hook], input=payload, capture_output=True,
                       text=True, env=env, cwd=FAKE_REPO)
    return "BLOCK" if p.returncode == 2 else ("PASS" if p.returncode == 0 else f"EXIT{p.returncode}")


fails = 0
w = max(len(c[0]) for c in CASES)
print(f"{'case':<{w}}  live     patched  expect   verdict")
for label, cmd, expect in CASES:
    live, patched = run(LIVE, cmd), run(PATCHED, cmd)
    ok = patched == expect
    # regression rule: anything the live guard blocked must stay blocked
    if live == "BLOCK" and patched != "BLOCK":
        ok = False
    fails += (not ok)
    print(f"{label:<{w}}  {live:<8} {patched:<8} {expect:<8} {'ok' if ok else '<<< FAIL'}")

print(f"\n{'ALL OK' if fails == 0 else f'{fails} FAILURES'}")
sys.exit(1 if fails else 0)

            # Nested-path fix (2026-07-21 audit; same mechanism as the G-24 gate fix):
            # the private target regex required a delimiter right after the token, so
            # every nested subpath passed — `rm -rf research/companies`, and notably
            # `rm -rf research/portfolio` (the REAL portfolio home). REPO_TOKENS already

            # env-independent) — OR it in. Old regex kept: it alone covers `/` root.
            if re.search(rf"(\s|=|'|\"|^)({re.escape(REPO)}(/research|/\.git|/portfolio)?/?|"

patches.rationale: Fixes the confirmed DEFECT: docstring line 22 promises the rm branch protects record trees, but the private tail regex required a delimiter immediately after the (optional) trailing slash, so every nested subpath passed — 'rm -rf research/companies', 'rm -rf research/predictions/', 'rm -rf /home/user/LLMNA/research/companies', and 'rm -rf research/portfolio' (the REAL portfolio home; bare 'portfolio' which blocked does not exist at repo root). OR-ing the tail against REPO_TOKENS reuses the exact mechanism of the 2026-07-20 G-24 has_repo fix: its research token '(^|[\s'\"=(/])research(/|\b)' matches nested and slash-prefixed forms, and its hardcoded /home/user/LLMNA alternative makes absolute-path blocking independent of CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR (probe showed 'rm -rf /home/user/LLMNA' itself passed under a foreign CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR because the literal lived only in REPO_TOKENS). The old private regex is retained, not replaced, because it alone covers the bare '/' root form. Verified FP widening is precedented and accepted: 'rm -rf /tmp/research-cache' and compound commands mentioning research/ after the rm now block — identical semantics to the has_repo gate that already governs the find/xargs/truncate/dd branches today ('find /tmp/research-cache -delete' blocks on live code); OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION=1 override verified intact. One-condition diff; determinism preserved; blast radius stays MEDIUM (working-tree-only, git-recoverable, pre-commit F4 one layer down).

patches.new_string:
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Selftest — rm-target matcher fixtures (added 2026-07-21 with the nested-path
# fix). Re-invokes this file as a subprocess with CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR pointed at
# a temp dir, so log_line/ensure_hooks_path side effects never touch the real
# repo (REPO_TOKENS keeps its hardcoded /home/user/LLMNA alternative, so
# absolute-path fixtures stay faithful). Run: --selftest
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SELFTEST = [
    # (name, command, expected exit)  0 = allow, 2 = block
    ("nested subdir blocks", "rm -rf research/companies", 2),
    ("nested trailing slash blocks", "rm -rf research/predictions/", 2),
    ("nested absolute blocks (env-independent)",
     "rm -rf /home/user/LLMNA/research/companies", 2),
    ("real portfolio home blocks", "rm -rf research/portfolio", 2),
    ("long-flag nested blocks", "rm --recursive --force research/companies", 2),
    ("whole-token research blocks", "rm -rf research", 2),
    ("repo .git blocks", "rm -rf .git", 2),
    ("bare dot blocks", "rm -rf .", 2),
    ("scratch path allowed", "rm -rf /tmp/build", 0),
    ("node_modules allowed", "rm -rf node_modules", 0),
    ("non-recursive rm allowed", "rm -f notes.txt", 0),
    ("operator token honored",
     "OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION=1 rm -rf research/companies", 0),
    ("parent dir allowed", "rm -rf ../other-repo", 0),
    ("no rm at all allowed", "git status", 0),
]


def selftest():
    import tempfile
    failures = []
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
        env = dict(os.environ, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=td)
        env.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION", None)
        env.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_FORCE_PUSH", None)
        for name, command, want in SELFTEST:
            payload = json.dumps({"tool_name": "Bash",
                                  "tool_input": {"command": command}})
            r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.abspath(__file__)],
                               input=payload, capture_output=True, text=True,
                               env=env, timeout=30)
            if r.returncode != want:
                failures.append(f"FAIL {name}: exit {r.returncode}, want {want}")
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(SELFTEST)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        selftest()
    try:
        main()
patches.rationale: House standard requires embedded --selftest with fixtures where behavior changes ship; git-guard-pretooluse.py had none. Follows the two existing conventions: session-prime-cascade-hook's SELFTEST fixture-list + '--selftest' in sys.argv gate, and anti-fabrication-hook's side-effect isolation (temp-dir log redirection). Subprocess re-invocation with CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=tempdir exercises the full stdin-JSON decision path (including the -m stripping and token checks) while log_line and ensure_hooks_path write only into the throwaway dir — the real hook-fire-log.md is never touched, and OPERATOR_APPROVED_* env pollution is scrubbed so fixtures are deterministic. 14 fixtures: 5 defect-class (all verified FAIL on pre-patch code — the exact probe matrix from the finding, plus the long-flag nested variant), 3+1 regression blocks (whole-token research/.git/bare-dot), and 6 allow-negatives guarding against FP creep (scratch paths, node_modules, non-recursive rm, parent dir, operator-token override, no-rm command). Verified 14/14 pass on the patched copy.

patches.old_string:
# Long-flag forms included (K3-Swarm G-23 fix): `rm --recursive --force`
        # escaped the short-flag-only regex; LLM agents emit long flags habitually.
        if re.search(r"\brm\s+(-[a-zA-Z]*r[a-zA-Z]*f|-[a-zA-Z]*f[a-zA-Z]*r|-r\s+-f|-f\s+-r"
                     r"|--recursive\b[^|;&]*--force\b|--force\b[^|;&]*--recursive\b"
                     r"|--recursive\b[^|;&]*-[a-zA-Z]*f|-[a-zA-Z]*r[a-zA-Z]*\b[^|;&]*--force\b)", s):
patches.new_string:
# Recursive ALONE suffices (2026-07-21 audit fix): in the non-interactive
        # Bash-tool context `rm -r` deletes a writable tree exactly as thoroughly as
        # `rm -rf` (no tty to prompt), so the force requirement of the old branches
        # (K3-Swarm G-23 lineage) carried no safety information — `rm -r research/`
        # passed. Short (-r/-R, incl. clusters like -fr) and long (--recursive) forms;
        # leading flag tokens allowed so separated forms (`rm -f -r`) still match.
        if re.search(r"\brm\s+(-[^\s|;&]+\s+)*(-[a-zA-Z]*[rR]|--recursive\b)", s):
patches.rationale: Core fix. Old regex required recursive AND force in every branch, so 'rm -r research/' / 'rm -r /home/user/LLMNA' passed (probe-confirmed exit 0) despite being identically destructive in this harness — all rm goes through the non-interactive Bash tool where no tty exists to prompt. New regex: recursive flag alone (short -r/-R incl. clusters like -fr/-ir, or --recursive) triggers the existing repo-target tail check (unchanged). `(-[^\s|;&]+\s+)*` permits leading flag tokens so separated forms (rm -f -r, rm -v -r) match, while requiring each token to start with '-' and excluding shell separators — this anchoring prevents matching hyphens inside filenames or long flags containing 'r' (probe-verified: 'rm --verbose research/notes.md', 'rm --force research/notes.md', 'rm -r my-notes-dir', 'rm research/notes.md' all still pass; all 4 old force-form fixtures still block; operator token and -m payload stripping unaffected). Also adds -R (uppercase POSIX recursive), previously uncovered even with force.

patches.old_string: block("recursive force-delete aimed at repo root / research / portfolio / .git", cmd)
patches.new_string: block("recursive delete aimed at repo root / research / portfolio / .git", cmd)
patches.rationale: Actionable-stderr accuracy (house standard) + fire-log audit trail: after the regex change, fires include forceless 'rm -r' — the reason string must describe what actually matched, not claim a force flag that may be absent. Probe-verified the new reason logs correctly via log_line on block.

RESIDUAL RISKS ACCEPTED (documented, unchanged mechanics): (1) marginal FP widening on quoted mentions — a Bash command containing e.g. grep 'rm -r research' blocks; the identical class already blocks today for 'rm -rf' (guard scans raw text; only -m/--message payloads are stripped); in this harness all rm is non-interactive so the force-flag distinction carried no safety information, per the finding's risk note. (2) Pre-existing naive tail scoping via s.split("rm", 1) is untouched (separate mechanic/finding). (3) destructive-change-governance.md §1c wording sync recommended as a follow-up doc edit in that file.

patches.old_string: python/perl/ruby/node rmtree/unlink/remove on repo path ;
patches.new_string: python/perl/ruby/node rmtree/unlink/rm (via -c/-e/heredoc; script-file contents unscannable) on repo path ;
patches.rationale: Docstring honesty. The old COVERAGE line reads as an unconditional 'python/perl/ruby/node rmtree/unlink on repo path' guarantee, but the code only ever scanned -c/-e inline forms. This states the true scope: -c/-e AND heredoc are now covered, while script-file bodies (python3 /tmp/x.py) are an inherent, documented residual of command-text scanning. No behavior change; closes the docstring-overclaim half of the finding.

patches.old_string: if re.search(r"\b(python3?|perl|ruby|node)\b[^|;&]*(-c|-e)\b", s) and \
patches.new_string:
# Heredoc (<<) forms + node require('fs').rm / rmSync families included —
        # the -c/-e + literal fs.rm regex missed both (heredoc carries no inline
        # flag; `require('fs').rm(` has no contiguous `fs.rm`). Script-file bodies
        # (python3 /tmp/x.py) stay unscannable at the command-text layer — residual.
        if re.search(r"\b(python3?|perl|ruby|node)\b[^|;&]*(-c\b|-e\b|<<)", s) and \
patches.rationale: Adds heredoc detection to the interpreter-flag gate. `python3 <<EOF ... rmtree ... EOF` carried no -c/-e flag, so the first re.search failed and the whole interpreter-deletion check was skipped even with a repo path and a deletion verb present (probe-confirmed PASS pre-patch). New alternation `(-c\b|-e\b|<<)` keeps the word-boundary discipline on -c/-e (so -central etc. don't match) and adds a literal `<<` alternative without a trailing \b (heredoc delimiters may be quoted `<<'EOF'` or dash-stripped `<<-EOF`, which would defeat a \b). The `[^|;&]*` span already crosses newlines (negated class matches \n), so the verb/has_repo searches over the full heredoc body still apply. FP surface is identical to the pre-existing -c/-e check (requires interpreter + deletion verb + repo path jointly). The 4-line comment matches the file's existing verbose fix-comment density (cf. lines 46-49, 125-127, 146-147, 166-169).

patches.old_string: re.search(r"(rmtree|unlink|os\.remove|removedirs|\brmdir\b|shutil\.rmtree|fs\.rm)", s) and has_repo:
patches.new_string: re.search(r"(rmtree|unlink|os\.remove|removedirs|\brmdir\b|shutil\.rmtree|fs\.rm|rmSync|rmdirSync|require\(.fs.\)(\.promises)?\.rm)", s) and has_repo:
patches.rationale: Broadens the node deletion-verb list. The old list matched literal `fs.rm` only, so the idiomatic `require('fs').rm(...)` (and the promise/Sync variants) passed even with -e and a repo path (probe-confirmed PASS pre-patch), because `require('fs').rm` has no contiguous `fs.rm` substring. Added: (a) `rmSync` / `rmdirSync` — Node-fs-specific method names, near-zero FP, catching the Sync forms regardless of how the fs handle was obtained (fs.rmSync, f.rmSync, require('fs').rmSync); (b) `require\(.fs.\)(\.promises)?\.rm` — the async `.rm` off a require, covering both `require('fs').rm(` and `require('fs').promises.rm(`. The `.fs.` char-generic quote match avoids embedding a double-quote inside the raw double-quoted regex string (which would force a backslash into the char class); `require(Xfs X)` is specific enough to keep FP negligible. `unlinkSync` needs no new token — the existing bare `unlink` already covers it as a prefix. Still gated by has_repo, so non-repo node scripts are unaffected.

designs.fixtures:
git-guard-pretooluse.py has NO embedded --selftest harness (verified: only def main() at L92 and the __main__ guard at L179 — no argv/--selftest branch), so there is no in-file fixture table to extend. The behavior fixtures are the stdin-JSON payload set below, driven through the hook as a subprocess. Each MUST flip PASS(exit0)->BLOCK(exit2) after the patch; the controls MUST stay PASS. This exact set is embedded in the runnable probe under 'tests'.

MUST-BLOCK-AFTER (were PASS before):
  H1  heredoc:        python3 <<EOF\\nimport shutil\\nshutil.rmtree('<REPO>/research')\\nEOF
  H2  heredoc quoted: python3 <<'EOF'\\nimport shutil\\nshutil.rmtree('<REPO>/research')\\nEOF
  N1  node require:   node -e "require('fs').rm('<REPO>/research',{recursive:true})"
  N2  node reqSync:   node -e "require(\"fs\").rmSync('<REPO>/research')"
  N3  node promises:  node -e "require('fs').promises.rm('<REPO>/research')"

The fixture below follows that precedent — a dual-mode sandbox probe already written to /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/probe_root_claude_md.py and RUN (9/9 PASS, exit 0). Pre-apply it demonstrates the live gap (case A: root CLAUDE.md deletion commits unblocked) then verifies the in-memory-patched hook; post-apply it detects the fix in the repo hook and runs the patched matrix as-is. Never writes the live repo; all destructive-shaped operations stay inside the python file per the PreToolUse guard constraint. Exact code:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Sandbox probe for pre-commit Function 4a root-CLAUDE.md gap (read-only on the repo).

Builds a disposable git repo in the scratchpad, installs (a) the CURRENT repo
pre-commit hook and (b) a PATCHED copy with the proposed PROTECTED_RE change,
then runs the block/override/regression/shrinkage/false-positive matrix.
The live repo at /home/user/LLMNA is never written."""
import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

REPO_HOOK = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/git/pre-commit"

OLD = r"""PROTECTED_RE='^(research/CLAUDE\.md|\.claude/settings\.json|research/INDEX\.md|research/meta/hooks/|research/meta/tools/|research/meta/methodology\.md|research/meta/biases-watchlist\.md|research/meta/session-prime\.md|research/predictions/lessons\.md|research/predictions/grading-log\.md|research/predictions/calibration-ledger|research/portfolio/)'"""

NEW = r"""# ^CLAUDE\.md: root boot-order pointer — governance §1b lists "CLAUDE.md" unqualified; the
# anchored list covered only research/CLAUDE.md, so root deletion passed (audit catch 2026-07-21;
# git-guard's bare CLAUDE\.md token already covered root — this restores pre-commit parity).
PROTECTED_RE='^(CLAUDE\.md|research/CLAUDE\.md|\.claude/settings\.json|research/INDEX\.md|research/meta/hooks/|research/meta/tools/|research/meta/methodology\.md|research/meta/biases-watchlist\.md|research/meta/session-prime\.md|research/predictions/lessons\.md|research/predictions/grading-log\.md|research/predictions/calibration-ledger|research/portfolio/)'"""


def sh(cwd, *args, env=None):
    e = dict(os.environ)
    e.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION", None)
    if env:
        e.update(env)
    return subprocess.run(args, cwd=cwd, env=e, capture_output=True, text=True)


def make_sandbox(hook_text):
    d = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="precommit-probe-", dir=os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
    sh(d, "git", "init", "-q")
    sh(d, "git", "config", "user.email", "probe@test")
    sh(d, "git", "config", "user.name", "probe")
    hooks = os.path.join(d, ".hooks")
    os.makedirs(hooks)
    hp = os.path.join(hooks, "pre-commit")
    with open(hp, "w") as f:
        f.write(hook_text)
    os.chmod(hp, 0o755)
    sh(d, "git", "config", "core.hooksPath", ".hooks")
    # seed tree mirroring the protected layout; root CLAUDE.md = 23 lines like the real one
    with open(os.path.join(d, "CLAUDE.md"), "w") as f:
        f.write("# boot pointer\n" * 23)
    os.makedirs(os.path.join(d, "research"))
    with open(os.path.join(d, "research", "CLAUDE.md"), "w") as f:
        f.write("# research OS\n" * 30)
    with open(os.path.join(d, "CLAUDE-notes.md"), "w") as f:
        f.write("scratch notes\n")
    with open(os.path.join(d, "unprotected.md"), "w") as f:
        f.write("ordinary file\n")
    sh(d, "git", "add", "-A")
    r = sh(d, "git", "commit", "-q", "-m", "seed")
    assert r.returncode == 0, "seed commit failed: " + r.stdout + r.stderr
    return d


def attempt(d, name, mutate, expect_block, env=None):
    base = sh(d, "git", "rev-parse", "HEAD").stdout.strip()
    mutate(d)
    sh(d, "git", "add", "-A")
    r = sh(d, "git", "commit", "-q", "-m", "probe: " + name, env=env)
    blocked = r.returncode != 0
    ok = blocked == expect_block
    verdict = "PASS" if ok else "FAIL"
    want = "BLOCK" if expect_block else "ALLOW"
    got = "BLOCK" if blocked else "ALLOW"
    print(f"  [{verdict}] {name}: expected {want}, got {got}")
    if not ok:
        print("    --- hook output ---")
        print("    " + (r.stdout + r.stderr).replace("\n", "\n    "))
    # roll the sandbox back to the pre-attempt commit so cases stay independent
    # (a successful commit, e.g. the token-override case, would otherwise move HEAD)
    sh(d, "git", "reset", "-q", "--hard", base)
    sh(d, "git", "clean", "-qfd")
    return ok


def rm_root_claude(d):
    os.remove(os.path.join(d, "CLAUDE.md"))


def rm_research_claude(d):
    os.remove(os.path.join(d, "research", "CLAUDE.md"))


def rm_notes(d):
    os.remove(os.path.join(d, "CLAUDE-notes.md"))


def rm_unprotected(d):
    os.remove(os.path.join(d, "unprotected.md"))


def shrink_root_claude(d):
    with open(os.path.join(d, "CLAUDE.md"), "w") as f:
        f.write("# boot pointer\n" * 5)  # 23 -> 5 lines, >40% shrink


def append_root_claude(d):
    with open(os.path.join(d, "CLAUDE.md"), "a") as f:
        f.write("# one more line\n")


def main():
    with open(REPO_HOOK) as f:
        current = f.read()
    results = []
    if OLD in current:
        # PRE-APPLY mode: demonstrate the gap live, then verify the in-memory patch.
        assert current.count(OLD) == 1, "old_string is not unique/byte-exact in pre-commit"
        patched = current.replace(OLD, NEW)
        print("== CURRENT hook (gap demonstration) ==")
        d1 = make_sandbox(current)
        results.append(attempt(d1, "A: delete root CLAUDE.md (unpatched)", rm_root_claude,
                               expect_block=False))  # the GAP: commit passes today
        results.append(attempt(d1, "B: delete research/CLAUDE.md (unpatched)", rm_research_claude,
                               expect_block=True))
        shutil.rmtree(d1)
    elif r"'^(CLAUDE\.md|research/CLAUDE\.md|" in current:
        # POST-APPLY mode: repo hook already carries the fix — verify it as-is.
        print("== repo hook already patched; running patched matrix only ==")
        patched = current
    else:
        print("ERROR: pre-commit PROTECTED_RE matches neither known state — inspect manually")
        sys.exit(1)

    print("== PATCHED hook (fix verification) ==")
    d2 = make_sandbox(patched)
    results.append(attempt(d2, "C: delete root CLAUDE.md", rm_root_claude, expect_block=True))
    results.append(attempt(d2, "D: delete root CLAUDE.md + operator token", rm_root_claude,
                           expect_block=False, env={"OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION": "1"}))
    results.append(attempt(d2, "E: delete research/CLAUDE.md (regression)", rm_research_claude,
                           expect_block=True))
    results.append(attempt(d2, "F: delete CLAUDE-notes.md (FP check, ^-anchor)", rm_notes,
                           expect_block=False))
    results.append(attempt(d2, "G: delete unprotected.md (regression)", rm_unprotected,
                           expect_block=False))
    results.append(attempt(d2, "H: shrink root CLAUDE.md 23->5 lines (Function 4c now armed)",
                           shrink_root_claude, expect_block=True))
    results.append(attempt(d2, "I: append a line to root CLAUDE.md (no shrink FP)",
                           append_root_claude, expect_block=False))
    shutil.rmtree(d2)

    n_ok = sum(results)
    print(f"== {n_ok}/{len(results)} cases as expected ==")
    sys.exit(0 if n_ok == len(results) else 1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Selftest — fixtures are the verified attack/control matrix: the K3 13-bypass
# set (2026-07-19 adjudication, all closed in v2), the rest of the v2 coverage
# surface, the G-23..G-26 regression classes (2026-07-20 — patched with
# external-script verification only; these are the embedded fixtures that
# patch lacked), and the no-false-positive controls. Hermetic: log_line +
# ensure_hooks_path stubbed and operator tokens cleared before any fixture
# runs — no fire-log writes, no git-config mutation. Run: --selftest
# NOT fixtured (known-open defects, tracked separately — append fixtures here
# with their fixes): `git restore .` (dead `\.(\s|$)` alternative) and rm of
# an unexpanded $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR (G-24 fix absent from the rm tail regex).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SELFTEST = [
    # (name, Bash command, expect_block) — K3 v1 13-bypass matrix
    ("K3 E3 -c hooksPath override", "git -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null commit -m 'x'", True),
    ("K3 E3 config hooksPath override", "git config core.hooksPath /dev/null", True),
    ("K3 E5 +refspec force-push", "git push origin +main", True),
    ("K3 E4 interpreter rmtree", "python3 -c \"import shutil; shutil.rmtree('research')\"", True),
    ("K3 E4 find -delete", "find research -delete", True),
    ("K3 E4 pipe xargs rm", "find research -name '*.md' | xargs rm", True),
    ("K3 E4 truncate -s0", "truncate -s0 research/CLAUDE.md", True),
    ("K3 E2 reset --hard", "git reset --hard HEAD~3", True),
    ("K3 E2 clean -fdx", "git clean -fdx", True),
    ("K3 E2 reflog expire + gc prune", "git reflog expire --expire=now --all && git gc --prune=now", True),
    ("K3 E2 branch -D", "git branch -D main", True),
    ("K3 E2 update-ref -d", "git update-ref -d refs/heads/main", True),
    ("K3 E7 mv .git out", "mv .git /tmp/gone", True),
    # v1's one original catch + the rest of the v2 coverage surface
    ("v1 original catch: short-flag rm", "rm -rf research/", True),
    ("commit --no-verify", "git commit --no-verify -m 'x'", True),
    ("push --force", "git push --force origin main", True),
    ("push --force-with-lease", "git push --force-with-lease origin main", True),
    ("push -f", "git push -f origin main", True),
    ("push --delete remote ref", "git push origin --delete feature", True),
    ("push :ref deletion", "git push origin :feature", True),
    ("config --unset hooksPath", "git config --unset core.hooksPath", True),
    ("stash drop", "git stash drop", True),
    ("checkout -f", "git checkout -f main", True),
    ("worktree remove --force", "git worktree remove --force ../wt", True),
    ("filter-branch", "git filter-branch --tree-filter x HEAD", True),
    ("restore --staged (07-20 add)", "git restore --staged research/", True),
    ("dd of= repo file", "dd if=/dev/zero of=research/CLAUDE.md", True),
    # G-23..G-26 regression classes (patched 2026-07-20)
    ("G-23 long-flag rm", "rm --recursive --force research/", True),
    ("G-24 find bare-dot -delete", "find . -delete", True),
    ("G-24 rmtree bare-dot", "python3 -c \"import shutil; shutil.rmtree('.')\"", True),
    ("G-25 lowercase hookspath", "git config core.hookspath /tmp/nohooks", True),
    ("G-26 redirect onto CLAUDE.md", "echo x > research/CLAUDE.md", True),
    ("G-26 redirect onto grading-log", "echo x > research/predictions/grading-log.md", True),
    # no-false-positive controls (the 07-19 "18 no-FP" + 07-20 "9/9 benign" classes)
    ("PASS normal add+commit+push", "git add -A && git commit -m 'thesis update' && git push", False),
    ("PASS -m strip: message describing blocked cmds",
     "git commit -m 'guard: block rm -rf research + git push --force'", False),

def _selftest_exit(payload: str) -> int:
    import io
    old_in, old_err = sys.stdin, sys.stderr
    sys.stdin, sys.stderr = io.StringIO(payload), io.StringIO()
    try:
        main()
    except SystemExit as e:
        return int(e.code or 0)
    finally:
        sys.stdin, sys.stderr = old_in, old_err
    return 0


def selftest():
    global log_line, ensure_hooks_path
    log_line = lambda msg: None       # fixtures must not pollute the fire log
    ensure_hooks_path = lambda: None  # ...or touch the live git config
    for var in ("OPERATOR_APPROVED_FORCE_PUSH", "OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION"):
        os.environ.pop(var, None)     # a live token would flip every block fixture
    checks = [(name, json.dumps({"tool_name": "Bash", "tool_input": {"command": cmd}}),
               2 if want_block else 0) for name, cmd, want_block in SELFTEST]
    checks += [  # exit-path edges
        ("non-Bash tool exits 0", json.dumps({"tool_name": "Edit", "tool_input": {}}), 0),
        ("empty command exits 0", json.dumps({"tool_name": "Bash", "tool_input": {}}), 0),
        ("malformed stdin exits 0", "not json{", 0),
    ]
    failures = []
    for name, payload, want in checks:
        got = _selftest_exit(payload)
        if got != want:
            failures.append(f"FAIL {name}: expected exit {want}, got {got}")
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{len(checks)} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(checks)}/{len(checks)} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        selftest()  # exits 0/1 itself — deliberately OUTSIDE the fail-open wrapper
    try:
        main()
patches.rationale: Embeds the house-standard --selftest (session-prime-cascade precedent) into the newest safety-critical hook, closing the GAP that let the 07-20 regex patches ship with external-script verification only. Fixture set = the verified attack/control matrix: K3's 13 live bypasses (07-19 adjudication), v1's one original catch, the remaining v2 coverage surface (no-verify, 4 force-push forms, --unset, stash/checkout/worktree/filter-branch/restore/dd), the 5 G-23..G-26 regression classes, and 12 no-false-positive controls incl. the -m strip and both operator-token exemptions, plus 3 exit-path edges (non-Bash tool, empty command, malformed stdin). Hermetic by construction: log_line + ensure_hooks_path rebound to no-ops and operator tokens cleared before any fixture runs — proven zero fire-log growth (900/900 lines) across a full 48-check run. Runtime path is untouched: argv-less invocation (how settings.json calls it) never reaches selftest(); verified benign payload exit 0/no output and block payload exit 2 with identical stderr prefix on the patched copy. The two KNOWN-OPEN regressions this audit confirmed (git restore . dead alternative — exit 0 today; rm of unexpanded $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR — G-24 non-application to the rm tail regex) are deliberately NOT fixtured (they would fail green-at-ship) and are named in the header comment as the append-with-fix obligations for their own DEFECT patches. All 48 expected outcomes were probe-verified against the CURRENT code before writing the table — the selftest ships green and any future regex edit that regresses a covered class now fails deterministically on demand instead of silently.

It is state-aware so it serves as BOTH the pre-patch defect demonstration AND the post-patch acceptance test (scratchpad is session-scoped — recreate from this exact code if needed):

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""G-55 patch probe (read-only on repo; all writes under scratchpad//tmp)."""
import os, subprocess, sys, shutil

REPO_SETUP = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/tools/setup.sh"
SCRATCH = "/tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/g55"
ALT = os.path.join(SCRATCH, "altclone")  # deliberately NOT /home/user/LLMNA
OUTFILE = "/tmp/llmna-boot-status.txt"

PATCHES = [
    ("OUT=/tmp/llmna-boot-status.txt\n{\n",
     "OUT=/tmp/llmna-boot-status.txt\n"
     "# Repo root resolved from this script's own location (G-55 fix): setup.sh lives at\n"
     "# <root>/research/meta/tools/, so root = three dirs up. CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is NOT\n"
     "# available here (setup runs before Claude Code launches) — script-relative is the\n"
     "# only correct form. The setup-field's absolute invocation path only locates the\n"
     "# script; everything below works wherever the clone landed.\n"
     "ROOT=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"$0\")/../../..\" 2>/dev/null && pwd)\"\n"
     "{\n"),
    ("  cd /home/user/LLMNA 2>/dev/null && echo \"HEAD:",
     "  cd \"$ROOT\" 2>/dev/null && echo \"HEAD:"),
    ("cd /home/user/LLMNA 2>/dev/null && git config core.hooksPath research/meta/hooks/git || true",
     "cd \"$ROOT\" 2>/dev/null && git config core.hooksPath research/meta/hooks/git || true"),
]

def run(cmd, **kw):
    return subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, **kw)

def main():
    src = open(REPO_SETUP).read()
    already_patched = 'ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../.."' in src

    if not already_patched:
        for old, _ in PATCHES:
            n = src.count(old)
            assert n == 1, f"old_string not unique (count={n}): {old!r}"
        print("PASS 1: all 3 old_strings byte-exact and unique in live setup.sh")
    else:
        assert "/home/user/LLMNA" not in src.split("\n", 8)[-1], \
            "patched file still contains hardcoded path outside header comment"
        print("PASS 1: live setup.sh already patched (ROOT resolution present, "
              "no hardcoded path outside the setup-field doc comment)")

    if os.path.exists(SCRATCH):
        shutil.rmtree(SCRATCH)
    os.makedirs(os.path.join(ALT, "research/meta/tools"))
    os.makedirs(os.path.join(ALT, "research/meta/hooks/git"))

    env = dict(os.environ, GIT_AUTHOR_NAME="probe", GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL="p@x",
               GIT_COMMITTER_NAME="probe", GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL="p@x")
    assert run(["git", "init", "-q", ALT]).returncode == 0
    open(os.path.join(ALT, "f.txt"), "w").write("x\n")
    assert run(["git", "-C", ALT, "add", "f.txt"]).returncode == 0
    assert run(["git", "-C", ALT, "commit", "-q", "-m", "init"], env=env).returncode == 0

    if not already_patched:
        neg = src.replace("/home/user/LLMNA", "/nonexistent/LLMNA-g55-probe")
        neg_path = os.path.join(ALT, "research/meta/tools/setup-neg.sh")
        open(neg_path, "w").write(neg)
        r = run(["bash", neg_path], cwd="/tmp")
        assert r.returncode == 0, f"neg control should still exit 0 (fail-open), got {r.returncode}"
        boot = open(OUTFILE).read()
        hp = run(["git", "-C", ALT, "config", "core.hooksPath"])
        assert "HEAD: " not in boot.replace("HEAD: (", "HEAD@GONE("), \
            "neg control unexpectedly produced a HEAD line"
        assert hp.returncode != 0 and hp.stdout.strip() == "", \
            f"neg control unexpectedly set hooksPath: {hp.stdout!r}"
        print("PASS 2: NEGATIVE CONTROL — clone-elsewhere: exit 0, NO HEAD report, "
              "hooksPath NOT activated (silent no-op confirmed = G-55 defect)")
    else:
        print("PASS 2: (skipped — defect form no longer present in live file)")

    fixed = src
    if not already_patched:
        for old, new in PATCHES:
            fixed = fixed.replace(old, new)
    fix_path = os.path.join(ALT, "research/meta/tools/setup.sh")
    open(fix_path, "w").write(fixed)
    r = run(["bash", fix_path], cwd="/tmp")
    assert r.returncode == 0, f"patched script exit {r.returncode}: {r.stderr}"
    boot = open(OUTFILE).read()
    head = run(["git", "-C", ALT, "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"]).stdout.strip()
    assert f"HEAD: {head}" in boot, f"patched boot report missing 'HEAD: {head}':\n{boot}"
    hp = run(["git", "-C", ALT, "config", "core.hooksPath"])
    assert hp.stdout.strip() == "research/meta/hooks/git", \
        f"patched script did not activate hooksPath: {hp.stdout!r}"
    print(f"PASS 3: FIX — alt-clone at {ALT}: HEAD {head} reported, "
          "core.hooksPath activated to research/meta/hooks/git")
    print("ALL PROBES PASS")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

#   append `# secretscan-ok` to the flagged line.

# Fire telemetry (added 2026-07-21): every BLOCK below appends one line to meta/hook-fire-log.md,
# the git-layer adoption of the PreToolUse git-guard's log_line() convention. Without it the
# governance falsifier ("Function-4 firing ONLY on false positives over 60 days -> loosen list";
# re-eval 2026-10-19, destructive-change-governance.md) is unmeasurable from repo data — a
# refused commit leaves no persistent record. Clean passes are NOT logged (no per-commit noise).
# A blocked commit leaves the tree dirty anyway, so the Stop git-check sweeps the log line into
# the next commit. Fail-open: logging failure must never itself block (or allow) a commit.
FIRELOG="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)/research/meta/hook-fire-log.md"
log_fire() {
  { echo "- $(date -u +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%SZ') git-pre-commit BLOCK ($1)" >> "$FIRELOG"; } 2>/dev/null || true
}

echo "=== pre-commit: staged changes (diff-before-commit rule, 2026-07-16) ==="
patches.rationale: Adds the fail-open telemetry helper mirroring git-guard-pretooluse.py log_line(): identical timestamp format (date -u %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%SZ matches the guard's strftime), identical '- {ts} {hook} BLOCK (reason)' line shape, identical never-break-the-hook posture ({...} 2>/dev/null || true — verified fail-open by probe T8 with the log path replaced by a directory). git rev-parse --show-toplevel makes the log path cwd-independent (git runs hooks from the toplevel, but this survives any future invocation context; on failure FIRELOG resolves to an unwritable path and the append silently no-ops). Clean passes are deliberately unlogged — matches session-prime-cascade-hook v2's 'clean passes unlogged' convention and avoids a dirty tree on every successful commit (which would fight the commit-msg claims-guard and the Stop git-check on every turn).

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""G-firelog probe: behavior tests for the fire-telemetry patch, run against a patched copy
of pre-commit inside a throwaway git repo (hook installed via .git/hooks — no hooksPath, no
repo mutation). All git commands live inside this file per the safety protocol."""
import os, shutil, subprocess, sys, tempfile

HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
# default = patched scratch copy; pass a path (e.g. the real repo hook, post-apply) to re-verify
HOOK = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else os.path.join(HERE, "pre-commit.firelog.patched")
repo = tempfile.mkdtemp(dir=HERE, prefix="firelog-scratchrepo-")
LOG = os.path.join(repo, "research", "meta", "hook-fire-log.md")

def run(*args, env_extra=None):
    env = dict(os.environ)
    env.pop("OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION", None)
    if env_extra:
        env.update(env_extra)
    return subprocess.run(list(args), cwd=repo, env=env, capture_output=True, text=True)

def git(*args, **kw):
    return run("git", "-c", "user.email=probe@test", "-c", "user.name=probe", *args, **kw)

def log_lines():
    return [l for l in open(LOG, encoding="utf-8").read().splitlines() if "git-pre-commit BLOCK" in l]

def w(path, content):
    p = os.path.join(repo, path)
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(p), exist_ok=True)
    open(p, "w", encoding="utf-8").write(content)

results = []
def check(name, cond, detail=""):
    results.append((name, bool(cond), detail))
    print(f"{'PASS' if cond else 'FAIL'}  {name}  {detail}")

r = subprocess.run(["bash", "-n", HOOK], capture_output=True, text=True)
check("T0 bash -n syntax", r.returncode == 0, r.stderr.strip())

git("init", "-q", ".")
shutil.copy(HOOK, os.path.join(repo, ".git", "hooks", "pre-commit"))
os.chmod(os.path.join(repo, ".git", "hooks", "pre-commit"), 0o755)
w("research/meta/hook-fire-log.md", "# Hook fire log (probe copy)\n---\n")
w("README.md", "probe\n")
w("research/CLAUDE.md", "\n".join(f"line {i}" for i in range(25)) + "\n")
for i in range(1, 5):
    w(f"d{i}.txt", f"dummy {i}\n")

git("add", "-A")
r = git("commit", "-q", "-m", "probe: initial")
check("T1 clean commit passes, no log line", r.returncode == 0 and len(log_lines()) == 0,
      f"rc={r.returncode} lines={len(log_lines())}")

w("leak.py", 'API_KEY = "abc123def456ghi789jkl"\n')
git("add", "leak.py")
r = git("commit", "-q", "-m", "probe: leak")
ll = log_lines()
check("T2 secrets BLOCK logged", r.returncode != 0 and len(ll) == 1 and "secrets-scan" in ll[-1]
      and "abc123" not in ll[-1], f"rc={r.returncode} line={ll[-1] if ll else None}")
git("reset", "-q", "HEAD", "leak.py"); os.remove(os.path.join(repo, "leak.py"))

w("research/meta/session-prime.md", "a" * 30001)
git("add", "research/meta/session-prime.md")
r = git("commit", "-q", "-m", "probe: cap")
ll = log_lines()
check("T3 cap BLOCK logged", r.returncode != 0 and len(ll) == 2 and "session-prime cap: 30001 chars" in ll[-1],
      f"rc={r.returncode} line={ll[-1] if ll else None}")
git("reset", "-q", "HEAD", "research/meta/session-prime.md")
os.remove(os.path.join(repo, "research", "meta", "session-prime.md"))

git("rm", "-q", "research/CLAUDE.md")
r = git("commit", "-q", "-m", "probe: del protected")
ll = log_lines()
check("T4 protected-deletion BLOCK logged", r.returncode != 0 and len(ll) == 3
      and "protected-path deletion" in ll[-1] and "research/CLAUDE.md" in ll[-1],
      f"rc={r.returncode} line={ll[-1] if ll else None}")
git("checkout", "-q", "HEAD", "--", "research/CLAUDE.md")

git("rm", "-q", "d1.txt", "d2.txt", "d3.txt", "d4.txt")
r = git("commit", "-q", "-m", "probe: mass del")
ll = log_lines()
check("T5 mass-deletion BLOCK logged", r.returncode != 0 and len(ll) == 4
      and "mass deletion: 4 files" in ll[-1], f"rc={r.returncode} line={ll[-1] if ll else None}")
git("checkout", "-q", "HEAD", "--", "d1.txt", "d2.txt", "d3.txt", "d4.txt")

w("research/CLAUDE.md", "line 0\nline 1\nline 2\n")
git("add", "research/CLAUDE.md")
r = git("commit", "-q", "-m", "probe: shrink")
ll = log_lines()
check("T6 shrinkage BLOCK logged", r.returncode != 0 and len(ll) == 5
      and "shrinkage: research/CLAUDE.md 25->3 lines" in ll[-1],
      f"rc={r.returncode} line={ll[-1] if ll else None}")
git("checkout", "-q", "HEAD", "--", "research/CLAUDE.md")

git("rm", "-q", "d1.txt", "d2.txt", "d3.txt", "d4.txt")
r = git("commit", "-q", "-m", "probe: approved mass del", env_extra={"OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION": "1"})
ll = log_lines()
check("T7 operator-token commit passes, no new log line", r.returncode == 0 and len(ll) == 5,
      f"rc={r.returncode} lines={len(ll)}")

# fail-open: chmod is no barrier to root, so force a real append failure (log path -> directory)
saved = open(LOG, encoding="utf-8").read()
os.remove(LOG); os.mkdir(LOG)
w("leak2.py", 'API_TOKEN = "zzz999yyy888xxx777www"\n')
git("add", "leak2.py")
r = git("commit", "-q", "-m", "probe: leak2")
blocked_ok = r.returncode != 0 and "BLOCKED" in (r.stdout + r.stderr)
no_noise = "Is a directory" not in (r.stdout + r.stderr)
os.rmdir(LOG)
open(LOG, "w", encoding="utf-8").write(saved)
check("T8 fail-open on unwritable log (block preserved, no crash, no stderr noise)",
      blocked_ok and no_noise and len(log_lines()) == 5,
      f"rc={r.returncode} lines={len(log_lines())}")
git("reset", "-q", "HEAD", "leak2.py"); os.remove(os.path.join(repo, "leak2.py"))

import re
fmt = re.compile(r"^- \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}Z git-pre-commit BLOCK \(.+\)$")
check("T9 all lines match house format", all(fmt.match(l) for l in log_lines()),
      str(log_lines()[:2]))

shutil.rmtree(repo, ignore_errors=True)
fails = [n for n, ok, _ in results if not ok]
print(f"\n{len(results) - len(fails)}/{len(results)} passed" + (f"; FAILED: {fails}" if fails else ""))
sys.exit(1 if fails else 0)

patches.rationale: Confirmed GAP (COSMETIC): banner said 16; live wiring is 19 scripts / 20 entries per the 07-20 K3-Swarm G-01 recount, and the sync sweep missed this deprecated file. Probe-verified the technically exact double-fire population is 17, not 16 and not 19: the legacy mirror research/meta/hooks/settings.json wires 18 script entries, of which 17 overlap /home/user/LLMNA/.claude/settings.json (2 SessionStart + 1 UserPromptSubmit + 14 Stop); stop-hook-git-check.sh is mirror-only (would newly single-fire) and git-guard-pretooluse.py + session-prime-cascade-hook.py are project-only (unaffected). The fix (a) states the structurally-true claim (every dual-wired hook doubles) that stays correct under future wiring drift, (b) gives today's exact overlap with the house-style recount date tag, and (c) points at DURABLE-ACTIVATION.md as the canonical count home so the next sync sweep need not touch this non-canonical file — killing the recurring stale-count class here (this number rotted twice). Comment-only change inside an existing # block: zero execution-path risk; the abort guard's stderr at line 57 is already count-free and needs no companion edit.

The runnable verification fixture is the standalone read-only probe already created and executed at /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/overlap_check.py — it loads research/meta/hooks/settings.json (legacy mirror) and .claude/settings.json (live), keys wired hooks by (event, script basename), skips the non-script hooksPath one-liner, and prints the double-fire overlap set plus mirror-only/project-only remainders. If future wiring changes make this probe print a number other than 17, the banner's dated parenthetical is stale again — but its structural claim ('every hook wired in both files') remains true, and the canonical tally pointer (DURABLE-ACTIVATION.md) is the file the sync sweep must update. patches.new_string:
**Plus environment-level (system-managed, NOT in project settings):** `~/.claude/stop-hook-git-check.sh` — requires commit+push before Stop completes, plus commit-signature/committer-email verification. The copy of it in this directory is a dated SNAPSHOT for visibility; upstream is the system-managed file.

## Git-level hooks (`git/` subdirectory, layer live since 2026-07-16)

A second deterministic layer at the git gates. Version-controlled at `research/meta/hooks/git/`; activated by `git config core.hooksPath research/meta/hooks/git` (the SessionStart one-liner above; `meta/tools/setup.sh` and the PreToolUse guard also re-arm it). Governance matrix: `research/meta/destructive-change-governance.md`. Honest limit: `--no-verify` skips these — the PreToolUse guard blocks that flag, which is why both layers exist.

| Hook | Enforces |
|---|---|
| `pre-commit` | Diff-before-commit printout (binding review, 2026-07-16); secrets scan on added lines (NEVER-ECHO; `# secretscan-ok` override); `session-prime.md` 30,000-char cap gate; destructive-change guard — protected-path deletion, >3-file deletion, >40% shrink of a protected file (override: `OPERATOR_APPROVED_DELETION=1`). |
| `commit-msg` | Claims guard — blocks a commit whose staged diff is ONLY `hook-fire-log.md` while the message claims substantive work (silent-write-failure signature; say "telemetry"). |
| `pre-push` | CATASTROPHIC tier — blocks remote-ref deletion and non-fast-forward (history-rewrite) pushes; fail-closed when the remote SHA is unknown locally (fetch first); override: `OPERATOR_APPROVED_FORCE_PUSH=1` after verbatim operator instruction. |

**Hook conventions (all Stop hooks):**
patches.rationale: The entire git-level enforcement layer (pre-commit 07-16/17/19, commit-msg 07-18, pre-push 07-19) lives under this directory but appeared nowhere in the hook-docs entry point — the newest enforcement layer was invisible to a first-read auditor. Section placed after the environment-level note (same 'other layers' altitude), with the governance pointer and the --no-verify honest-limit note lifted from the pre-push header's own instructions-vs-hooks doctrine. Each row condensed from the respective hook's header comments, including override tokens and the pre-push fail-closed behavior.
