patches.rationale: Adds the two parsing helpers plus embedded --selftest fixtures. _paths_from_porcelain_line splits 'R  old -> new' rename/copy porcelain lines into both paths (old side is nonexistent on disk and drops out via the existing os.path.exists guard in extract_named_tickers — zero FP risk; new side gets enforced, closing the confirmed silent exit-0 on staged renames). _unquote_git_path strips git's C-style double-quoting (space/special-char paths) that made paths fail the ^research/ regex anchor and get silently skipped. Rename-split is gated on R/C in the XY status columns so an ordinary path containing ' -> ' is never mis-split. Fixtures/selftest follow the house pattern (anti-fabrication-hook.py embedded --selftest).

Routes porcelain lines through the new parser. This is the confirmed defect site (lines 65-68): 'R  old -> new' previously entered files as one combined string that matched *-comparison.md$ but failed open(), and quoted paths kept their surrounding double-quotes and failed the ^research/ anchor — both silent enforcement skips. Sandbox-verified: staged-rename case flips exit 0 -> exit 2, space-in-name case flips exit 0 -> exit 2, cascade-complete negative control stays exit 0.

Same silent-skip class in the same function's second leg: git diff-tree --name-only C-quotes special-char paths too (core.quotePath), so a committed non-ASCII artifact path would keep its quotes and silently miss the artifact regexes. One-line application of the shared helper; no-op for ordinary unquoted lines.

patches.rationale: Confirmed via sandbox probe 2026-07-21: with a synthesis artifact created inside a not-yet-tracked directory, `git status --porcelain` reports only '?? research/meta/' (collapsed, verified output), which matches no SYNTHESIS_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS regex → files_in_current_changeset() omits the artifact → hook exits 0 and the Critical Rule #10 cascade check is silently skipped until the file is staged/committed (docstring lines 10-16 promise working-tree coverage; the HEAD window only catches it one commit later). Adding -uall makes porcelain enumerate untracked files individually ('?? research/meta/2026-07-21-thesis-comparison.md'), which the existing 'XY <path>' parser at line 67-68 handles unchanged — same two-char-status + space format, no parser edit needed. Ignored files stay excluded (-uall only expands untracked, non-ignored). No new false-positive class: probe case D shows a completed cascade in the same untracked-dir state still exits 0, and the staged/HEAD windows are untouched. old_string includes the enclosing 'try:' so the new comment lands inside the try block; the porcelain arg line makes the block unique in this file (verified count==1). NOTE for the ledger, not this patch: research/meta/hooks/signal-ingest-cascade-hook.py:218 runs the identical `git status --porcelain` call and inherits the same collapse defect — flag as a sibling finding rather than silently widening this diff.

designs.fixtures:
# New file: research/meta/tests/test_cascade_untracked_dir.py
# (cascade-enforcement-hook.py has no embedded --selftest to extend, so the
# fixture follows the existing external-test precedent of
# research/meta/tests/test_framework_codifications.py: standalone script,
# PASS/FAIL lines, exit 0/1. Validated in-session: 4/4 green against a
# patched copy of the hook, 2/4 red against the live unpatched hook — the
# two untracked-dir checks are the ones that flip.)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Regression test: cascade-enforcement-hook must see synthesis artifacts
created inside a NOT-yet-tracked directory (untracked-dir collapse fix,
2026-07-21 — `git status --porcelain -uall`).

Without -uall, porcelain reports a new untracked directory as a single
'?? dir/' entry; a synthesis artifact inside it matches no artifact
pattern and the cascade check silently passes until the file is staged
or committed. Critical Rule #10 requires detection regardless of
whether the parent directory is newly created.

Builds a throwaway git sandbox (never touches the real repo) and runs
the live hook against it via CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR.

Exit code: 0 → all assertions passed; 1 → at least one failed.

Usage:
  python3 research/meta/tests/test_cascade_untracked_dir.py
"""

import os
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
HOOK = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else str(
    REPO_ROOT / "research" / "meta" / "hooks" / "cascade-enforcement-hook.py"
)

GIT_ENV = dict(os.environ)
GIT_ENV.update({
    "GIT_AUTHOR_NAME": "test", "GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL": "t@t",
    "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME": "test", "GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL": "t@t",
})


def write(root, rel, content):
    p = os.path.join(root, rel)
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(p), exist_ok=True)
    with open(p, "w") as f:
        f.write(content)


def git(root, *args):
    subprocess.run(["git", "-C", root, *args], check=True,
                   capture_output=True, env=GIT_ENV)


def build_sandbox(root):
    """Tracked baseline WITHOUT research/meta/, so the artifact's parent
    directory is itself untracked — the collapse precondition."""
    os.makedirs(root, exist_ok=True)
    git(root, "init", "-q")
    write(root, "research/companies/NVDA/thesis.md",
          "# NVDA thesis\nNo back-references yet.\n")
    write(root, "README.md", "sandbox\n")
    git(root, "add", "-A")
    git(root, "commit", "-q", "-m", "baseline")
    write(root, "research/meta/2026-07-21-thesis-comparison.md",
          "# Comparison\n\n| **NVDA** | vs field |\n")


def run_hook(root):
    env = dict(GIT_ENV)
    env["CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"] = root
    r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, HOOK], cwd=root, input="{}",
                       capture_output=True, text=True, env=env)
    return r.returncode, r.stderr


def main():
    checks = []
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
        root = os.path.join(tmp, "sandbox")
        build_sandbox(root)

        # 1. Artifact inside an untracked directory MUST block (exit 2).
        rc, err = run_hook(root)
        checks.append(("untracked-dir artifact detected (exit 2)", rc == 2))
        checks.append(("stderr names the missing thesis file",
                       "research/companies/NVDA/thesis.md" in err))

        # 2. Same state, cascade completed → must pass (exit 0).
        with open(os.path.join(root, "research/companies/NVDA/thesis.md"),
                  "a") as f:
            f.write("\n## Cross-source synthesis\n"
                    "See 2026-07-21-thesis-comparison.md — implication.\n")
        rc, _ = run_hook(root)
        checks.append(("cascade complete passes (exit 0)", rc == 0))

        # 3. Staged path still detected (pre-existing behavior preserved).
        with open(os.path.join(root, "research/companies/NVDA/thesis.md"),
                  "w") as f:
            f.write("# NVDA thesis\nNo back-references yet.\n")
        git(root, "add", "-A")
        rc, _ = run_hook(root)
        checks.append(("staged artifact still detected (exit 2)", rc == 2))

    failed = 0
    for name, ok in checks:
        print(f"{'PASS' if ok else 'FAIL'}  {name}")
        failed += 0 if ok else 1
    print(f"\n{len(checks) - failed}/{len(checks)} passed")
    sys.exit(1 if failed else 0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

patches.rationale: GAP fix: docstring lines 10-11 and CLAUDE.md Critical Rule #10 both name 'source-reliability update' as an enforced synthesis-artifact class, but research/meta/source-reliability.md (which exists and substantively references companies/MURATA/thesis.md at line 166 and companies/VICR/facts.md at line 232) matched none of the 7 patterns — source-reliability updates never triggered cascade enforcement. New pattern is exact-path anchored (probe-verified: matches only research/meta/source-reliability.md; trust-map.md and nested foo/source-reliability.md do NOT match), so zero new false-positive surface. Same patch drops the r"^research/meta/.*-thesis-comparison\.md$" line: probe-verified strict subset of pattern 1 (both ^/$-anchored; every -thesis-comparison.md path ends in -comparison.md), so removal is behavior-neutral — kept in this patch because leaving it invites future editors to treat thesis-comparisons as a separately-tunable class. Comment style matches the existing dated wiki-pattern comment at lines 91-92. OPERATIONAL NOTE (intended enforcement, flag in the commit message): live-corpus probe shows MURATA and VICR theses currently lack back-references to source-reliability.md, so the hook will exit 2 the next time that file enters a changeset — that is the latent Rule #10 debt this class was supposed to catch; the fire completes the cascade at next touch, it does not fire retroactively (file is not in the current changeset, probe-verified changeset membership = False).

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Selftest — pattern-classification fixtures (added 2026-07-21 with the
# source-reliability pattern fix). Run: --selftest
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SELFTEST = [
    # (path, expected is_synthesis_artifact)
    ("research/meta/patel-vs-aschenbrenner-thesis-comparison.md", True),
    ("research/meta/source-reliability.md", True),  # 2026-07-21 gap fix
    ("research/meta/trust-map.md", False),
    ("research/meta/foo/source-reliability.md", False),  # exact path only
    ("research/signals/events/2026-05-16-13f.md", True),
    ("research/signals/triangulation.md", True),
    ("research/sector/foo-comparison.md", True),
    ("research/wiki/memory-cycle-primer.md", True),
    ("research/wiki/foo-scaling.md", True),
    ("research/companies/NVDA/thesis.md", False),
    ("research/signals/cross-source-log.md", False),
]


def selftest():
    failures = []
    for path, expected in SELFTEST:
        got = is_synthesis_artifact(path)
        if got != expected:
            failures.append(f"FAIL {path}: expected {expected}, got {got}")
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(SELFTEST)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        selftest()
    main()
patches.rationale: The hook had NO embedded selftest and research/meta/tests/test_framework_codifications.py does not cover it, so the pattern change would otherwise ship untested (house standard: embedded --selftest with fixtures where present; every behavior change needs a fixture). Structure mirrors the house precedent in session-prime-cascade-hook.py v2 (SELFTEST fixture list + selftest() + __main__ dispatch, lines 247-355 there). Fixture 1 proves the redundancy removal did not regress -thesis-comparison classification; fixture 2 pins the gap fix (fails on the unpatched pattern list, so the fixture is load-bearing, not decorative); fixture 4 pins the exact-path anchor against over-match. --selftest exits before main(), so wired Stop-hook invocations (no argv) are unaffected.

patches.rationale: The defect fix. Both character classes widen to [A-Z0-9][A-Z0-9_-]+ (hyphen + digit-leading support) and the bold pattern's {2,8} cap is dropped (2-char minimum preserved via first-char + '+'). Probe-verified against the live 90-folder companies/ dir: old patterns covered 89/90 names across both shapes combined (MITSUI-KINZOKU matchable by NEITHER — cascade structurally unenforceable; LGINNOTEK/PAGOODDOCTOR/SEMCOTECH/UNITEDIMAGING invisible to bold refs); new patterns cover 90/90 on BOTH shapes and are a strict superset of old extraction (zero regressions). Widening is FP-safe by construction: the pre-existing '& valid_tickers' intersection is the guard — 16 raw bold matches on house-shorthand noise (**TC-13**, **B45**, **P-1**, **13F**, **2026-07-21**, **HOLD**, **SNOWFLAKE**) all die at the intersection, 0 false tickers. The two-line pattern body is factored into _extract_from_text so the new embedded selftest exercises the shipped code path instead of duplicating regexes that could drift; extract_named_tickers keeps its exact signature and file-I/O fail-open behavior.

patches.new_string:
def selftest():
    """Deterministic fixtures for _extract_from_text (added 2026-07-21 with
    the pattern widening). Run: python3 cascade-enforcement-hook.py --selftest
    Exit 0 = all fixtures pass; exit 1 = fixture failure."""
    valid = {"NVDA", "AVGO", "MU", "MITSUI", "MITSUI-KINZOKU", "LGINNOTEK",
             "PAGOODDOCTOR", "SEMCOTECH", "UNITEDIMAGING"}
    cases = [
        # (name, artifact text, expected extraction)
        ("path ref, plain",
         "see companies/NVDA/thesis.md", {"NVDA"}),
        ("path ref, hyphenated (pre-fix: escaped both patterns)",
         "cascade to companies/MITSUI-KINZOKU/thesis.md", {"MITSUI-KINZOKU"}),
        ("bold ref, plain table row",
         "| **AVGO** | beneficiary | 1st |", {"AVGO"}),
        ("bold ref, 2-char minimum preserved",
         "**MU** capacity", {"MU"}),
        ("bold refs, 9-13 chars (pre-fix: dropped by {2,8} cap)",
         "**LGINNOTEK** **PAGOODDOCTOR** **SEMCOTECH** **UNITEDIMAGING**",
         {"LGINNOTEK", "PAGOODDOCTOR", "SEMCOTECH", "UNITEDIMAGING"}),
        ("bold hyphenated + prefix-ticker disambiguation",
         "**MITSUI-KINZOKU** vs **MITSUI**", {"MITSUI-KINZOKU", "MITSUI"}),
        ("house shorthand / dates / non-tickers never survive intersection",
         "**TC-13** **B45** **P-1** **13F** **2026-07-21** **HOLD** "
         "**SNOWFLAKE** companies/NVDA-old/", set()),
    ]
    failed = 0
    for name, text, expected in cases:
        got = _extract_from_text(text, valid)
        ok = got == expected
        failed += 0 if ok else 1
        print(f"  [{'ok' if ok else 'FAIL'}] {name}: got {sorted(got)}")
    if failed:
        print(f"SELFTEST: {failed}/{len(cases)} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"SELFTEST: {len(cases)}/{len(cases)} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)

patches.old_string:
# Anti-pattern triggers. Each tuple: (anchor_regex, dismissal_regexes_or_None).
# If anchor matches AND any dismissal in the list matches (within the same
# message), the anti-pattern fires. If dismissal list is None, the anchor
# alone is sufficient (used for explicit phrasings of the bias).
patches.new_string:
# Anti-pattern triggers. Each tuple: (anchor_regex, dismissal_regexes_or_None).
# If anchor matches AND any dismissal in the list matches (within
# PROXIMITY_WINDOW chars of that anchor occurrence), the anti-pattern fires.
# If dismissal list is None, the anchor alone is sufficient (used for
# explicit phrasings of the bias).
patches.rationale: Spec-comment update: the old comment codified whole-message scope ('within the same message'), which is exactly the mechanism producing cross-topic false blocks. The new comment states the windowed pairing so spec and code stay in agreement after the behavior change.

patches.new_string:
# Pattern 5: "segment is too small" near dismissive
    (
        r"segment\s+is\s+too\s+small",
        None,
    ),
]

# Proximity window (chars, each side) for anchor↔dismissal pairing in paired
# patterns. Whole-message co-occurrence produced cross-topic false blocks
# (e.g. "only 3%" about one topic + an unrelated "skip" 600 chars later);
# dismissive verdicts empirically cluster next to the % claim they dismiss.
PROXIMITY_WINDOW = 250
patches.rationale: Named deterministic constant with the FP-class justification inline, placed directly after TRIGGER_PATTERNS so the pairing rule reads top-to-bottom. 250 chars each side comfortably covers the real fixtures (dismissal lands within ~60 chars of the anchor in BAD_FIXTURE) and the near-200 probe, while excluding the 600+ char cross-topic probe.

Core change. Standalone patterns (2-5, dismissal_list=None) are untouched in behavior. Paired pattern 1 now iterates ALL anchor occurrences (re.finditer, not the first-only re.search) and searches dismissals only in text[anchor.start-250 : anchor.end+250]. finditer is load-bearing: with single re.search a message whose FIRST 'only X%' is far from the dismissal but whose SECOND is adjacent would regress from fire to pass (multi-anchor probe: orig 2 / patched 2 confirms no regression). 'break' after the first fired anchor preserves the existing at-most-one-fire-per-pattern behavior, and the stderr "'<anchor>' near '<dismissal>'" wording becomes literally true. Residual edge accepted and documented: window slicing can truncate a word at the boundary so \bskip\b could match a truncated 'skipping' — requires the word to start exactly at the window edge; negligible vs the killed FP class. Fail-open posture, exit codes, logging (none in this hook), stdin handling, and recursion guard all untouched. Verified: all 3 patches applied byte-exact+unique via Edit on an identical copy; probes BAD 2/GOOD 0/NEUTRAL 0/distant-FP 2→0/multi-anchor 2/near-200 2.

patches.old_string:
msg = entry.get("message") or entry
        role = msg.get("role") or entry.get("role")
        if role != "assistant":
            continue
        content = msg.get("content")
patches.new_string:
msg = entry.get("message") or entry
        role = msg.get("role") or entry.get("role")
        if role == "user":
            # Turn boundary. A genuine user prompt (string content, or a
            # content list with no tool_result blocks) marks the end of the
            # PREVIOUS turn — anything older was already adjudicated when
            # that turn stopped, so never fall through to it. Tool-result
            # carriers (role=user, tool_result blocks) are intra-turn:
            # keep scanning past them.
            u_content = msg.get("content")
            if isinstance(u_content, list) and any(
                isinstance(c, dict) and c.get("type") == "tool_result"
                for c in u_content
            ):
                continue
            break
        if role != "assistant":
            continue
        content = msg.get("content")
patches.rationale: Fixes the confirmed stale-fallback DEFECT: the reversed scan previously skipped ALL non-assistant entries, so a tool_use-only (or empty-text) final assistant entry fell through to an OLDER assistant message from a previous, already-adjudicated turn and could re-block on it (probe: exit 2 on prior-turn content). The fix bounds the scan at the current turn: a genuine user prompt (string content, or content list with no tool_result blocks) is a turn boundary -> break -> return "" -> hook exits 0 (fail-open per house standard). Tool-result carrier entries (role=user with tool_result blocks) are intra-turn plumbing and are scanned past, so a turn that ends with a trailing tool_use entry is STILL adjudicated on its own text — no enforcement skip is introduced (probe SAME-TURN TOOLUSE still exits 2). old_string is byte-exact and unique in this file. REPLICATION: get_last_assistant_message is md5-identical (e5b05b29c2e7e356238f5edabb0f4e1b) in research/meta/hooks/nth-order-cascade-hook.py and research/meta/hooks/bypass-route-hook.py, and this exact old_string is verbatim-unique in both (verified) — apply the identical patch to both siblings in the same commit to keep the three copies md5-identical.

The behavioral fix. Pattern 3 extracts plain-prose ALL-CAPS mentions, gated three ways against FPs: (a) intersection with the companies/ directory listing (same guard as pattern 2); (b) AMBIGUOUS_PROSE_TICKERS stoplist — NOW is empirically ambiguous in-scope ('happening NOW' hbm-primer.md, 'SHIPPING NOW' networking-primer.md, vs 'NOW held position'); NET/TEAM/WAY are plausible caps-English with zero observed in-scope ticker-coverage cost; BE/ARM/MU/TE/DISCO/HIVE/SYM/MOD/SNOW deliberately kept because corpus probes show ticker-only usage in-scope ('Hyperscalers buy BE'); (c) whole-first hyphen-compound matching so MITSUI-KINZOKU never false-extracts MITSUI and SEMCOTECH never leaks SEMCO. Pattern 3 is scoped to changeset-ADDED lines (whole file only for git-new artifacts) because a whole-file extractor was measured to create 148 latent missing-backref pairs across 25 of 27 live artifacts (triangulation.md alone: 20) — i.e., the next routine TC update would hit a 20-item exit-2 wall of historical debt. Added-lines scoping enforces Rule #10 on all NEW writing (including incremental triangulation.md rows, the highest-value case) at zero retro cost. The window (working tree + staged + HEAD) mirrors files_in_current_changeset() exactly — it inherits known-open G-19 (multi-commit turns) without worsening it. Git failures fall back to less enforcement via except/pass, consistent with the hook's existing fail-open style; patterns 1-2 keep their whole-file scope so no existing enforcement is weakened.

patches.new_string:
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Selftest — extraction fixtures (added 2026-07-21 with pattern 3; prose
# shapes harvested from the live corpus). Run: --selftest
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SELFTEST_VALID = {"NVDA", "BE", "DDOG", "MDB", "NOW", "MITSUI",
                  "MITSUI-KINZOKU", "SEMCO", "HYNIX", "ARM"}

# (name, full_text, prose_text — None means same as full_text, expected)
SELFTEST = [
    ("path-ref", "see companies/NVDA/thesis.md", None, {"NVDA"}),
    ("bold-ref", "| **NVDA** | beneficiary |", None, {"NVDA"}),
    ("plain-prose (2026-07-21 audit fixture)",
     "NVDA is the main beneficiary; AMD lags", None, {"NVDA"}),
    ("plain two-letter (power-for-ai-primer real shape)",
     "Hyperscalers buy BE for marginal capacity", None, {"BE"}),
    ("ambiguous English caps must NOT fire (hbm-primer real shape)",
     "The HBM4 transition is happening NOW", None, set()),
    ("ambiguous ticker still counts via bold",
     "**NOW** held position confirmed", None, {"NOW"}),
    ("slash list (agentic-ai-enterprise real shape)",
     "DDOG/NOW/MDB lose share, not disappear", None, {"DDOG", "MDB"}),
    ("hyphen compound matches whole, not parts",
     "MITSUI-KINZOKU capacity shift", None, {"MITSUI-KINZOKU"}),
    ("no substring leak from longer token",
     "SEMCOTECH reallocates supply", None, set()),
    ("hyphen prefix kept (edge-hardware real shape)",
     "an ARM-architecture CPU", None, {"ARM"}),
    ("possessive", "NVDA's margin holds", None, {"NVDA"}),
    ("lowercase never matches", "now the way to be", None, set()),
    ("caps jargon ignored", "TL;DR GPU TDP HBM CAGR FY27 TC-13 P0", None, set()),
    ("pattern 3 scoped to prose_text; patterns 1-2 stay whole-file",
     "NVDA is the main beneficiary; see companies/HYNIX/thesis.md", "",
     {"HYNIX"}),
]


def selftest():
    failures = []
    for name, full_text, prose_text, expected in SELFTEST:
        prose = full_text if prose_text is None else prose_text
        got = extract_tickers_from_text(full_text, prose, SELFTEST_VALID)
        if got != expected:
            failures.append(
                f"FAIL {name}: expected {sorted(expected)}, got {sorted(got)}")
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(SELFTEST)}/{len(SELFTEST)} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        selftest()
    main()
patches.rationale: Embedded selftest per house standard (mirrors session-prime-cascade-hook.py v2's module-level SELFTEST list + selftest() + argv dispatch before main()). 14 fixtures: the audit's exact GAP fixture, real corpus shapes for the plain/BE/ARM/slash-list cases, the NOW ambiguity in both directions (prose skipped, bold still counts), both hyphen-compound edges, substring-leak and caps-jargon negatives, and one fixture pinning the prose_text scoping contract itself. Verified 14/14 pass on the patched copy.

patches.rationale: Restores the house FAIL-OPEN (exit 0) standard for the transcript reader. (1) OSError is the parent of FileNotFoundError/PermissionError plus IsADirectoryError, so a transcript_path that is a directory (or any other IO failure) returns "" instead of an uncaught traceback + exit 1; UnicodeDecodeError is raised lazily inside readlines() within the try, so non-UTF-8 transcripts now fail open too. (2) isinstance guards skip valid-JSON scalar lines ("str"/42/null) and entries whose 'message' field is a truthy non-dict — previously AttributeError on .get — mirroring the existing JSONDecodeError continue. No enforcement change: well-formed assistant entries have dict message; lines with no 'message' key still fall back to msg = entry (a dict). Probe-verified: [dir-transcript]/[nonutf8]/[scalar-line]/[str-message] go from exit 1 + traceback to exit 0 silent; canonical BAD/GOOD/NEUTRAL fixtures unchanged at 2/0/0. old_string verified byte-exact and unique in the file. Matches the fail-open reader posture of the newer-generation hooks (macro-anchor, reasoning-tagging) as a surgical diff rather than a rewrite. NOTE: the identical old_string pattern exists in nth-order-cascade-hook.py (~lines 140-149) and bypass-route-hook.py (~lines 122-131); the same patch shape applies there under their own findings.

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 2026-07-21: fail-open coverage — malformed transcripts must exit 0
    # (house standard: hooks FAIL-OPEN on internal error), never
    # traceback with exit 1.
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def run_hook_payload(hook_path: Path, transcript_path: str) -> int:
        payload = json.dumps({"transcript_path": transcript_path})
        r = subprocess.run(
            ["python3", str(hook_path)],
            input=payload,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            cwd=str(REPO_ROOT),
            timeout=10,
        )
        return r.returncode

    if SEGMENT_HOOK.exists():
        print()
        print("segment-trajectory-hook — fail-open on malformed transcripts")

        # transcript_path is a directory -> IsADirectoryError class
        dir_path = tempfile.mkdtemp()
        try:
            result.check(
                "fail-open — directory transcript exits 0",
                run_hook_payload(SEGMENT_HOOK, dir_path) == 0,
            )
        finally:
            os.rmdir(dir_path)

        # non-UTF-8 transcript -> UnicodeDecodeError class
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
            mode="wb", suffix=".jsonl", delete=False
        ) as f:
            f.write(b'{"role":"assistant","content":"\xff\xfe"}\n')
            nonutf8_path = f.name
        try:
            result.check(
                "fail-open — non-UTF-8 transcript exits 0",
                run_hook_payload(SEGMENT_HOOK, nonutf8_path) == 0,
            )
        finally:
            os.unlink(nonutf8_path)

        # valid-JSON scalar line + string message field -> AttributeError class
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
            mode="w", suffix=".jsonl", delete=False
        ) as f:
            f.write('"just a scalar string"\n')
            f.write('{"message":"plain string","role":"assistant"}\n')
            scalar_path = f.name
        try:
            result.check(
                "fail-open — scalar/non-dict JSONL lines exit 0",
                run_hook_payload(SEGMENT_HOOK, scalar_path) == 0,
            )
        finally:
            os.unlink(scalar_path)

patches.rationale: has_any/find_first_match apply re.IGNORECASE to every pattern, which defeats the case-sensitivity these two anchors depend on per the docstring (lines 25-27: TICKER pattern, Core/Active/Watchlist/Avoid tier vocabulary) and the CLAUDE.md hook bullet. The (?-i:...) local flag group (Python 3.6+) disables IGNORECASE only inside the group, so: (a) the uppercase ticker stub and the four tier proper nouns become case-sensitive as documented, (b) the noun tail (thesis|stock|name|position|holding) and every other anchor stay case-insensitive exactly as before, and (c) the shared has_any/find_first_match helpers and the single ANALYTICAL_ANCHORS list are untouched — no cased/uncased list split, no helper signature change.     r"\b\w+[-\s]constrained\b",
    r"\bTime-to-[A-Z]\w*",
patches.new_string:
r"\bsupply[-\s]constrained\b",
    r"\bcapacity[-\s]constrained\b",
    # <noun>-constrained compounds per docstring: hyphen required, negation
    # prefixes excluded (narrowed 2026-07-21 — the old [-\s] alternation also
    # matched verb phrases like 'not constrained' / 'is constrained')
    r"\b(?!(?:non|un|not|less)-)\w+-constrained\b",
    r"\bTime-to-[A-Z]\w*",
patches.rationale: DEFECT fix, spec-conformant narrowing. Docstring line 25 specifies '<noun>-constrained' supply-side COMPOUND vocabulary; the shipped generic pattern \b\w+[-\s]constrained\b also accepts a SPACE separator, so any predicate-adjective phrase — 'is/not/less/no longer constrained', i.e. explicit statements that something is NOT binding — fires exit 2 and demands bypass-route language (probe: exit 2, Trigger: 'not constrained'). Fix = (a) require the hyphen, which is what makes it a compound per the spec, and (b) negative-lookahead the four realistic hyphenated negation prefixes (non-/un-/not-/less-constrained), which would otherwise still invert meaning. Verified trade-offs: zero TP loss on the documented vocabulary — 'supply constrained'/'capacity constrained' space forms remain covered by the dedicated patterns two lines above (probe shows Trigger: 'supply constrained'/'capacity constrained' still exit 2), and novel hyphen compounds (power-constrained, HBM-constrained, wafer-constrained) still fire. Residual accepted narrowing: a NOVEL noun in space form ('power constrained') no longer triggers the generic pattern — that form was never in the documented spec, and keeping it is exactly what re-admits the verb-phrase FP class. One-line regex change + dated comment matching the file's existing comment style (cf. the dated 2026-06-27 comment block in EXEMPTION_PATTERNS); docstring needs no edit since line 25 already states the now-actual behavior. Verified end-to-end on a patched scratchpad copy: 9/9 cases pass (3 FP classes fixed, 5 TP paths retained, exemption path unaffected); old_string asserted byte-exact and unique (count==1) against the live file.

patches.old_string:
r"\b\w+[-\s]constrained\b",
    r"\bTime-to-[A-Z]\w*",
    r"WORKFLOW:\s*BOTTLENECK-FORECAST",
patches.new_string:
r"\b\w+[-\s]constrained\b",
    # case-sensitive: house Time-to-X framework only, not generic "time-to-market"
    r"(?-i:\bTime-to-[A-Z]\w*)",
    r"WORKFLOW:\s*BOTTLENECK-FORECAST",
patches.rationale: Fixes the confirmed DEFECT: has_any()/find_first_match() compile every TRIGGER pattern with re.IGNORECASE, which collapses [A-Z] to any-letter and makes generic business phrases ('time-to-market', 'time-to-value', sentence-start 'Time-to-market') fire the constraint trigger, contradicting docstring line 26 ('Time-to-X framework usage' — the house TTQ/TTP/TTD family, which is always written capitalized). The inline (?-i:...) local flag group (Python >=3.6) disables IGNORECASE for this one pattern only, leaving the shared IGNORECASE call sites and all other 13 trigger patterns untouched — the smallest possible diff versus restructuring to per-pattern compiled flags. Verified by probe: 'Their time-to-market improved a lot this year.' and 'Time-to-market matters here for the launch.' go exit 2 -> exit 0; 'Time-to-Production is the binding lever...' stays exit 2; TTQ-exempt and neutral cases unchanged; existing BAD/GOOD suite fixtures unaffected (no Time-to token). Residual accepted FP: fully title-cased 'Time-to-Market' still matches — it is orthographically indistinguishable from house framework usage and rare in prose; kills the error class without adding FN risk on real Time-to-X discussion.

patches.new_string:
Hooks are not permanent. Per the fluid-loop rules in `research/CLAUDE.md`: a hook that fires <5×/month for 3 consecutive months is INERT (retire), and every hook codification carries a falsifier.

**Measurability precondition (added 2026-07-21 audit):** the INERT metric is only computable for hooks instrumented to `meta/hook-fire-log.md` (see Hook conventions above). The 11 un-instrumented Stop hooks have zero log entries by construction — for them "no observed fires" is absence of instrumentation, NOT evidence of inertness, and the retirement rule must not be applied until they log (retiring a live enforcement hook is a HIGH-tier destructive change per Critical Rule #19; misreading an empty log as inertness is exactly the error this note blocks). Instrumenting the remainder is mechanical — copy the fail-open `log_fire` pattern from `macro-anchor-hook.py` into each exit-2 path; `meta/structural-output-metric.py` matches only `structural-output.*FIRE` lines, so new hook names cannot pollute the priming-experiment numerator — but it is an 11-file behavior change: queued for the 2026-07-24 review-grade slot, each hook's logging shipping with a selftest fixture per house standard.

Current open decision: the two-bracket experiment (llm-native-priming + structural-output) 30-day close was due 2026-07-01 and is pending — see `meta/todo.md`.
patches.rationale: Closes the confirmed GAP at the spec layer NOW: the retirement rule as written invites a deterministic mis-read (empty log = inert = retire) against 11 of 15 Stop hooks whose fires are structurally unloggable, which would delete live enforcement — a Rule #19 HIGH-tier action. The caveat makes the rule honest and self-guarding, pre-verifies the one interaction risk of the real fix (structural-output-metric.py line 34 regex requires 'structural-output', so instrumenting other hooks cannot contaminate the 08-06 priming-experiment decision, independent of open G-28), and routes the 11-file behavior change to the 2026-07-24 review-grade slot per house precedent instead of half-shipping it here. Error class killed: false retirement of un-measured enforcement hooks; added complexity: none (doc-only, zero runtime change); FP risk: none.

patches.rationale: CONFIRMED DEFECT (line 69, TRIGGER_PATTERNS forward-horizon branch): docstring line 31 and research/CLAUDE.md both spec the trigger generically ('by YYYY' / 'H1/H2 YYYY' / 'FYXX' / 'forward year/quarter'), but the regex caps calendar years at 20[2-4]\d (2049 ceiling) and fiscal years at 2[2-9] (FY29 ceiling) in ALL FOUR alternatives. Probes on the live hook confirm 'by FY30', 'by 2050', 'by H2 2050', 'by Q1 FY30' each exit 0 on a horizon-only predictive message while 'by FY28'/'by 2049' exit 2 — so with today=2026, live FY30/FY31 guidance horizons and 2050 power-roadmap horizons escape principle #1 / B23 / L1 enforcement entirely. Fix widens the ceiling one character per alternative: 20[2-4]\d -> 20[2-5]\d (through 2059) in both the bare-year and H[12] branches, and 2[2-9] -> [2-3]\d (FY20-FY39) in both the FY and Q[1-4] branches. Side benefit verified by probe: four-digit 'by FY2030' now matches via the FY-branch prefix (it previously matched nothing). Marginal FP surface is negligible and probed: 'by FY20'/'by FY21' (backward fiscal references, essentially nonexistent in forward prose) and 'by 2050-2059' basis-point-style numbers — the same FP class the pattern already accepts for 2020-2049; 'costs fell by 15%', 'grew by 1900 people', and 'by 2060' all remain non-matching. All other mechanics in this file were re-verified as MATCH during patch design and left untouched: in_scope() cwd-prefix scoping with CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/path-relative fallback, stdin-JSON fail-open (exit 0 on JSONDecodeError), stop_hook_active recursion guard, missing/empty-transcript exit 0, last-assistant-message extraction (str + list-of-text-blocks), trigger-then-exemption ordering, exit-2 stderr message citing methodology #1 / lessons #1 / B23. This hook has no fire-log call and no embedded --selftest — consistent with its 2026-05-23 pre-telemetry generation; its canonical fixtures live in research/meta/tests/test_framework_codifications.py (lines 733-767), which is where the fixture additions below go. old_string verified byte-exact and unique in the file (programmatic count == 1).

patches.rationale: Remove the undocumented third-leg alternative so code matches spec. Every documentation layer enumerates only 'Tier classification OR Position target' as the third trigger leg: docstring lines 27-31, the CLAUDE.md Enforcement-hooks bullet ('ALL THREE of P(bull, P(bear, and Tier: (Core/Active/Watchlist/Avoid) OR Position target'), hooks/README.md line 32, the birth commit 3ad098f's own message, and the hook's own stderr block text at lines 160-161 ('P(bull) + P(bear) + Tier/Position present'). The r"\bConviction\s+Format\b" literal makes a bare meta-mention of the format name satisfy the third leg, so 'P(bull): 60% P(bear): 20% Conviction Format applies.' blocks (probe: exit 2) with no tier and no position — broader than the narrow trigger the user explicitly mandated 2026-05-23 to minimize false positives. It is also mis-targeted against the bias this hook exists to catch: B24 is tier-WITHOUT-M/N, which presupposes a tier is present. Error class killed: meta-discussion FPs where P(bull)/P(bear) probabilities are being discussed alongside the format's name (the 'hook'/'principle #' exemptions usually but not always co-occur). Error class conceded: a thesis using a 'Conviction Format' heading with both P legs but no Tier:/Position-target line and no M/N would now pass — that class is outside B24 and outside every documented spec, and the canonical Conviction Format template always carries a Tier section. Docstring, CLAUDE.md, README, and stderr text already describe post-patch behavior, so this one-line removal needs zero documentation edits — minimal diff, spec-aligning direction.

Fix adds exactly the two bold variants mirroring the Tier leg's existing three-variant structure: 'Position target: **12%**' (value bolded, analog of line 59) and '**Position target:** 12%' / '**Position target** 12%' (label bolded with optional colon inside, analog of line 60). Two added lines, no other behavior touched; the trigger still requires all three conjuncts plus no exemption, so false-positive surface is unchanged. Verified on a patched scratch copy: all 8 probe cases pass including the 3 existing suite fixtures (regression-clean).

patches.rationale: Closes the confirmed GAP: triggers were evaluated without re.DOTALL while exemptions (has_any, line 166) got re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL, so any PT-vs-price framing whose .{0,30}/.{0,20} gap wraps across a newline (markdown lists, hard-wrapped prose — probe: 'The consensus PT sits ¥4,000\nbelow the current price.' exited 0 pre-patch, 2 post-patch) escaped enforcement of B28/B37 while exemptions matched maximally. Adding DOTALL to the trigger side is the minimal symmetric fix; the alternative (removing DOTALL from exemptions) would raise FP rate, against the FP-averse house design. Residual FP surface is not a new class: cross-sentence stitches within a 30-char gap already match pre-patch when on one line (probed: 'trading at a premium; below, the consensus view' blocks on the LIVE hook today), and the broad meta-discussion exemption list is the existing mitigation. Bounded gaps ({0,30} max) cap the added reach. NOTE for the ledger: the identical asymmetry exists in bottoms-up-hook.py (lines 153-159) and borrowed-vs-firstprinciples-hook.py (lines 198-204) — same one-flag fix applies there, left for their own file-audit slots per one-finding scope.

patches.new_string:
def selftest() -> int:
    # Wrapped-trigger regression fixtures (DOTALL parity fix, 2026-07-21).
    # Each: (text, expect_trigger, expect_exempt).
    fixtures = [
        # newline inside pattern-3's .{0,30} gap — the escape the fix closes
        ("The consensus PT sits ¥4,000\nbelow the current price.",
         True, False),
        # same framing on one line — must keep triggering
        ("The consensus PT sits ¥4,000 below the current price.",
         True, False),
        # wrapped trigger + explicit structural-context classification
        ("The consensus PT sits ¥4,000\nbelow the current price. "
         "Classification: analysts behind the curve (structural rerating).",
         True, True),
        # benign wrap: no target/PT follows 'street' — must NOT trigger
        ("Street estimates vary.\nAbove all, capacity discipline matters.",
         False, False),
    ]
    failed = 0
    for i, (text, want_trigger, want_exempt) in enumerate(fixtures, 1):
        got_trigger = bool(find_first_match(text, TRIGGER_PATTERNS))
        got_exempt = has_any(text, EXEMPTION_PATTERNS)
        if got_trigger != want_trigger or got_exempt != want_exempt:
            print(f"FAIL fixture {i}: trigger={got_trigger} (want "
                  f"{want_trigger}), exempt={got_exempt} (want {want_exempt})")
            failed += 1
    if failed:
        return 1
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(fixtures)}/{len(fixtures)} fixtures pass")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        sys.exit(selftest())
    main()

patches.new_string:
def selftest() -> int:
    """Regression suite for the trigger/exemption regexes (added 2026-07-21
    with the lesson-ID collision fix). Run:
    python3 borrowed-vs-firstprinciples-hook.py --selftest
    Pure-regex fixtures — no transcript, no repo I/O."""
    failures = []
    ran = {"n": 0}

    def check(name, cond):
        ran["n"] += 1
        if not cond:
            failures.append(name)

    check("trigger: consensus expects",
          bool(find_first_match("Consensus expects 34% growth", TRIGGER_PATTERNS)))
    check("no trigger: plain bottoms-up prose",
          not find_first_match("Capacity gate math from the filing", TRIGGER_PATTERNS))

    # Lesson-ID collision (2026-07-21 fix): bare L1-L5 lesson/autonomy-level
    # citations must NOT exempt consensus framing.
    for s in [
        "Consensus expects 34% growth, per L3 rebalance rule",
        "The street expects a beat; no add per L1 lesson",
        "Analyst consensus is bullish; research gaps per L5 honesty discipline",
        "Consensus sees upside; ~$200 content per L2+ vehicle",
    ]:
        check("lesson-ID does not exempt: %r" % s[:40], not has_any(s, EXEMPTION_PATTERNS))

    # Genuine Layer-derivation context must still exempt.
    for s in [
        "derived per Layer 0 CapEx",
        "built per L0/L1/L2",
        "L0→L1 chain gives Y",
        "the L2 derivation says Y",
        "L0 CapEx baseline is $745B",
    ]:
        check("Layer context exempts: %r" % s[:40], has_any(s, EXEMPTION_PATTERNS))

    # L10-L39 were never Layer-exempt (unchanged behavior).
    check("L39 does not exempt", not has_any("per L39", EXEMPTION_PATTERNS))

    if failures:
        print("SELFTEST FAIL:\n  " + "\n  ".join(failures))
        return 1
    print("SELFTEST PASS (%d checks)" % ran["n"])
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) > 1 and sys.argv[1] == "--selftest":
        sys.exit(selftest())
    main()

patches.new_string: r"(?m)(\(|^)T[1-4]\)?\s*[:—-]",  # (?m): tier labels at line starts, not just message start
patches.rationale: Confirmed DEFECT (line 109, EXEMPTION_PATTERNS tier-label block). Two dead anchors in one pattern: (1) `^` without re.MULTILINE only matches offset 0 — has_any() passes re.IGNORECASE|re.DOTALL only, and DOTALL does not affect `^` — so a line-start 'T1:' / 'T2 —' tier label mid-message never exempts, contradicting the docstring's markdown-tier-label intent and the pattern's own `^` alternative; probe: 'Sources below.\nT3: street consensus view...' → live hook exit 2 (false block). (2) the leading `\b` before `(\(|^)` requires a WORD character before '(' , so the common ' (T1: ...)' parenthetical after whitespace also never matched (only 'foo(T1:' did). Fix: inline (?m) global flag (legal at pattern start on all Python 3.x, scopes MULTILINE to this single pattern — zero effect on the other 46 exemption regexes or 10 trigger regexes) and drop the `\b`, which is provably redundant on the `^T` branch (a \n→T or start→T transition is always a boundary) and actively harmful on the `\(` branch. No new FP surface: the `(\(|^)` alternation itself still blocks mid-word matches — probe-verified 'HTTPST1://' does not match the new pattern. Error class killed: false-blocks on bulleted/mid-message tier-labeled source lists that co-occur with consensus-trigger language and have no other co-firing exemption. Enforcement preserved: trigger-without-label control still exits 2 under the patch (end-to-end verified via subprocess with CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR pinned). old_string byte-verified unique in file (count==1, em dash in [:—-] preserved).

patches.rationale: CONFIRMED DEFECT (the assigned finding): in r"\b\+...", the \b sits between a non-word char (or string start) and the non-word '+', so it only matches when a word character immediately precedes '+' — i.e. only the unspaced typo form 'up+45%'. The documented trigger form '+X% YTD' (docstring lines 45-50) has been dead since ship: probe shows re.search False on 'is +85% YTD' and the live hook exits 0 end-to-end on 'The stock is +85% YTD so wait for a pullback before entry'. Fix = drop the \b entirely (one-token diff). Chosen over (?<![\w.])\+ because the lookbehind would regress the one form the old regex did catch ('up+45% YTD'); bare \+ is a strict superset of pre-fix behavior, and the mandatory trailing YTD/1Y/1-year/YoY anchor plus %-suffix keeps the FP surface exactly at the docstring's intended scope. def _log_fire(reason: str) -> None:
    """
    Append a fire event to `meta/hook-fire-log.md` with the failure
    type(s) so the codified retirement triggers (fires <3x/month over
    3 months -> retire; FP rate >40% -> refine) are measurable from
    the committed log. Transcript archaeology is fragile in ephemeral
    cloud containers; the committed log is the only fire record that
    survives container reclamation. Same pattern as
    structural-output-hook.py. Added 2026-07-21 audit (the docstring
    promised an activation log that was never built).
    """
    try:
        from datetime import datetime, timezone
        log_path = Path(ENFORCEMENT_PATHS[0]) / "research/meta/hook-fire-log.md"
        ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%SZ")
        with open(log_path, "a") as lf:
            lf.write(f"- {ts} llm-native-reasoning-hook FIRE ({reason})\n")
    except Exception:
        pass


def main():

The '- YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ {hook-name} FIRE (reason)' row format matches the fire-log header contract exactly. Anchor '    return findings


def main():' is unique in the file (find_triggers is the only function returning findings).

2. Analyst-note attribution pattern with multi-paragraph quote:

patches.rationale: Docstring Detection #2 said 'quoted paragraphs' but never defined the predicate; code counted all paragraphs. State the implemented predicate precisely so spec and code agree. The bare-name attribution tokens (Citrini, Gerstner) stay — the docstring itself lists them as markers; the paragraph predicate is where the GAP lives.

patches.new_string:
# A paragraph counts toward the Trigger-B threshold only if it looks like
# quoted note content: markdown blockquote / leading quote char, OR
# substantial prose (≥150 chars). Chat paragraphs run well under 150 chars;
# pasted analyst-note paragraphs run 180-340+ (calibrated 2026-07-21 audit —
# previous ALL-paragraph count let a bare 'Citrini' mention + any two short
# chat paragraphs block the Stop).
QUOTE_PARA_MIN_CHARS = 150
QUOTE_MARK_PREFIXES = (">", '"', "“")


def detect_analyst_note_with_quote(messages: list[str]) -> bool:
    """
    Returns True if recent user message contains analyst attribution
    combined with substantial quoted content (≥3 quote-like paragraphs:
    quote-marked OR ≥QUOTE_PARA_MIN_CHARS of prose). Bare attribution
    mentions amid short chat paragraphs do NOT count (2026-07-21 audit).
    """
    for msg in messages:
        if ANALYST_ATTRIBUTION_PATTERN.search(msg):
            quote_paras = 0
            for p in msg.split("\n\n"):
                p = p.strip()
                if not p:
                    continue
                if p.startswith(QUOTE_MARK_PREFIXES) or len(p) >= QUOTE_PARA_MIN_CHARS:
                    quote_paras += 1
            if quote_paras >= 3:
                return True
    return False
patches.rationale: The confirmed GAP: paragraph_count counted ALL non-empty \n\n-separated paragraphs, so any 3-paragraph message merely mentioning Citrini/Gerstner/Mirae exited 2 (probe confirmed end-to-end). New predicate counts only quote-like paragraphs: blockquote/'\"'/'“'-prefixed (covers quoted pastes) OR ≥150 chars (covers the common plain-prose paste — probe showed chat paragraphs at 18-42 chars vs note paragraphs at 185-337). Threshold of 3 unchanged; Trigger A, exemptions, git check, exit paths all untouched.

_NOTE_PLAIN = (
    "New Citrini note out today, pasting the relevant section:\n\n"
    "The AI capex cycle has entered its third phase, where the binding "
    "constraint migrates from advanced packaging to power delivery. We "
    "estimate hyperscaler capex reaches new records with the majority "
    "allocated to compute infrastructure, and grid interconnect queues now "
    "gate datacenter additions in every major US market through the decade.\n\n"
    "Within the power complex we prefer names with existing merchant capacity "
    "over regulated utilities, as the former capture spot-price upside from "
    "datacenter demand while the latter must socialize returns through rate "
    "cases. Turbine lead times have extended materially per our checks.\n\n"
    "We are adding exposure via a basket approach given single-name "
    "regulatory risk, and we flag transformer supply as the next derivative "
    "bottleneck for the cohort over the coming quarters."
)

_NOTE_BLOCKQUOTE = (
    "From the Goldman note this morning:\n\n"
    "> We upgrade our datacenter power demand forecast and see structural "
    "tightness persisting.\n\n"
    "> Grid interconnect timelines remain the binding constraint in our "
    "coverage.\n\n"
    "> We raise price targets across the cohort accordingly."
)

SELFTEST = [
    ("bare attribution + short chat paragraphs must NOT fire (audit probe)",
     "Citrini\n\nwhat do you think about that account generally?\n\n"
     "just curious, no note attached.", False),
    ("casual 3-paragraph name mention must NOT fire",
     "I saw Gerstner on CNBC yesterday.\n\nHe seemed bullish.\n\n"
     "Anyway, unrelated: what's our NVDA target?", False),
    ("plain-paste analyst note (substantial paragraphs) fires",
     _NOTE_PLAIN, True),
    ("blockquoted analyst note fires", _NOTE_BLOCKQUOTE, True),
    ("single substantial paragraph below ≥3 threshold must NOT fire",
     "New Citrini note out today:\n\n" + "x" * 200 + "\n\n", False),
]


def selftest():
    failures = []
    for name, msg, expected in SELFTEST:
        got = detect_analyst_note_with_quote([msg])
        if got != expected:
            failures.append(f"FAIL {name}: expected {expected}, got {got}")
    brief = "AI Intelligence Brief — Morning Edition\n\n42 sources scanned"
    if not detect_brief_in_user_messages([brief]):
        failures.append("FAIL Trigger A regression: brief header not detected")
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)} fixture(s) FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {len(SELFTEST) + 1}/{len(SELFTEST) + 1} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        selftest()
    main()
patches.rationale: House standard requires embedded --selftest with fixtures for behavior changes; this hook had none. Fixture set covers both over-trigger negatives (the confirmed probe + casual mention), both realistic paste styles as positives, the ≥3 threshold boundary, and a Trigger-A regression check. Style mirrors session-prime-cascade-hook.py's SELFTEST/selftest()/__main__ dispatch pattern.

Verification therefore ships as a standalone probe file. Exact code, written to /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/probe_exemption.py (already created and run pre-patch):

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Read-only probe: signal-ingest-cascade-hook user-exemption phrasing coverage.

1. Unit-level: import has_user_exemption and test phrasings.
2. End-to-end: run the hook with a synthetic transcript (brief trigger +
   exemption phrase) and check exit code. Exemption is checked BEFORE the
   git cross-source-log check, so exit 0 on exempt is git-state-independent.
"""
import importlib.util
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

HOOK = "/home/user/LLMNA/research/meta/hooks/signal-ingest-cascade-hook.py"

spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("sich", HOOK)
mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(mod)

cases = [
    ("don't cascade this", True),          # documented form
    ("skip the cascade", True),            # documented form
    ("no log needed", True),               # documented form
    ("do not cascade this", True),         # natural phrasing of documented opt-out
    ("no cascade please", True),           # natural phrasing of documented opt-out
    ("no need to cascade here", True),     # natural phrasing of documented opt-out
    ("please cascade this fully", False),  # must NOT exempt (regression guard)
    ("the cascade is required", False),    # must NOT exempt (regression guard)
]
fails = 0
for text, want in cases:
    got = mod.has_user_exemption([text])
    mark = "PASS" if got == want else "FAIL"
    if got != want:
        fails += 1
    print(f"[{mark}] has_user_exemption({text!r}) = {got} (want {want})")

# End-to-end: brief trigger + 'do not cascade' in same user turn
brief = "AI Intelligence Brief — Morning Edition\n47 sources scanned\n...content...\ndo not cascade this, just discuss."
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", suffix=".jsonl", delete=False) as f:
    f.write(json.dumps({"type": "user", "message": {"content": brief}}) + "\n")
    tpath = f.name
r = subprocess.run(
    [sys.executable, HOOK],
    input=json.dumps({"transcript_path": tpath}),
    capture_output=True, text=True, cwd="/home/user/LLMNA",
)
print(f"end-to-end ('do not cascade' + brief trigger): exit {r.returncode} "
      f"(0 = exempt honored, 2 = block)")
os.unlink(tpath)
sys.exit(1 if fails else 0)

def log_error(detail):
    """Append a house-format ERROR line to hook-fire-log.md. Never raises."""
    try:
        from datetime import datetime, timezone
        ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%SZ")
        with open(FIRE_LOG, "a") as f:
            f.write(f"- {ts} signal-ingest-cascade-hook ERROR ({detail})\n")
    except Exception:
        pass

patches.new_string:
def cross_source_log_file_created_or_modified() -> bool | None:
    """
    Check if any file in research/signals/cross-source-log/ was created/modified
    in working tree, staged area, or most recent commit.

    Returns None when BOTH git probes errored (missing binary, non-repo
    REPO_ROOT, corrupt/locked repo): verification is impossible — an internal
    infrastructure failure, not analyst non-compliance. Caller must fail-open
    (house standard). Added 2026-07-21 audit.
    """
    files = set()
    git_errors = 0
patches.rationale: Introduces a tri-state return: True (satisfied), False (verified non-compliant), None (unverifiable — both git probes errored). Keeps 'cannot verify' distinguishable from 'verified missing', which is the root cause of the fail-closed defect.

First git probe (status --porcelain): count the error instead of silently swallowing it into an empty file set. Trailing context (the diff-tree call) makes this old_string unique vs the second identical except-pass block.

Second git probe (diff-tree HEAD): count the error, then return the None sentinel only when BOTH probes failed. If either probe succeeded the file-set scan is still meaningful, so enforcement proceeds unchanged (verified: an empty git repo where only diff-tree HEAD fails still blocks).

patches.new_string:
# Check if cross-source-log file was created/modified
    log_state = cross_source_log_file_created_or_modified()
    if log_state is None:
        # Both git probes errored: cannot verify compliance. House standard:
        # FAIL-OPEN (exit 0) on internal error — never block on infrastructure
        # failure indistinguishable from non-compliance. Logged, not silent.
        log_error("both git probes errored; fail-open, enforcement skipped")
        sys.exit(0)
    if log_state:
        sys.exit(0)
patches.rationale: The fix itself: main() now fails open (exit 0) with a logged ERROR when verification was impossible, instead of falling through to the exit-2 BLOCK. True/False behavior is byte-identical to before, so no enforcement path changes when git works.

patches.old_string: "Accept-Encoding": "gzip, deflate",
patches.new_string: "Accept-Encoding": "gzip",
patches.rationale: The request header advertises deflate but _get only decompresses gzip (Content-Encoding check at lines 42-43). If SEC ever honored the deflate advertisement, the zlib-compressed body would be decoded as utf-8 with errors='replace' into garbage and crash json.loads in submissions()/companyfacts()/fts(). Dropping deflate makes the advertised-encodings contract self-consistent with a 1-word diff: a compliant server may now only send gzip or identity, both of which the code handles. No live-path behavior change — SEC currently serves gzip (edgar_client verified live 2026-07-16 per research/meta/data-access.md:25). Alternative (adding a zlib.decompress branch) rejected as more code + more surface for a latent LOW-severity path, contrary to house small-diff preference. old_string verified byte-exact and unique (count==1) in the file.

designs.fixtures:
edgar_client.py has NO embedded --selftest (house standard is selftest 'where present'; this is a data tool, not a hook — adding a selftest harness would be a larger diff than the fix). The fixture is therefore a standalone offline probe, written and already validated at /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/probe_edgar_encoding.py. It monkeypatches urllib.request.urlopen (zero network, zero SEC traffic) and asserts: (1) the advertised Accept-Encoding header; (2) gzip-encoded response decodes to valid JSON (regression); (3) if-and-only-if deflate is still advertised, serves a zlib-compressed body and exits 3 on the resulting JSONDecodeError (the defect). Exact code:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Offline probe for edgar_client._get content-decoding vs Accept-Encoding contract.

Monkeypatches urllib.request.urlopen — no network, no SEC traffic.
Usage: python3 probe_edgar_encoding.py <path-to-edgar_client.py>

Checks:
  1. What Accept-Encoding the client advertises.
  2. gzip response  -> must decode to valid JSON (regression check).
  3. deflate response -> only run if 'deflate' is advertised; shows the
     garbage-decode -> JSONDecodeError failure (the G-finding defect).
"""
import gzip, importlib.util, io, json, sys, urllib.request, zlib

spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("edgar_client", sys.argv[1])
ec = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(ec)

captured = {}

class FakeResp:
    def __init__(self, body, encoding):
        self._body = body
        self.headers = {"Content-Encoding": encoding} if encoding else {}
    def read(self):
        return self._body
    def __enter__(self):
        return self
    def __exit__(self, *a):
        return False

def make_urlopen(body, encoding):
    def fake_urlopen(req, timeout=None):
        captured["accept_encoding"] = req.headers.get("Accept-encoding")  # urllib capitalizes
        return FakeResp(body, encoding)
    return fake_urlopen

payload = json.dumps({"ok": True, "n": 42}).encode()

# 1. Advertised Accept-Encoding
urllib.request.urlopen = make_urlopen(gzip.compress(payload), "gzip")
ec._get("https://data.sec.gov/probe.json")
adv = captured["accept_encoding"]
print(f"advertised Accept-Encoding: {adv!r}")

# 2. gzip path (must always pass)
urllib.request.urlopen = make_urlopen(gzip.compress(payload), "gzip")
out = json.loads(ec._get("https://data.sec.gov/probe.json"))
assert out == {"ok": True, "n": 42}, out
print("gzip response: PASS (decoded to valid JSON)")

# 3. deflate path — only meaningful if the client advertises it
if "deflate" in (adv or ""):
    urllib.request.urlopen = make_urlopen(zlib.compress(payload), "deflate")
    try:
        json.loads(ec._get("https://data.sec.gov/probe.json"))
        print("deflate response: decoded OK (unexpected — no defect)")
    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
        print(f"deflate response: DEFECT CONFIRMED — garbage passed to json.loads ({e.__class__.__name__})")
        sys.exit(3)
else:
    print("deflate not advertised: server may not send it — contract consistent. PASS")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Selftest — added 2026-07-21 audit (house standard: embedded --selftest,
# session-prime-cascade v2 precedent). One block fixture per trigger family
# pins every regex family LIVE, so the silent-dead-regex class (cascade v1)
# fails loudly here instead of shipping dead. Run: --selftest
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SELFTEST = [
    # (name, text, should_trigger, should_exempt)
    ("family-1 cyclicality block",
     "Cyclicality is the main risk here; the equipment cycle could dampen "
     "FY27 orders.",
     True, False),
    ("family-2 rally-history block (B39 origin shape)",
     "The stock has rallied hard off the low; wait for a pullback before "
     "entry.",
     True, False),
    ("family-3 non-Western ticker block",
     "Adding 6146.T to the watchlist after the tool-order beat.",
     True, False),
    ("family-4 segment-dilution block",
     "AI upside is diluted by legacy tabletop revenue across the "
     "multi-segment business.",
     True, False),
    ("ATH-distance exemption passes a triggered message",
     "The stock has rallied hard off the low but sits 38% below its ATH.",
     True, True),
    ("everyday analytical prose must not trigger",
     "Orders accelerated two quarters in a row; capacity adds are booked "
     "through FY27.",
     False, False),
]


def _stdin_probe(payload: str):
    """Feed payload to load_last_assistant_message() via a fake stdin;
    return (exit_code or None, returned text or None)."""
    import io
    real_stdin = sys.stdin
    sys.stdin = io.StringIO(payload)
    try:
        return None, load_last_assistant_message()
    except SystemExit as e:
        return e.code, None
    finally:
        sys.stdin = real_stdin


def selftest():
    failures = []
    for name, text, want_trigger, want_exempt in SELFTEST:
        got_trigger = bool(find_triggers(text))
        got_exempt = has_exemption(text)
        if got_trigger != want_trigger or got_exempt != want_exempt:
            failures.append(
                f"FAIL {name}: trigger={got_trigger} (want {want_trigger}) "
                f"exempt={got_exempt} (want {want_exempt})")
    # Recursion guard: stop_hook_active must exit 0 before any scanning.
    code, _ = _stdin_probe('{"stop_hook_active": true}')
    if code != 0:
        failures.append(f"FAIL recursion guard: expected exit 0, got {code}")
    # Empty/garbage stdin: fail-open — returns "" (main then exits 0).
    code, text = _stdin_probe("")
    if code is not None or text != "":
        failures.append(
            f"FAIL empty-stdin fail-open: exit={code}, text={text!r}")
    total = len(SELFTEST) + 2
    if failures:
        print("\n".join(failures))
        print(f"{len(failures)}/{total} fixtures FAILED")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"selftest OK — {total}/{total} fixtures pass")
    sys.exit(0)


def main():

"""Offline probe for the edgar_client.py fts CLI branch (no network).
Monkeypatches urllib.request.urlopen with a canned efts.sec.gov response
whose shape was captured live 2026-07-21, then drives __main__ via runpy.

Usage: python3 probe_fts_dispatch.py <path-to-edgar_client.py>
Exit 0 = all asserts pass; AssertionError otherwise.
"""
import io, json, runpy, sys, urllib.request
from contextlib import redirect_stdout

TARGET = sys.argv[1]

# Canned response — shape captured live from
# https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q="HBM4"&forms=6-K on 2026-07-21
CANNED = {
    "took": 3, "timed_out": False,
    "hits": {"total": {"value": 1, "relation": "eq"}, "hits": [{
        "_index": "edgar_file",
        "_id": "0000937966-24-000026:exhibit993.htm",
        "_score": 10.0,
        "_source": {
            "ciks": ["0000937966"],
            "display_names": ["ASML HOLDING NV  (ASML, ASMLF)  (CIK 0000937966)"],
            "file_date": "2024-11-18",
            "form": "6-K",
            "file_type": "EX-99.3",
            "adsh": "0000937966-24-000026",
        },
    }]},
}

class FakeResp:
    headers = {}
    def read(self): return json.dumps(CANNED).encode()
    def __enter__(self): return self
    def __exit__(self, *a): return False

urllib.request.urlopen = lambda req, timeout=30: FakeResp()

def run_cli(*argv):
    sys.argv = [TARGET, *argv]
    buf = io.StringIO()
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        runpy.run_path(TARGET, run_name="__main__")
    return buf.getvalue()

# 1. fts subcommand must run the search, not print the docstring
out = run_cli("fts", "HBM4", "--forms", "6-K")
assert "Usage (import or CLI)" not in out, "REGRESSION: fts fell through to help text"
assert "2024-11-18" in out and "6-K" in out and "ASML HOLDING NV" in out, f"bad fts output: {out!r}"
assert "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/937966/000093796624000026/exhibit993.htm" in out, \
    f"Archives URL not reconstructed: {out!r}"

# 2. fts without --forms also dispatches

# 3. negative control: unknown command still prints the docstring

assert "Usage (import or CLI)" in out, "unknown-cmd fallback broken"

Probe already written and passing at /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/t86probe/probe_t86.py — exact code (monkeypatches urllib.request.urlopen; first attempt raises a simulated chunked-drop, second returns a canned T86 body incl. a whitespace-padded code ' 2330 ' to exercise the strip-filter):

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Offline probe for G-finding: t86_institutional bypasses _get() (no throttle/retry, timeout=20).

Usage: python3 probe_t86.py <path-to-twse_client.py> {old|new}
  old  -> expect DEFECT: first transient network error propagates (single attempt, timeout=20)
  new  -> expect HEAL:   first transient error retried via _get (attempt 2 succeeds, timeout=30,
                         throttle timestamp updated, codes filter still strips whitespace)
No network: urllib.request.urlopen is monkeypatched.
"""
import importlib.util, sys, time

path, mode = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("twse_client_under_test", path)
m = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(m)

calls = {"n": 0, "timeouts": []}
BODY = b'{"stat":"OK","data":[[" 2330 ","TSMC","1","2","3","4"],["2408","Nanya","1","2","3","4"]]}'

class FakeResp:
    def read(self): return BODY
    def __enter__(self): return self
    def __exit__(self, *a): return False

def fake_urlopen(req, timeout=None):
    calls["n"] += 1
    calls["timeouts"].append(timeout)
    if calls["n"] == 1:
        raise ConnectionResetError("simulated IncompleteRead/chunked-drop on www.twse.com.tw")
    return FakeResp()

import urllib.request
urllib.request.urlopen = fake_urlopen  # old code aliases urllib.request as _rq -> same module object patched

if mode == "old":
    try:
        m.t86_institutional("20260716", codes={"2330"})
    except ConnectionResetError:
        assert calls["n"] == 1, calls
        assert calls["timeouts"] == [20], calls["timeouts"]
        print("DEFECT REPRODUCED (old): single attempt, timeout=20, transient drop propagated as unhandled exception")
        sys.exit(0)
    print("FAIL: old code unexpectedly survived the transient error"); sys.exit(1)

# mode == "new"
before = m._last[0]
rows = m.t86_institutional("20260716", codes={"2330"})
assert calls["n"] == 2, "retry did not heal: %d attempt(s)" % calls["n"]
assert calls["timeouts"] == [30, 30], "expected module timeout=30 on both attempts: %r" % calls["timeouts"]
assert rows == [[" 2330 ", "TSMC", "1", "2", "3", "4"]], "codes strip-filter broken: %r" % rows
assert m._last[0] > before, "throttle timestamp _last[0] not updated -> t86 still outside MIN_INTERVAL discipline"
calls["n"] = 1  # next fake call succeeds immediately
m._last[0] = time.time()  # force throttle condition: last request "just happened"
t0 = time.time()
m.t86_institutional("20260716")
assert time.time() - t0 >= m.MIN_INTERVAL - 0.05, "MIN_INTERVAL throttle not applied on consecutive t86 calls"
print("HEAL VERIFIED (new): retry healed transient drop, timeout=30, codes filter intact, MIN_INTERVAL throttle applied")

Verification is instead a standalone offline probe (stubbed urlopen, zero network, zero repo writes), already written and PASSING at /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/twse/probe_monthly.py. Exact probe code (reproduce anywhere):

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Offline probe for twse_client.py CLI 'monthly' truncation (G-audit finding).

Stubs urllib.request.urlopen with a 10-row fake t187ap05_L payload, then runs
the target file as __main__ via runpy. No network, no repo writes.

Usage: python3 probe_monthly.py <path-to-twse_client.py> <pre|post>
  pre  = assert the DEFECT (7-code sweep clipped to 5; bare call clipped silently)
  post = assert the FIX (7-code sweep prints 7; bare call previews 5 + stderr notice)
"""
import contextlib, io, json, runpy, sys
import urllib.request

FAKE = [{"公司代號": "%04d" % i, "公司名稱": "co%d" % i,
         "營業收入-當月營收": str(1000 + i)} for i in range(1, 11)]  # 10 rows


class FakeResp:
    def __init__(self, payload):
        self._b = json.dumps(payload).encode("utf-8")
    def read(self):
        return self._b
    def __enter__(self):
        return self
    def __exit__(self, *a):
        return False


urllib.request.urlopen = lambda req, timeout=30: FakeResp(FAKE)

TARGET = sys.argv[1]
MODE = sys.argv[2]


def run_cli(argv):
    sys.argv = ["twse_client.py"] + argv
    out, err = io.StringIO(), io.StringIO()
    with contextlib.redirect_stdout(out), contextlib.redirect_stderr(err):
        runpy.run_path(TARGET, run_name="__main__")
    lines = [l for l in out.getvalue().splitlines() if l.strip()]
    return lines, err.getvalue()


codes7 = ["%04d" % i for i in range(1, 8)]  # 7 tracked-style codes

rows_codes, err_codes = run_cli(["monthly"] + codes7)
rows_bare, err_bare = run_cli(["monthly"])

if MODE == "pre":
    assert len(rows_codes) == 5, "pre: expected clipped 5, got %d" % len(rows_codes)
    assert len(rows_bare) == 5, "pre: expected clipped 5, got %d" % len(rows_bare)
    assert err_codes == "" and err_bare == "", "pre: expected silent truncation"
    print("PRE-PATCH DEFECT REPRODUCED: 7-code sweep -> %d rows, bare -> %d rows, stderr silent"
          % (len(rows_codes), len(rows_bare)))
elif MODE == "post":
    assert len(rows_codes) == 7, "post: expected all 7, got %d" % len(rows_codes)
    assert err_codes == "", "post: unexpected stderr on code-filtered call: %r" % err_codes
    assert len(rows_bare) == 5, "post: expected 5-row preview, got %d" % len(rows_bare)
    assert "5 more rows" in err_bare, "post: missing truncation notice, stderr=%r" % err_bare
    # regression: quote path still behaves (filters on Code, absent from fake payload)
    q_rows, _ = run_cli(["quote", "0003"])
    assert q_rows == [], "post: quote path regressed (fake payload has no Code field)"
    print("POST-PATCH PASS: 7-code sweep -> 7 rows; bare -> 5 rows + stderr %r"
          % err_bare.strip())
else:
    raise SystemExit("mode must be pre|post")

patches.new_string:
if "--selftest" in sys.argv:
        # known-answer test vs the FROZEN K3 pack (committed, never edited) — not the growing live ledger
        pack = ROOT / "meta" / "audits" / "2026-07-17-k3-benchmark-pack" / "test1_binary_rows.csv"
        b = rows(path=pack)
        n = len(b); base = sum(o for _,o in b)/n
        br  = sum((p-o)**2 for p,o in b)/n
        brb = sum((base-o)**2 for _,o in b)/n
        brs = sum(((p+base)/2-o)**2 for p,o in b)/n
        ok = n == 41 and abs(br-0.1978) < 5e-4 and abs(brb-0.1844) < 5e-4 and abs(brs-0.1777) < 5e-4
        print(f"selftest (frozen K3 pack): n={n} sys {br:.4f} / base {brb:.4f} / shrink {brs:.4f} "
              f"— expect n=41, 0.1978/0.1844/0.1777 — {'OK' if ok else 'CHECK'}")

patches.rationale: The confirmed UPGRADE: (1) fixture is the committed frozen K3 pack (research/meta/audits/2026-07-17-k3-benchmark-pack/test1_binary_rows.csv, exactly the n=41 rows the docstring pins to HEAD d816abf) — the pack's MIXED include=NO rows drop out via rows()'s own TRUE/FALSE gate, verified identical to the include=YES set; (2) all THREE docstring known answers checked (old code checked only sys-Brier) with tight 5e-4 tolerance (measured float distance from the rounded references: 4.3e-5/1.4e-5/4.6e-5; a single flipped outcome moves sys-Brier ~0.02, so zero FP risk while the pack is frozen); (3) n==41 guard catches fixture truncation/growth; (4) sys.exit(1) on CHECK makes the selftest usable as a gate — the old ok flag never influenced the exit code; (5) kills the n=0 live-ledger ZeroDivisionError inside selftest (pack is always n=41; the live report path already has its own n<2 guard, verified). A PASSING selftest still falls through to the live report, preserving current wake behavior. Kills the silent-drift class the old comment itself acknowledged ('ledger grows; loose bound'): as graded rows accumulate, the live-ledger recompute walks away from 0.1978 and a ±0.02 window on a moving target can both false-fire on healthy growth and mask a real regression.

#!/bin/bash
# G-55 regression test: setup.sh must work from ANY clone location (dynamic
# repo-root per hooks/README.md convention). Run: bash test_setup_sh_portability.sh
# Read-only on this repo (used as clone source only); scratch work in mktemp -d;
# preserves and restores /tmp/llmna-boot-status.txt.
set -u
REPO=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../.." && pwd)
SCRATCH=$(mktemp -d /tmp/g55-test-XXXXXX)
BOOT=/tmp/llmna-boot-status.txt
PASS=0; FAIL=0
ok()  { echo "PASS: $1"; PASS=$((PASS+1)); }
bad() { echo "FAIL: $1"; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)); }
[ -f "$BOOT" ] && cp "$BOOT" "$SCRATCH/boot.bak"
cleanup() { [ -f "$SCRATCH/boot.bak" ] && cp "$SCRATCH/boot.bak" "$BOOT"; rm -rf "$SCRATCH"; }
trap cleanup EXIT

git clone --quiet "$REPO" "$SCRATCH/clone" || { echo "ABORT: clone failed"; exit 1; }
SETUP="$SCRATCH/clone/research/meta/tools/setup.sh"

# 1. Relocated clone + env unset (container-start conditions): root must be the clone.
cd /tmp
env -u CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR bash "$SETUP" || bad "exit nonzero"
grep -q -- "--- repo ($SCRATCH/clone) ---" "$BOOT" && ok "root=clone (env unset)" || bad "wrong root: $(grep -- '--- repo' "$BOOT")"
grep -q '^HEAD: ' "$BOOT" && ok "HEAD reported" || bad "HEAD missing"
HP=$(git -C "$SCRATCH/clone" config --local core.hooksPath 2>/dev/null || echo none)
[ "$HP" = "research/meta/hooks/git" ] && ok "hooksPath activated on clone" || bad "hooksPath=$HP"

# 2. cwd-relative invocation (data-access.md item 4 mid-session re-run shape).
cd "$SCRATCH/clone/research/meta/tools"
env -u CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR bash setup.sh
grep -q -- "--- repo ($SCRATCH/clone) ---" "$BOOT" && ok "root=clone (cwd-relative)" || bad "wrong root (cwd-relative)"

# 3. CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR wins when set (hooks/README.md convention).
cd /tmp
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$SCRATCH/clone" bash "$SETUP"
grep -q -- "--- repo ($SCRATCH/clone) ---" "$BOOT" && ok "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR honored" || bad "env var not honored"

echo "----- $PASS passed, $FAIL failed -----"
[ "$FAIL" -eq 0 ]

Wake integration: `status` runs at each wake; any live key upgrades that data class from
web-chase to API pull; missing/blocked keys fall back silently to the web-chase path.
"""
patches.new_string:
Wake integration: `status` runs at each wake; any live key upgrades that data class from
web-chase to API pull; missing/blocked keys fall back silently to the web-chase path.
Keys with no probe wired (DART, EDINET) report UNPROBED, not BLOCKED — present means
usable for their data class; the first real call is the probe.
"""
patches.rationale: Docstring is the wake-integration contract the finding cites (lines 18-22): as written, a literal reader maps any BLOCKED state to web-chase fallback. This adds the UNPROBED semantics so the label change in patch 2 is spec-backed, not just a string swap — present no-probe keys are usable, and the first real fetch is the de-facto probe.

patches.new_string:
else:
            # no reachability probe wired (DART/EDINET) — a present key is UNPROBED, not blocked
            print(f"  {name}: present ({src}) — UNPROBED/no-probe-defined — {purpose}")
            continue
        blocked = err or ("denied" in (body or "").lower()[:200])
patches.rationale: The confirmed defect: the else-branch folded err='no-probe-defined' into the 'BLOCKED/'+err state string, so a present, valid DART/EDINET key printed 'BLOCKED/no-probe-defined' — semantically UNPROBED, and a wake reading BLOCKED literally web-chases Korean filings despite a live T1 key. Fix mirrors the existing ABSENT branch's print+continue style, keeps the 'no-probe-defined' token grep-compatible, and leaves the LIVE/BLOCKED logic for probed keys byte-identical (regression-probed). Compose-safe with the sibling FMP/AV patch in flight: that patch's old_string ends at (and preserves) the bare 'else:' line; this one alters only the lines after it — both remain unique in either application order.
