EXPERIMENT_START = date(2026, 6, 1)  # hook pair shipped 2026-06-01

# Numerator scope (G-28 fix 2026-07-21): the experiment counts only
# structural-markers fires — reasonless lines (pre-2026-06-21 log format)
# + (structural-markers-missing). Any other reason suffix — e.g.
# (position-implication-tier-missing), the Rule #11 tier-tagging
# mechanic, or the 06-12 smoke-test line — must NOT reach the 08-06
# adjudication numerator.
FIRE_RE = re.compile(
    r"- (\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}) .*structural-output.*FIRE(?: \(([^)]*)\))?")
EXPERIMENT_REASONS = {None, "structural-markers-missing"}
patches.rationale: Core G-28 fix as reason capture + explicit allow-set (the finding's proposed 'reason capture + exclusion list' variant, inverted to a whitelist). structural-output-hook.py emits exactly two reasons (line 294 position-implication-tier-missing, line 311 structural-markers-missing); pre-2026-06-21 log lines are reasonless and belong to the experiment, so {None, 'structural-markers-missing'} is the complete experiment scope. A whitelist (vs a blacklist of the one bad reason) kills the whole error class: any future fire-reason added to the hook is excluded from the adjudicator by default instead of silently contaminating it — which is exactly how G-28 happened. Bonus in the same mechanic: the 2026-06-12 smoke-test line (log itself says 'do not count') carries a non-matching parenthetical and drops out of W24 automatically.

Apply the scope filter at the count site: group(2) is the captured reason (None for pre-06-21 reasonless lines, which still count). Verified impact on live data: W24 5->4, W26 15->13, W27 24->22, W28 31 (was 32); W29/W30 unchanged; last-4-week trend corrects from 0.131->0.091 to 0.120->0.091 (still FALLING today, but tail[0] was inflated +0.011 toward the keep-priming verdict).

FIXTURES = [
    # (log line, counted-in-numerator?)
    ("- 2026-06-13 06:42:10Z structural-output-hook FIRE", True),  # pre-06-21 reasonless format
    ("- 2026-06-22 19:18:30Z structural-output-hook FIRE (structural-markers-missing)", True),
    ("- 2026-06-23 11:37:54Z structural-output-hook FIRE (position-implication-tier-missing)", False),  # Rule #11 mechanic, not the experiment (G-28)
    ("- 2026-06-12 06:20:05Z structural-output-hook FIRE (smoke-test against fake transcript — NOT a genuine fire; do not count in week-2 check)", False),
    ("- 2026-07-12 10:00:00Z anti-fabrication-hook FIRE (uncited-number)", False),  # other hooks never match
]


def selftest() -> None:
    for line, want in FIXTURES:
        m = FIRE_RE.match(line)
        got = bool(m) and m.group(2) in EXPERIMENT_REASONS
        assert got == want, f"fixture failed: {line!r} -> {got}, want {want}"
    print(f"selftest OK ({len(FIXTURES)} fixtures)")

Decision rule (pre-registered 2026-07-06): normalized rate FALLING over the
extension window -> priming works, keep both; flat/rising -> retire priming.

Trend operationalization hardened 2026-07-21 (audit): verdict window = last
4 COMPLETE non-NaN ISO weeks — the in-progress week is excluded (2026-08-06
is a Thursday; adjudication would otherwise hinge on a 4-day partial W32) —
compared by half-means, not endpoints. The registered rule is window-level;

flip the verdict (observed 2026-07-21: partial W30 as tail[-1] read FALLING
while the complete weeks W26-W29 read FLAT/RISING).

def week_key(d: date) -> str:
    iso = d.isocalendar()
    return f"{iso[0]}-W{iso[1]:02d}"


def trend_verdict(rates, today):
    """Half-mean trend over the last 4 COMPLETE non-NaN weeks.

    Excludes the in-progress ISO week (partial data — a single early-week
    burst can flip an endpoint compare) and drops NaN (zero-commit) weeks
    BEFORE windowing so they don't shrink the evidence base. Returns
    (this_week, n, first_mean, second_mean, direction), or None when fewer
    than 2 complete non-NaN weeks exist.
    """
    this_week = week_key(today)
    tail = [r for w, r in rates if w < this_week and r == r][-4:]
    if len(tail) < 2:
        return None
    mid = len(tail) // 2
    m1 = sum(tail[:mid]) / mid
    m2 = sum(tail[mid:]) / (len(tail) - mid)
    return this_week, len(tail), m1, m2, ("FALLING" if m2 < m1 else "FLAT/RISING")


def main() -> None:
patches.rationale: Extracts the verdict into a pure, selftest-able function. Three deterministic changes vs the inline block: (1) `w < this_week` drops the in-progress ISO week (lexicographic compare is safe: week_key zero-pads, verified '2026-W09'<'2026-W30' and '2026-W52'<'2027-W01'); (2) NaN weeks are filtered before the [-4:] window instead of after, so a zero-commit week no longer silently shrinks the window below 4 weeks of evidence; (3) verdict = first-half mean vs second-half mean, faithful to the registered window-level wording, not a 2-point endpoint compare. Bonus alignment: on 2026-08-06 (W32 in progress) the window becomes exactly W28-W31 = the 07-06→08-06 extension window the rule names. NOTE for the adjudication record: this fix is direction-changing on today's data (endpoint read FALLING via the 1.5-day partial W30; complete-weeks read 0.118→0.129 FLAT/RISING) — that flip is the measured noise being removed, and it moves the interim read AGAINST keeping the priming hook, so it cannot be construed as rigging toward keep. G-28 (tier-missing fires polluting the numerator) is a separate known-open defect with its own patch design and must also land before 08-06.

# Denominator ref (fixed 2026-07-21): session containers check out claude/*
    # branches with no local 'main' (git exits 128 -> crash), and a stale local
    # 'main' silently undercounts recent weeks -> inflated recent rate ->
    # biases the 08-06 endpoint compare (same class as the 11-day-stale-clone
    # incident setup.sh banners on). Best-effort fetch, then the first ref
    # that resolves: origin/main > main > HEAD.
    try:
        subprocess.run(["git", "-C", str(REPO), "fetch", "origin", "main"],
                       capture_output=True, timeout=20)
    except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, OSError):
        pass  # offline/no-remote: fall back to whatever resolves locally
    ref = next((r for r in ("origin/main", "main", "HEAD")
                if subprocess.run(
                    ["git", "-C", str(REPO), "rev-parse", "--verify", "--quiet", r],
                    capture_output=True).returncode == 0), "HEAD")
    print(f"(denominator ref: {ref})")
    out = subprocess.run(
        # NOT --first-parent: the 2026-07-06 migration grafted pre-migration
        # history onto a merge side-branch; first-parent would zero out all
        # pre-July weeks (caught 2026-07-07 on first computed run).
        ["git", "-C", str(REPO), "log", ref,
         f"--since={EXPERIMENT_START.isoformat()}", "--format=%as"],

patches.rationale: The denominator hard-requires a LOCAL, CURRENT 'main' ref. Probe-confirmed (4 clone worlds, scratchpad/probe_som_mainref_g50.py): (A) claude/* session-branch clone with no refs/heads/main -> git 128 -> CalledProcessError, exit 1 -- the pre-registered 2026-08-06 adjudicator cannot run in the normal web-container shape (8 claude/* remote branches exist); (B) local main 200 commits behind origin/main (the documented 11-day-stale-clone class that setup.sh ships a STALE banner for) -> exit 0 but W29 denominator 95 vs 220 and W30 0 vs 77, printing 'trend 0.131 -> 0.326 = FLAT/RISING' where truth is '0.131 -> 0.091 = FALLING' -- the keep-vs-retire decision FLIPS silently. Fix: best-effort `git fetch origin main` (timeout-guarded, exceptions swallowed -- offline falls through), then log the first ref that resolves via rev-parse --verify: origin/main > main > HEAD, and print the chosen ref so the 08-06 run is auditable (kills the silent-wrong class, matches the house 'never silently skip' value). setup.sh precedent already treats origin/main as ground truth. Residual accepted: an offline laptop with unpushed local-main commits would prefer a slightly-behind origin/main -- transient by construction since stop-hook-git-check forces commit+push every turn. Docstring ('weekly main-branch commits') stays accurate. Redteam 07-20 r2 'Noted' only the crash half and folded it into 07-24; the decision-flip variant plus the NO-DELAY rule justify shipping this probe-validated 4-line-logic diff now (parent may still choose to land it inside the 07-24 pass -- the patch is ready either way). Exact code (currently at /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-LLMNA/2cdd4d7c-348c-5477-8a2b-451a053e6a43/scratchpad/probe_date_basis.py):

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Probe: demonstrate --since (committer-date filter) vs %as (author-date
bucketing) divergence at an ISO week boundary, and that %cs is consistent.

Synthetic commit: authored Sun 2026-06-07 (ISO W23), committed Mon
2026-06-08 (ISO W24). A --since=2026-06-08 window admits it (committer
date passes) but %as buckets it into W23 — entirely OUTSIDE the since
window. %cs buckets it into W24, matching the filter.
"""
import subprocess
import tempfile
import os

with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as d:
    env = dict(os.environ,
               GIT_AUTHOR_DATE="2026-06-07T23:50:00+00:00",
               GIT_COMMITTER_DATE="2026-06-08T00:10:00+00:00",
               GIT_AUTHOR_NAME="probe", GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL="p@x",
               GIT_COMMITTER_NAME="probe", GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL="p@x")
    subprocess.run(["git", "init", "-q", "-b", "main", d], check=True)
    subprocess.run(["git", "-C", d, "commit", "-q", "--allow-empty",
                    "-m", "boundary commit"], env=env, check=True)
    for fmt in ("%as", "%cs"):
        out = subprocess.run(
            ["git", "-C", d, "log", "main",
             "--since=2026-06-08 00:00 +0000", f"--format={fmt}"],
            capture_output=True, text=True, check=True).stdout.strip()
        print(f"--since='2026-06-08 00:00 +0000' --format={fmt}: {out!r}")
    print("EXPECT: %as yields '2026-06-07' (admitted by committer date, "
          "bucketed BEFORE the window); %cs yields '2026-06-08' (consistent).")

Note for fixture authors: the since argument uses explicit ' 00:00 +0000' because git approxidate completes a bare date with the CURRENT time-of-day (discovered during probing — first probe run with bare --since=2026-06-08 returned empty for both formats).
